reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 17, 2026
reviews.title: Best-in-Class Quality and Sound
reviews.body: These Apple EarPods with USB-C are truly best in class. The sound quality is clear and well-balanced, with good bass and crisp vocals. Calls are very clear, and the built-in remote works perfectly for controlling volume and answering calls. The fit is comfortable for long use, and the build quality feels premium as expected from Apple. The USB-C connection is convenient and works seamlessly without any setup issues. If you’re looking for reliable wired earbuds with excellent audio quality and mic performance, this is a great choice. Highly recommended.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 11, 2026
reviews.title: Great Sound and Comfort
reviews.body: I bought these for my son, who prefers traditional wired headphones over wireless ones, and these turned out to be a great choice. The sound quality is clear and well-balanced, and they’re very comfortable to wear even for extended periods. They fit securely without causing any discomfort, which is important for daily use. I initially purchased one pair, and after seeing how well they performed, I went ahead and bought another. That alone says a lot about the overall quality and reliability.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 10, 2026
reviews.title: The $9 Audiophile Secret: Small Dongle, Big Practicality
reviews.body: Let’s be honest: nobody actually wants to live the "dongle life." When the headphone jack started disappearing from our devices, most of us were annoyed at the thought of yet another accessory to lose. But after spending a significant amount of time with the Apple USB-C to 3.5 mm Headphone Jack Adapter, I’ve realized it’s less of an annoyance and more of a practical hero in my daily toolkit. Whether I’m grading student dissertations or jumping between devices for lectures, this little white cable has become surprisingly indispensable. The "Hidden" Audio Quality As someone who appreciates high-fidelity gear, I was skeptical. I’ve used high-end headphones from brands like Beyerdynamic and Audio-Technica, and usually, you’d expect a $9 adapter to muddy the sound. Consumer feedback often points this out, but the reality is a pleasant surprise: this isn't just a physical adapter; it’s a tiny, highly efficient Digital-to-Analog Converter (DAC). In the audiophile community, this dongle is actually legendary for its transparency. It has an incredibly low output impedance, which means if you’re like me and enjoy using sensitive in-ear monitors (IEMs), you won’t hear that annoying background hiss that plagues cheaper adapters. The sound is neutral and clean—it doesn't try to "color" the music, which is exactly what I want when I’m focusing on work. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 17, 2026
reviews.title: better than overhead headphones
reviews.body: I honestly love these headphones so much. I actually prefer them over my over-the-head headphones, which surprised me. The biggest reason is how they let me stay aware of my surroundings while still enjoying great sound quality. I can listen to music or take calls without feeling completely cut off from what’s going on around me, which is perfect for walking around campus or studying in public spaces. It stays in space and I love the USB connectivity. I wish there was a good charging capability because it goes in the charging port. It's held up really well and has great sturdiness. The sound is clear, the fit is comfortable, and I love how easy they are to use with the built-in remote for volume and calls. The USB-C connection is also super convenient and works seamlessly with my devices. Overall, these have become my go-to headphones. Simple, reliable, and way more practical for everyday use than bulky over-the-head ones.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 25, 2026
reviews.title: Worth the extra money. They're the best ones I own.
reviews.body: Best you can buy. I have 4 usbc ear buds, these are the best. They sound the best, work perfectly, feel good. Worth the extra money.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
reviews.title: Fit issues
reviews.body: If you’ve used an iPhone from before the USB-C switch, you already know these EarPods. They’re plug-and-play and easy to use. The sound is decent, with solid bass and clear treble for a basic wired pair. The main issues are fit, durability, and price. The fit isn’t great for everyone. The angle doesn’t always sit right, so they can sound a bit thin unless you adjust them. I usually need earbud covers to keep them in place, and even then I sometimes have to press them slightly forward or back to get the best sound. Durability is hit or miss. After about a year, the rubber near the Y-junction where the wires split tends to loosen. Once that happens, the wiring feels exposed and more fragile. I’ve had a few pairs fail this way, and it’s usually cheaper to replace them than repair them. The price also feels high for such an old design. They’re okay and mostly reliable, but the fit issues, weak point at the Y-junction, and unchanged pricing are real drawbacks.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 23, 2026
reviews.title: My Thoughts As a Samsung "Fanboy"
reviews.body: Like the title says, I'm a Samsung guy through and through. But, out of curiosity, I ordered these EarPods just to try them out. I gotta say, these are really good earbuds, especially for the price. They fit perfectly in my ears, and works with most USB C devices. I use them with my Galaxy S24 Ultra, and my older Tab S7 without any issues. My brother recently got an iPhone, and said they work perfectly well (of course) for him. Sound quality is pretty good regardless of what you listen to. While they're not made for audiophiles, I found these to be above average relative to other earbuds in the same price range. The built-in mic works fine for phone calls and video chatting, and never had anyone complain about any sort of issue on their end. I like that it has buttons for volume, media control, etc. Noise cancellation isn't the best, but it's to expected due to how they fit in your ears. All in all, whether you're on Team Apple or Team Android, I highly recommend these EarPods!

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 14, 2026
reviews.title: Great product
reviews.body: Great product, exactly what I expected. The sound quality is clear and they work perfectly with my iPhone. The cable feels durable and not cheap. They arrived on time and in perfect condition. I would definitely buy them again.

reviews.date: Reviewed in Canada on December 20, 2025
reviews.title: Best... BEST Budget wired USBC earbuds .
reviews.body: Apple EarPods (USB-C) Okay honestly for $24 these are the best sounding cost effective wired earbuds money can buy. Money can buy It's nothing fancy, no noise canceling, just an ear bud that fits into most people's ears comfortably, has a little volume up and down controlled at works even on an Android phone, and actually sounds fantastic for the price. It's still a great value and easily can recommend this as a backup to Bluetooth. There are lots of USBC wired earbuds out there for about the same amount of money... But these ones are hands down actually. Very good. Sound quality wise. The bass to treble is well balanced and seals right into your ears quite nicely. Honestly, I really can't complain Great value cost effective. Pretty good sound for the price. You really can't go wrong. If you're looking for a wired USBC backup for your Apple or even Android phone, you literally can't go wrong with these and I don't recommend looking for third-party cheaper no name brands because I've tried a dozen of those other brands and nothing compared to the sound quality that these offer. reviews.date: Reviewed in Mexico on April 22, 2025
reviews.title: Clásicos, confiables y actualizados
reviews.body: Los Apple EarPods con conector USB-C representan una actualización significativa de los tradicionales EarPods, adaptándose a los dispositivos más recientes de Apple que han optado por este estándar. Ofrecen una opción económica para quienes buscan una experiencia auditiva básica pero confiable. El diseño de los EarPods sigue la geometría del oído, lo que proporciona una comodidad superior en comparación con los auriculares tradicionales. Su forma ergonómica permite un ajuste adecuado sin necesidad de almohadillas adicionales, siendo ideales para uso prolongado sin causar molestias., incluso para hacer ejercicio sin tanto problema en que sufran una caída. Estos EarPods admiten audio sin pérdidas, con una resolución de hasta 24 bit/48 kHz, lo que significa que pueden reproducir música en alta calidad, similar a la de un estudio de grabación. Aunque no ofrecen una experiencia auditiva de gama alta, proporcionan un sonido claro y nítido adecuado para la mayoría de los usuarios. Son compatibles con dispositivos Apple que cuentan con puerto USB-C, incluyendo modelos recientes de iPhone, iPad y Mac. Además, incorporan un control remoto integrado que permite ajustar el volumen, controlar la reproducción de música y responder llamadas con facilidad, lo cual es bastante cómodo y práctico en pleno 2025. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 10, 2026
reviews.title: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Excellent Connectivity and Great Value
reviews.body: I absolutely love the Apple AirTag (2nd Generation). Setup was incredibly simple, and it connected to my iPhone instantly through the Find My app. The connectivity is excellent—location updates are quick and accurate, which makes it very easy to track my keys, bag, or anything I attach it to. The Precision Finding feature is one of the best parts. It guides you directly to your item with clear directions, which makes locating things fast and stress-free. Knowing I can quickly find my belongings gives me a lot of peace of mind, especially when traveling or when I misplace my keys around the house. Another big plus is the value for money. For such a small device, it offers powerful tracking, a long-lasting replaceable battery, and seamless integration with Apple devices. It’s durable, reliable, and incredibly useful in everyday life. Overall, the AirTag is a smart, convenient, and affordable way to keep track of your belongings, and I would definitely recommend it to anyone looking for a simple tracking solution. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 1, 2026
reviews.title: Been waiting a while for these
reviews.body: They're finally here, the second generation AirTags. I'd been waiting for these for a while to be released. The AirTags are the same size as the generation 1, so they fit in cases that we've had for generation 1. Some cases came in multipacks, so it's nice to see that these newer AirTags fit in the older cases. Yes, the speaker in these is louder and the connectivity and distance is better than gen 1 making these easier to use. They work well, and the ability to easily share or add family members to an AirTag really helps when keys are shared, etc. Overall, a good value for helping me better find my keys, and tracking luggage when traveling.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 20, 2026
reviews.title: Nice upgrade to a very useful device
reviews.body: I love Apple devices and smart home appliances. As such, I was a very early adopter of the original AirTags and have used them while traveling (tucked inside of luggage) and in my daily life for years. The second generation of the AirTag represents a small, but worthwhile change to the function of the device. The ability to precisely track an object is noticeably better and the speaker inside pings very so slightly louder (I personally don’t buy the 50% louder claim, but there is a difference). If you’re the type to have keys slip behind couch cushions or get buried under laundry, this is the change you’ve been waiting for. The 2nd generation AirTag keeps the exact same footprint (both weight and size) so it fits perfectly into any existing accessories or holders you might have. Set up is a breeze and the Find My app works like a charm. I was able to see my checked bag move throughout a major airport and its arrival at my destination using the app. You can also disable forgotten item notifications easily if you happen to be leaving luggage in a hotel and don’t want to be pinged if you head out for the day. This AirTag weighs next to nothing and is worth slipping into a bag or attaching to a keychain for peace of mind. It seems just as durable as the first generation which I have toted all over the planet. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 19, 2026
reviews.title: Great Product
reviews.body: Easy Setup. Compatible With IOS. The Size It Shows. Battery Life Is Good. Good Value For Money.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 21, 2026
reviews.title: Simple, Reliable Peace of Mind
reviews.body: Setup was effortless. My phone detected the tag automatically and walked me through the whole process. If that doesn't happen for some reason, you can add it manually through the Find My app, but either way it takes about a minute. Just keep in mind you need an iPhone for any of this to work. I use mine on my dog's collar as a safety net in case he ever gets out and gets lost. Thankfully I haven't had to use it in an emergency yet, but I've tested it and it works exactly as advertised. There's real peace of mind in knowing it's there. One thing worth mentioning that doesn't get talked about enough: the battery needs to be changed periodically. It's a simple swap and the tag will notify you when it's getting low, but if you ignore those alerts and the battery dies, the tag is useless when you need it most. Easy to manage, just don't set it and forget it completely. Overall a great product. Simple to use and does exactly what it promises.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 17, 2026
reviews.title: Dogs, Keys, Kids… I Track Everything Now 🐾 (Why Didn’t I Buy These Sooner?) Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)
reviews.body: Quick answers: * Does it work with iPhone and iPad? Yes * Is Precision Finding available on all devices? Yes (newer iPhones with U1 chip work best) * How long does the battery last? About 1 year * Can I share location with others? Yes * What’s included in the 4-pack? 4 AirTags We bought these mainly for our dogs (paired with the Elevation Lab TagVault collar holder—highly recommend), and now I’m wondering how we ever lived without them. Setup was ridiculously easy—tap, connect, done. They work seamlessly with Find My, and the tracking is impressively accurate. I can see exactly where my dogs are at any time, and the sound feature makes it easy to locate them quickly. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 21, 2026
reviews.title: Nice tool, but the notifications are somewhat annoying
reviews.body: The safety notifications are annoying. The thing will make a noise and "ping" at you to let you know its there. It's so people don't use them to spy on a spouse or something. But really this gets annoying when there are two people in a household and one of them is getting constant notifications that there is a device around, or the thing is "pinging". It would be nice to not have such a nanny-state type of product.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 8, 2026
reviews.title: Great Tracking Device
reviews.body: I bought this AirTag for my German Shepherd’s new collar and it works beautifully. It was quick and easy to setup. I dropped it into the sealed case on the collar and it started tracking her throughout the house and yard. Very accurate and simple to use with my iPhone. Great value for the money and for peace of mind. Love it!

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 16, 2026
reviews.title: 10/10 comfort, connectivity, and sound
reviews.body: Blown away by these. I’ve owned Powerbeats, studio beats, and JBL, but these are by far the lightest, most comfortable buds I’ve ever had. I actually bought these over the pros cause I heard they were more comfortable, and although I’ve never had the pros I don't see how they could possibly be any better of a fit than this. I work in construction and wanted something I could pop in one ear and forget about and these far exceeded my expectations. The sound quality is solid as well, a lot of music is actually mixed and mastered for these headphones due to their popularity and it shows. Very balanced sound, was expecting more higher end than bass but it’s actually comparable to my old beats on the low end without overwhelming the rest of the mix. Lastly, the way they are interfaced with iPhone is seamless. My studio beats drove me CRAZY with how difficult they were to connect, constantly flipping open and closing the case trying to get it to connect (and they were more expensive than these LOL). I had these in and listening within 1 minute of opening the package. Not to mention the battery is insane. 10/10, just buy them, you won’t be disappointed.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 8, 2026
reviews.title: Excellent Sound, But Fit May Not Work for Everyone
reviews.body: The Apple AirPods 4 are really impressive overall, and I’d give them a solid four stars. Apple did a great job with sound quality—audio is crisp, balanced, and honestly comes very close to what you’d expect from AirPods Pro (3rd gen). Music sounds full, voices are clear, and they perform well for both calls and everyday listening. As expected, the connectivity is seamless. They pair instantly with Apple devices, switch easily between them, and just work without any hassle. That level of convenience is a big plus and one of the main reasons to go with AirPods. However, the biggest downside for me is the fit. They just didn’t sit quite right in my ears. After wearing them for a while, they started to hurt, especially during longer listening sessions. I noticed pressure and discomfort that made it hard to keep them in for extended periods. I also didn’t feel like they were as secure as I’d like, which makes me hesitant to use them during workouts or when moving around a lot. Overall, these earbuds sound great and have all the convenience you’d expect from Apple, but the fit just wasn’t right for me. If they were more comfortable, they’d easily be a five-star product. reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 30, 2026
reviews.title: All day comfort and reliable sound that feels effortless
reviews.body: I bought these because I wanted a pair of wireless earbuds that could handle long listening sessions, workouts, and everyday calls without constant fiddling. The redesign with a shorter stem and a softer contour looked like it would sit more stably in my ears, and the quick press controls seemed handy for skipping tracks or taking a call on the go. Pairing in day to day use was straightforward. I just opened the charging case near my phone and tapped connect, and they were ready to go. They stay in place during workouts and commutes, and the ear sensor pauses audio when I take them out which is convenient for quick interruptions or when I need to listen to something short without fumbling with the device. What stood out most is the overall clarity of calls and the way music and shows feel more immersive with spatial audio that moves with my head. The sound isn't harsh at higher volumes and the voice isolation helps keep conversations clear even in busy environments. Battery life is solid too with about five hours per charge and a total of around 30 hours with the case, and the charging case itself charges via USB-C which is convenient during travel. A small caveat is the learning curve with the quick press controls, which took a moment to get used to. The case is noticeably smaller than the previous generation, but it still adds a bit of bulk when tucked into a pocket. Overall, they feel reliable, comfortable, and genuinely enjoyable to use, delivering a five star experience. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 28, 2026
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: These are amazing. After going thru a lot of different pairs of other brands, I came back to these Apple ones that cannot be beat. They are a little more expensive than the other brands but there are reasons for it. The quality is great, the sound is incredible. They are truly noise cancelling. They are durable and the battery life is great. I have not tested the water resistance yet, but it may be handy in case you loose them in the washer. As much as I did not want to spend the money, they are really worth every penny. Charging is fast and great.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
reviews.title: Best AirPods Ever
reviews.body: Best decision ever to buy these. Your voice is isolated and nobody can hear ANYTHING going on in the background. So if you're on an important call and the dog barks, no big deal. If you are walking outside, wind, music, other voices - all filtered out. If you're starving and can't help but rudely eat a cracker on call - they can't hear you crunch! The other reason these airpods are awesome is because they FIT! The original AirPods pro did not fit me at all. I bought foam tips. Fail. I bought special over the ear hooks. Fail. So I just muddled my way through until they finally didn't work anymore and then I switched to Beats for a a few years. What changed it all? Talking with my bestie on the phone was driving me crazy. She was always on the move and making tons of noise that was ear piercing for me. When she unloaded the dishwasher it was a special kind of hell in my ears. She shopped for ear buds that would save my sanity, and she hit the jackpot with these. We spoke the other day and I was in shock - I couldn't hear her dog barking, her dishes clanking or....the final convincing moment: I could not hear her blowdryer! (I asked her to turn it on as a test). I had to buy them! I read reviews that said the fit was better so I figured I would give them a try. Last thing: This version has features I never knew I needed but LOVE. I was listening to an audiobook when I saw someone and started chatting and the book automatically stopped playing until the conversation was over. The Pro 3 also includes a hearing test and measures to protect your precious ears. Do I work for Apple? NO! I just wanted to share my enthusiasm to take the guesswork out of this purchase for you. They are pricey (if you didn't agree you wouldn't read a review this long), but they are worth it! They work great (seamless with an iPhone) and fit great. Now I just have to take really good care of them and make sure they last and don't get lost. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 25, 2026
reviews.title: Excellent noise cancellation and perfect for college life
reviews.body: I purchased these for my 21-year-old daughter who’s in college, and she absolutely loves them—especially the noise cancellation. What we liked: • Noise cancellation is impressive: She says it blocks out almost all background noise, which is perfect for studying in dorms, libraries, or coffee shops • Sound quality: Clear, balanced audio for both music and calls • Comfortable fit: She can wear them for long periods without discomfort • Easy pairing: Seamless connection with her iPhone and other Apple devices • Battery life: Lasts through classes and study sessions without constantly needing to recharge Things to consider: • A little pricey, but you definitely get what you pay for Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 1, 2026
reviews.title: A Solid Product At An Incredible Price
reviews.body: I've had two sets of professional hearing aids in the past, one of them being fairly new, but these are my new favorite even though they are far less expensive. I will warn that pairing to Android devices can be somewhat difficult (seems to work best if you pair before pairing to your Apple device), but I was able to pair to both my Samsung S25+ and Samsung Watch 8 Classic (the 25+ being the more difficult). Particularly nice is "Adaptive" mode, which changes the setting automatically when it hears real conversation (not just TV conversation), but oddly you have to change the setup so that you can switch to that mode by just touching the device. That is a real game changer, because it eliminates the stray white-type noise you hear with hearing aids, and you just hear your surroundings in a passthrough mode. Battery life seems to exceed the 10 hours claimed using them in Adaptive mode with the hearing aid function on. The hearing aid testing was fairly easy, but I question its accuracy since it showed too similar of results for my left and right ear, when there is a difference at high frequencies. Doesn't seem to affect the overall operation though. My main complaint which almost results in a one star reduction is the lousy user documentation. Rather than just having a webpage that covers this item it's combined with all the other similar devices. And it really doesn't explain that well what the various settings do. Finally, it doesn't seem to be available in just a PDF version that you can download. Another item to note is switching between Android devices is manual, although that may be an advantage. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 11, 2025
reviews.title: A subtle but significant upgrade for iOS users.**UPDATE**
reviews.body: I am an ear bud junky and have been on a quest for the perfect set for a long time. I have owned and own a lot of the big names from Apple, B&0, B&W, Sony, Bose, Klipsch, Devialet, Technics, many off brands, and more. I use both iOS and Android devices and don’t have a particular favorite so I can speak to how ear buds interact with both. This is an interesting update. I almost passed on it and I probably should have as my AirPod Pro 2’s are just fine. However, they don’t fit me perfectly and there has been a lot made of the new fit these have. My oldest son will be getting the 2’s. These look and feel a lot like the 2’s. The physical design is nearly identical. However, the upgrades, while subtle, are mostly notable. I will break these down below and try to help those that are deciding on whether the upgrade is worth your money. Sound: Those that have read any of my other reviews on buds, know I always start with sound. It is the primary function of an ear bud. Apple has a new retuned driver here and it is a good one. The sound is natural and has good definition. You will feel the bass and highs are crispy. It is hard to define how much better these are from the 2’s but they seem louder and have more clarity. A lot of this probably has to do with the new seal these give you in your ears as well (more on that later) but these are well tuned and are noticeably improved. This was an area that needed to improve as most other high end buds were better. Sony, Technics, and Bose were all better sounding at similar prices. Some of them offer higher end codecs for more lossless streams which Apple appears to still be avoiding. I still think there are better sounding buds out there but Apple has closed the gap in meaningful ways. Well done. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 25, 2026
reviews.title: Weak bass and hurts my right ear
reviews.body: I'm trying to decide which one to keep. I know the other brand is cheaper. It's my first time with AirPods, the sound is good, but it hurts my right ear! I don't know if I will return it. I bought a comparison of one brand. The sound of one brand is louder, the bass is also stronger! I always work out, so I need good bass, and APP3 has weak bass. Yes, the sound is very clear, the quality is really good - theres a song (lyrics) that I only heard it the first time, lol - that how clear it is, but you have to turn up the volume to make the sound loud. I can't say anything about the noise cancellation because it's really great. One day there was a parade of motorcycles here that was really loud, a lot of them so I tried to put on my airpods and I couldn't hear a sound! I was really blown away. There are also a lot of settings, and it's easy to connect, which is good. This is small and easy to slide into my hand,if i keep it i will buy a rubber case’. It also slides when picked up from the case. Another thing I don't like is i feel that the battery drains easily (maybe because i am always in noise cancellation mode). I bought memory foam tips, but I still need to trim the right one to be XS because even small size tips are still painful, now it is better but theres still a bit pain. Overall, this is really great earbuds for the price, if my right ear didn't hurt, I wouldn't hesitate to keep it. 🟢UPDATE (04-01/26) Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 16, 2026
reviews.title: 5 stars, but hedge your expectations against Apple audio products
reviews.body: Best AirPods I’ve ever owned. I needed these for Spaces on X (Twitter), and the audio quality is the most pristine of all AirPods I’ve had. I am a bit of an audiophile, so they are not necessarily the “highest tier” audio quality you could purchase for this price. If you want something better for the price without caring if whether or not they are AirPods, you’re better-off looking at anything from Sennheiser. Thus, the value for the money is absolutely as inflated as you could imagine for a typical Apple product. I’ve noticed that these fit and stay in my ears much better than previous AirPods I’ve owned before. What actually makes these special is the ability to set up realistic spatial sound. If you take the time to set it up when you get these, you will be very happy with how insanely immersive these are. You turn your head in any direction, and you will hear the audio as if you are near it, and you will hear through one ear a little more than the other as you move your head in different directions. It’s a genius implementation for the purpose of immersive audio, and I love it very much. This can be shut off in Settings if you don’t want this feature. I should mention that this only occurs for the Music app (I’m not sure about Spotify or other streaming platforms, so DYOR), and not for other audio sources like YouTube or X Spaces. If you can afford this premium price, I would say go ahead and get them. However, it really needs to be said again that Apple product prices are inherently inflated. I have no intention of “testing” durability at all whatsoever because I ordered a shell for the AirPods case, but I will say that you definitely do not want to drop AirPods on hard surfaces. I dropped one AirPod several years ago when I owned the first generation, and that AirPod broke. Treat them with delicacy, because they generally are not very durable. Stay safe by purchasing AppleCare coverage for these AirPods. If you are already willing to spend this much on earbuds, then you have no excuse to not buy the coverage. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 29, 2026
reviews.title: Great earbuds.
reviews.body: I bought these to replace my Nothing Ear headphones, they were ruined by a run or two through the wash. The Airpods Pro 3 work really well, the active noise cancelling does a good job of blocking out regular sounds like vibrations, some conversations, etc. They do seem to struggle to block the sound of people coughing. I was able to test them in a loud environment the other day when I was operating a garden tractor to plow my drive, they weren't able to cancel out the very loud tractor, but they did reduce the sound significantly. I was able to enjoy my music while plowing with the volume only at 50% or so. I did also have these headphones washed by my wife, and they made it through one wash cycle with no noticeable change to the performance. I did pay for Apple care for these since I know I'm prone to leaving things in my pockets and I know my wife doesn't check pockets. Hopefully I'll never have to use that, but we'll see. The headphones were completely dead after being washed and I did have to recharge them. Read more Read more

reviews.title: Very Nice Tablet
reviews.body: Very nice and works well with the apps we use ! I was very impressed with the speed that it has when opening up the apps , it’s just like having an Apple I-phone only bigger . I also want to mention that it is lite weight and setup is a breeze . I have it setup to mirror the phone so all the apps , passwords and texts message are available on either device . Another great thing is you can answer phone calls on this tablet if your phone is elsewhere in the house , FaceTime works really good ! So far the battery life is great , around 6 hours depending on what you’re doing . Great tablet to buy and use .

reviews.title: Excellent tablet
reviews.body: This is the first iPad I've owned but I've had a variety of tablets in the past. Product & packaging has fit & finish one would expect from Apple. Setup was quick and easy as I wasn't importing data. I'm accustomed to iOS from iPhones so it was no problem to jump on and get to using it. The display is crisp and the system responds quickly to touches. Easy to carry around to take notes, etc. Pairs easily with my phone as mobile hotspot for use on the go. The battery life has been excellent so far and the device charges quickly. Good for watching videos, reading, etc. I purchased a knockoff stylus which works great with the iPad. I am overall quite pleased with the purcase and got another for my wife who also really likes hers.

reviews.title: Quality Apple product
reviews.body: The Apple iPad is great as always. It’s reliable, easy to use, and performs smoothly for everyday tasks like browsing, streaming, and apps. The display is sharp and responsive, and overall it just works well without any hassle.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 19, 2026
reviews.title: When Apple Stepped up its Tablet Game
reviews.body: This iPad is perfect for my needs. Low-latency music recording, watching videos. I don't see a point to upgrading from it until the IOS updates make it completely unusable. If you have a lesser tablet/Ipad, it's worth it. Older ipads are vastly inferior. I don't have lag issues, big screen, Apple quality, quick response. If they get cheap enough, I'll buy a few more for my kids and they'll have a lot less miniature mental breakdowns.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 27, 2026
reviews.title: Versatile Use, Bendy Screen
reviews.body: I have enjoyed my iPad very much. I got it mainly for school to write digital notes and lighten up my backpack. I use it for a lot more too. I stream movies, play games, and use productivity apps like reading kindle, canva, and drawing. I have separate homepages for school, lifestyle, and entertainment. Here are a couple of features I like. I didn’t know it came with a Finger ID power button! Makes things way more efficient. It also has focus mode. I custom made one called “Study” and I can organize which apps are available to me to minimize distraction. With this focus mode on, I am only able to access my school page. That’s it. And the only people who are able to contact me during this time is immediate family. As for the physical aspects, not too impressed. Since I use my iPad often, the battery life is not as good as I thought it was going to be for a fairly new iPad model. I wish it lasted longer. Another gripe I have is the durability of the screen. I have noticed that within 3 months of using it, the screen bends inward when I am writing notes! This is scary!! I hope this iPad last me a long time and is well worth my money.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 20, 2026
reviews.title: Great IPad tablet!
reviews.body: I use this so many times a day. It’s my second iPad that I’ve bought over the years and will always buy Apple products! They are the best tablets. I have an Apple Watch and laptop and everything works so well together! The only thing I didn’t like was the back was not like some of the other pictures I had seen. I liked the matte pink in pictures but the actual back is a very metallic pink. Some people might like that better but I didn’t. A new cover solved that though!

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 25, 2026
reviews.title: best value streaming stick
reviews.body: I got two of these because they were selling for half the price of other Roku sticks. We already own a 4k Roku stick, and on our kids' non-4k TVs, there is absolutely no difference in quality. SO if you don't have 4k tv's just get this one. We have not tested the difference on our tv that is 4k, so I'm not sure how they would compare. Setting the Rokus streaming stick up Is EASY. Much easier and more direct than the firestick. i love that we can personalize themes and sounds. It connected quickly to our WIFI, which was a relief because some device have trouble with our cement walls. The remote buttons are simple and straightforward, so even our youngest child and our oldest family members were able to use all of the functions without assistance. The batteries seem to last for a while. We havent had to replace them yet. It comes with a variety of free streaming apps and even some games. again, that was much easier for the preschooler to use than firestick.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 18, 2026
reviews.title: Works well, very fast, don't need an extra HDMI cable and remote works at any angle.
reviews.body: Pretty fast little streaming device. Feels much nicer than the older versions, also updated format for the home screen and controls etc. It might be great for some that it allows the volume to be adjusted on the tv from your roku remote, but my tvs are older and it was hard to bypass the setup for that. I was also disappointed that you still have to plug a usb cord into your tv, projector etc to power the streaming stick. I did not expect to have to use external power sources for this device, that was the reason I bought this device and was disappointed. It connected quickly and easily though, it is much smaller and nice that I don't have to find a flat surface for it to sit on. Especially since most of our tvs are mounted on the wall and I have to tape or sticky tack the old versions to the tvs so the remote can connect to them. This one however is great because it does not need to be seen or faced perfectly for the remote to control the device. I was able to point the remote in the opposite direction of the tv and streaming stick and it still controlled the streaming stick without even being pointed in the direction of the stick. Overall I really like it, but was a little disappointed in the need for external power.

reviews.title: Roku streaming
reviews.body: Excellent product, easy to install. It makes programming my TV much easier and is reasonably priced.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 3, 2026
reviews.title: Perfect for an old Not-Smart TV
reviews.body: Easy to use (I did not give any payment info to the device. I simply set up a Roku account with my email address). This HDMI device plugs right in (comes with a power cord that connects to the TV's USB port for power), it sets itself up automatically (at least it did with my old Sanyo television, which is not a smart device, so no apps and no internet connectivity). After setting the password for the wi-fi, then signing up for Roku with my email and checking my email to verify the account, I chose which apps to show on the menu, the device worked. Now watching free movies and television shows (though for-pay streaming services are available). My old TV gets a new life as a streaming device, too, thanks to Roku.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 11, 2026
reviews.title: If you own an older Kindle, buy this new Paperwhite Kindle , you won't be disappointed
reviews.body: Such a great upgrade from the Kindle I bought in 2010. Loved that Kindle, but it no longer worked for downloads and book purchases as it was an older operating system. Amazon offered a discount to upgrade, so I took advantage of that offer and bought this Kindle paper white 16gb. It's really a great e-reader. So small and light yet many great features. It can hold up to 5500 books, and I know that is much more than enough for me. It reacts so much faster than my older kindle and set up , pairing with my iPhone took about 3 mins...so easy. I love the screen..no glare, really looks like the paper in a book. Easy on the eyes, so I will be using this in bed before going to sleep. Still learning all the tricks of the screen, but it's easy to figure out as you go along. It says the battery lasts for 10 weeks, and if that is true, that will be incredible. Looking forward to enjoying many books on my new Kindle paperwhite

reviews.body: This is an excellent reading device for those who have so many books that they cannot store any more. I’ve already foreclosed 25 novels and it takes minimal room on the device. It is easily charged, and the charge lasts quite a good while. The typeface is easy to read, and the instructions are very clear and easy to follow. I anticipate many good years of service from this lightweight, convenience-oriented product.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 24, 2026
reviews.title: Perfect Kindle! I love it so much!
reviews.body: I lost my old one at a resort, and I needed it again so badly I bought it again right away. It’s pretty expensive, but it is 100% worth it. It just like a real book, but with many different inside it, and you can turn the brightness off and still see the words. It fits great in my hand, especially when I’m reading in my bed at night. You can also turn warmth up to adjust to the light around you.
reviews.variant: Color: BlackOption: Without Kindle UnlimitedOffer Type: Ad-supported

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 28, 2026
reviews.title: Awesome reader
reviews.body: Love it 90% of the time. Reading on it is pretty great and it's very sturdy. Ive read around 500 books on it so far and if you just want it to read it's hard to compare the battery life and general ease of use. I don't have a screen protector and I don't have the case and I can confidently say this thing doesn't need that protection. Ive dropped it, chipped the outside edge bumper somehow and it's been smashed at least a few times. Works fine, Its pretty flexible and I think that saves it from most damage that would otherwise mess with it. It does have some issues though, Navigating the menus could be better. The touch controls can be a little frustrating sometimes even when I know how they are supposed to work. Like accidentally clicking links in books when Im trying to turn to a new page or go back to my library. Book covers look meh at best on it and sometimes I look at them using my iPad. My biggest gripe would be one handed reading. If I hold the kindle with my left hand its challenging to turn the page with a finger (the left side of the screen turns back the page) You might be wandering when you would read with one hand, lazy reading on the couch or in bed. It does seem to have issues updating with other readers, like switching between the kindle and my phone or iPad. If I only switched between my phone and tablet my pages would always be about were I left off... not so much with the kindle in the mix. That isn't normally a concern because I switch less now that I have a kindle.. but if you still want to switch devices it isn't updating activity between devices often though that gives an opportunity to read different things on other devices which ive done and that is nice sometimes too. Read more Read more
reviews.variant: Color: RaspberryOption: Without Kindle UnlimitedOffer Type: Ad-supported

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 27, 2026
reviews.title: Better than Expected
reviews.body: My old Kindle was around 7 or 8 years old and was getting tired, so I decided to get a new one. This is a lot nicer than the old models. The higher resolution does make quite a difference and is great for reading. Along with adjusting the brightness, you can also adjust the tint from levels of bright white to page yellow. Very nice. I also didn't think I would notice the faster page turns, but I do, it's a plus. And they added some more minor personal options that I really like. Overall, a good buy. Note: I've been happily using the smaller Kindle for years. I paid extra for this 7" model, and it was a very good decision. Technically, it reads the same (font size), but the larger one just allows some white space for a near-book experience.
reviews.variant: Color: BlackOption: Without Kindle UnlimitedOffer Type: Ad-supported

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 15, 2024
reviews.title: Perfect little carry-along...
reviews.body: I'm a relatively new e-ink convert, having bought a tricked-out Kindle Scribe exactly two years ago (when the device was brand new). Long story short? I loved that device... Literally, to death... After dropping it on terrazzo floors in SEVERAL airports world-wide, not to mention the myriad spills at home on more forgiving carpet. In fact, I loved it so much that when the 11th gen Scribe went on fire-sale a few weeks ago, I bought the cheapest version to replace my original (recently non-functional) unit, simply swapping my old premium pen for the basic one. Clearly, I loved the device enough to buy it again... I adore the ability to mark up .pdf's, the note-taking capabilities and that even with this extra functionality, it's still a very limited device that allows me to focus on reading first. What I don't love, and never have? The weight. It's heavy to the point that I never used it if I couldn't prop it on something. Which led to it being dropped. A lot. Which led to the original unit failing. Not that it wasn't pretty sturdy... I mean, it survived repeated collisions with very unforgiving surfaces. It even weathered some relatively large liquid spills on and near it despite not being water resistant. Now? I just use the Scribe when I need to mark up .pdf's (like, when I'm gaming... I can keep my character sheets on the Kindle), or need something that can display larger printed materials... Like RPG basic books or comics. Hence, the new Kindle Paperwhite gen 12... Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 12, 2025
reviews.title: Beautiful Device! (Upg. frm 2018 Kindle PW)
reviews.body: I received my Kindle PW (Paper White) 2024 yesterday! My Kindle History Up to Current: I was using the previous PW which I loved until losing it on a flight - so I dusted off my 2018 PW, popped on a new case and have been using it again for the past 2 years again. Initially I was holding out for the Colorsoft version. However, when it finally came out the reviews were mediocre to poor. The clincher against my buying the Colorsoft was reading that even in B&W mode, the resolution wasn't as crisp as the standard Kindle PW products. So given the choice between mediocre resolution + mediocre color vs excellent B&W I opted for the 2024 PW. From reading reviews on it across the spectrum here, I admit I had the return already prepared. However, my unit arrived in beautiful condition. Color: The chief thing I was looking for was the complaint regarding predominantly yellow screen with the bottom ‘yellower’ band. Not sure if these were faults with the earlier units. reviews.variant: Color: BlackOption: Without Kindle UnlimitedOffer Type: Ad-supported

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 31, 2025
reviews.title: Hold A Thousand Books in Your Hand
reviews.body: Imagine holding a thousand books in your hand. Imagine wearing your jammies while getting that next book in your series, and starting into it a minute later. Imagine reading at night in bed without needing a lamp because your book is backlit (and the light doesn’t hurt your eyes). Imagine having your kindle read to you (even if you don’t have audiobooks) with text to speech while you drive or fold laundry. The 12th edition of the Kindle Paperwhite is a fabulous device and well-worth considering if you read a lot. It’s not too big, not heavy, waterproof, and easy to carry in your purse or backpack. Note that if you get a cover with it (highly recommended), when you open the cover the Kindle turns on automatically to the last page you were reading. With the cover open it feels like you’re holding a thin book -- or fold back the cover if you prefer. The Kindle E-reader is not a computer. It’s not a tablet or a phone. It is a gizmo that acts like a book with a black-and-white flat e-ink screen showing typed sentences and paragraphs. You turn pages one at a time, just like a book. There is no movement on the page and no distraction or advertisement*. It is just text, plus any images that the author has included to illustrate his book. *NOTE: The advertisements on the Kindle are on the main page not the individual book pages and just show books you might like. They are unobtrusive. Read more Read more

reviews.title: Great but gets a bit slow over time
reviews.body: I’ve bought a bunch of these Amazon Fire TV Stick for family and have them on pretty much every TV in the house. They’re quick to set up, easy to use, and especially worth it when on sale. Over time they do get a bit slower, my oldest one is a little glitchy now but for how cheap they are, it’s not a big deal. Only thing I don’t like is how hard it is to force close apps.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 30, 2026
reviews.title: Great product
reviews.body: This is my third fire stick in our home, they work great, I even use them on our smart TV’s to access apps that aren’t available on the TV. They seem to have better range than my smart TV as well which was the reason for this purchase, I am currently using it on a TV in the garage.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 19, 2026
reviews.title: Fast easy set up, smooth streaming
reviews.body: This is my 6th Amazon Fire TV stick. We have them on 3 TV's in our home and they get some pretty heavy use, so after a couple of years, I have to buy new ones because the old ones either stop working or slow down tremendously. This is a newer version and has HD quality, Alexa voice , pre- installed apps and takes just minutes to set up. This one I purchased to replace the one on my daughters TV in her room and I was about to set it up for her (she's 12) when she says, "I got this mom". "Ok great just let me know if you need help getting it set up and paired because sometimes the volume doesn't Always pair properly on first try". I go about my house work expecting to hear my daughters voice yell "Mom, can you help me"? Never came. My 12 year old set it up all on her own, no help from mom. That is how easy it is to set up the fire TV Stick. Albeit my daughter is a bright gifted Honor Roll student, lol. Seriously though you don't need to be tech savvy to set this up or use it. It's got Alexa voice built in so you can just press the blue button on the remote and ask Alexa to find a program for you. I've noticed zero buffering, connects to our wifi fast and easy and loads apps quickly. Even with our smart TVs, having a fire TV Stick is a plus. But if you have an older TV or an older smart TV that doesn't update anymore, this is the way to go. The design is easy to understand and flows smoothly. Definitely worth every penny. While I was ordering this one I read that Amazon will even take your old fire sticks that don't work anymore. Since I've got a few of those lying around, I think I'll check into it. For $20 , we get several years out of the fire stick so it's worth it to me. Read more Read more

reviews.body: Okay had to rewrite my review I plugged my firestick straight into the wall instead of the power strip and now it works again I don’t know why it only works in the wall outlet but it works now.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 13, 2026
reviews.title: Would recommend as a reliable, cost friendly option in comparison to competitors!
reviews.body: I went back and forth on getting this watch for quite a while due to the shorter battery life but I’m so glad I took the leap! I had a Garmin that I loved and would have stuck with instead but it doesn’t work with Natural Cycles. I went back and forth for well over a month between this and the Oura Ring. I’m really pleased with my choice given all the Oura battery failures. Overall, an Apple Watch is an Apple Watch. They are consistent, intuitive, and handy. I shut off all the extra fuss notifications to keep it “quieter” and it’s been great as someone who doesn’t love constantly being plugged in. The 11 has a better battery than my old 8 I had before I went to Garmin which has been great and it charges fast. Is it as convenient as the DAYS and extreme durability you get from Garmin, definitely not - but it does what it’s supposed to. It’s a clean, minimal looking watch and I appreciate the increased durability. The value for money is almost unmatched- and IS unmatched (in my humble opinion) when you look at the costs of Garmin and Oura these days VS reliability (my husband has the Garmin Instinct and it had nonstop software issues until recently and to fix it we had to go through a whole process and delete files - plug it into a computer etc, Oura’s batteries dying within a year). Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 21, 2026
reviews.title: Solid daily smartwatch that fits real life
reviews.body: I bought this smartwatch to get a clearer picture of my sleep, heart activity, and workouts without pulling out my phone all day. The sleep score and health monitoring sounded helpful, and the option to take ECG readings whenever I want and track blood oxygen felt reassuring for daily use. Day to day it works smoothly. The always on display is easy to glance at, and the watch stays comfortable for all day wear and even sleep. It handles workouts with solid metrics, and the band stays secure during runs. Battery life is up to 24 hours of normal use, and a quick 15 minute charge can add several hours. What stood out is how the insights feel actionable rather than just numbers. The alerts for high or low heart rate show up when I need them, and the sleep score lines up with how rested I feel. The durable glass plus a 50 meter water resistance makes it feel ready for everyday life, and safety features like fall detection and automatic check-in when I reach a destination give me extra peace of mind. One small caveat is that the size is a bit bulky on smaller wrists and the wealth of features can take a little time to navigate without checking the app often. It also depends on having it paired with a phone for some functions. Still, in everyday use it feels accurate and reliable, and the overall experience is genuinely satisfying. Overall, it's earned a five-star impression. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 21, 2026
reviews.title: I love that Amazon to pre-configures the device for you before it even arrives at the door
reviews.body: I deliberately stayed with this Echo Dot model for a reason. I’ve had the Show, and while it’s nice to see pictures or who is at the door, I noticed it simply didn't last as long as my original Echo that I’ve had for years and still functions today. I ended up replacing my Show with this one because I prioritize the long-term functionality. I like the size and the charcoal color, but the real upgrades are in the sound—it's much clearer than my original one. The simplicity of the setup is where Amazon really gets it right. I love that they allow you to pre-configure the device before it even arrives at your door; it was basically a plug-and-play situation. As soon as I hooked it to my Wi-Fi, it already knew my name. It’s a simple, reliable upgrade that actually works for the long haul

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 23, 2026
reviews.title: Small Speaker, Big Upgrade with Alexa+
reviews.body: We love the Amazon Echo Dot (newest model). Sound quality is surprisingly good for the size, and it’s perfect for a bedroom setup. Alexa+ feels like a noticeable upgrade... faster, more helpful, and easier to use day-to-day. Overall, a great little device that just works.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 5, 2026
reviews.title: Title: The perfect gift! Amazing sound and very helpful
reviews.body: I bought the newest Amazon Echo Dot as a gift, and it turned out to be an excellent choice. It’s exactly what they needed. Despite its compact size, the speaker delivers a surprisingly vibrant and clear sound, making it great for listening to music, podcasts, or even just setting timers and alarms. The design is sleek and fits perfectly in any room, but the real value is in how well it integrates with Alexa. It does exactly what it’s supposed to do: it’s responsive, easy to set up, and incredibly helpful for daily tasks. Whether it's controlling smart lights, checking the weather, or answering quick questions, it has become an essential part of their routine. If you are looking for a reliable, high-quality smart speaker to give to someone (or even for yourself), this model is a win. It’s a powerful little device that offers great performance for the price. They use it every day and couldn't be happier with it!

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
reviews.title: Sounds Great — But I Miss the Audio Jack
reviews.body: I’ve owned several Echo Dots over the years, and the newest model definitely sounds better than the older ones. The audio is fuller, clearer, and surprisingly punchy for such a small speaker. For casual music, podcasts, and general Alexa use, it does exactly what it’s supposed to do. Setup is simple, Alexa responsiveness is solid, and it integrates easily into a smart home setup. As a compact speaker for a bedroom, office, or kitchen, it’s a nice upgrade in sound quality compared to earlier generations. That said — I really hate that Amazon removed the 3.5mm audio jack on the latest models. That was a genuinely useful feature. Being able to hardwire it into better speakers or an amp gave you flexibility. Now you’re locked into Bluetooth or the internal speaker, which feels like a step backward for people who actually use their equipment beyond basic voice commands. If you just want a standalone smart speaker with better sound than older Dots, it’s a solid choice. But if you relied on the audio-out jack like I did, be aware it’s gone — and that’s disappointing.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 31, 2026
reviews.title: Great quality fun for the whole family, you can't go wrong with this item.
reviews.body: I ordered this one to replace my echo dot the 3rd generation . Alexa + works really great meant to order the blue one but ordered the black one looks really great. Will buy again. Easy to set up took just a minute to set up great quality has voice recognition fun for the whole family.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 14, 2023
reviews.title: Another great smart home purchase, to say the least.
reviews.body: My previous Echo Dot, purchased back in 2018, was a third-generation. It was a white shuffleboard stone covered in cloth and it is amazing. I'll never say it's not; in fact it's so amazing that I passed it on to my mom and she enjoys it a great deal. So, my main motivation for purchasing this, the fifth generation issued in 2022 – aside from familiarity with the brand – was I needed a Matter hub so my then-incoming Nanoleaf smart lightbulbs could work with my voice. But of course it is so much more. First thing I see is that the Echo Dot is no longer a white shuffleboard stone but is a smaller, navy blue sphere. Aside from being my favorite color, and thus very pleasing to look at, I find it that it is not practical any longer to mount on my wall because of its increased focus on the forward firing speaker; if I had mounted it, it would not be effective in spreading out to my entire room – the top of the speaker is reserved for the buttons which were present on the third-generation: volume up, volume down, action/wake and mute microphone, which leave no room for a speaker in that area. So it sits pretty on my side table facing the rest of my room, and does fine. First thing I hear is the sound quality is so much better than the third-generation Dot. The third-generation was okay in that regard, but it tended to sound a little overexposed when the volume was up high. Now, I don't find myself connecting via Bluetooth to my sound bar nearly as much because the speaker quality is just that good. The quiet parts of music are quiet and calming the way they should be, but the bass thumps quite effectively, surprisingly for such a small and lightweight speaker. Read more Read more

reviews.title: Love my Firesticks!
reviews.body: I had just moved into my new apartment and found some older flat screens off FB Marketplace. They weren't too old, but a little outdated and didnt have streaming apps on them, so I opted for getting a couple firesticks for them. Super easy to set up, no lagging from the devices, and pretty quick to set up too. Best decision I've made. They work great. Video quality is top-tier even if the TV quality itself isnt the best on the market. They're pretty solid but easy to hide behind your TV. Depending on your TV functions, the remote will also control your TV so you can ditch the main remote when you need to turn the volume up and down or the TV on and off. It doesn't offer input switch though.

reviews.body: Wow!!! I wish I'd purchased these 4 years ago, thinking of all the money I could have saved, especially as a retired, fixed-income person!!! Easy-peasy to set up and use!!! With our existing Amazon Prime account, I added a couple of subscriptions, and we are extremely happy with performance, content, and $$ savings now!!! Thanks, Amazon!!! P.S., we do not have an Alexa, and also, these units are completely compatible with our 16 year old televisions!!!

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 25, 2026
reviews.title: I don’t know why others try when hands down fire stick is the best! They cannot compare!
reviews.body: I’m genuinely impressed with the Amazon Fire Stick 4K. It completely upgraded my home entertainment experience. From the moment I opened the box, everything was straightforward. Setup took less than 10 minutes: plug it in, connect to Wi-Fi, sign into my accounts, and I was ready to go. The streaming quality is incredible. The 4K Ultra HD picture is sharp, with rich colors and great contrast—especially noticeable when watching movies with Dolby Vision and HDR. Even fast-moving scenes play smoothly without buffering or lag, which was a big issue with my previous streaming device. The interface is clean and easy to navigate. I love that all my apps like Netflix, Prime Video, YouTube, and Disney+ are in one place, and switching between them is quick. The remote is another highlight—it’s simple, responsive, and the Alexa voice control works surprisingly well. I can just say what I want to watch, and it pulls it up instantly, saving a lot of time. Performance-wise, it’s fast and reliable. Apps load quickly, and I haven’t experienced any freezing or crashes. I also appreciate how compact it is—it fits neatly behind my TV without any clutter. Overall, for the price, this is one of the best streaming devices out there. It’s user-friendly, powerful, and delivers excellent picture quality. I would absolutely recommend the Fire Stick 4K to anyone looking to upgrade their TV setup without spending a fortune. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 26, 2026
reviews.title: Voice search actually works
reviews.body: I wasn’t expecting a huge difference, but the Fire TV Stick 4K really impressed me. Setup took about 5 minutes, and everything just worked. Menus feel faster, apps open almost instantly, and streaming is smooth even during peak hours. The 4K picture quality is sharp, and the voice search is surprisingly helpful—I can just say “action movies with car chases” and it actually finds good options. The remote is simple, Alexa works well, and having all the apps in one place makes everything convenient. For the price, this feels like a real upgrade—easy to use, fast, and reliable.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 8, 2026
reviews.title: Great Streamer, But Remote Needs Work
reviews.body: I’ve been using the Firestick 4K for a while now and overall, I'm quite pleased with its performance and ease of use. The setup was a breeze, and I really appreciate the consistent user interface it provides across all my TVs, which is especially helpful for my wife. However, I have a serious issue with the remote. It feels cheap and flimsy, and honestly, the removal of the IR Blaster is baffling. I don't understand how this design made it to market, as it complicates setup for those of us with soundbars or surround systems that connect via optical cables. Additionally, the dedicated app buttons are a real annoyance. I often find myself accidentally launching other apps instead of streaming my preferred content on YouTube TV. I wish there were an option to disable them or even assign them to apps I actually use. If you're considering this device and can find it on sale for about $25, it's worth it for the streaming capabilities, but be prepared to navigate a frustrating remote experience.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 20, 2025
reviews.title: Reliable Instax Mini Film with Consistent Results
reviews.body: This Instax Mini film performs exactly as expected and delivers consistent, recognizable Instax-style results. The film loads easily into the camera and develops smoothly, with no issues during use. Colors are generally vibrant, and images have the classic soft contrast and slightly warm tone that Instax film is known for. Exposure is reliable in good lighting conditions, and the white border remains clean and uniform, making the prints suitable for scrapbooks, albums, or display. The film develops within the typical timeframe and produces clear images without streaking or uneven processing. That said, image quality is highly dependent on lighting, and low-light conditions can result in darker or less detailed photos, which is typical for instant film but still worth noting. The cost per photo is also relatively high compared to digital options, so it’s best suited for special moments rather than frequent casual use. Overall, this is dependable, authentic Instax Mini film that works well for everyday instant photography. A solid choice for Instax camera owners who want predictable results, with minor limitations inherent to the format. Read more Read more

reviews.title: Overall, a great purchase if you need a simple, no-fuss Roku TV remote replacement.
reviews.body: I recently purchased this pack of replacement remote controls for my Roku TV, and overall I’m very satisfied with the value and performance. The fact that it comes with two remotes is a huge plus—great for having a backup or keeping one in another room. Setup was incredibly easy. There’s no complicated pairing process—just insert batteries and it works instantly with my TCL Roku TV. The buttons are responsive, and the layout is very similar to the original remote, so there’s no learning curve. I also like that it’s compatible with a wide range of Roku TV brands like Hisense, Onn, Sharp, Element, Westinghouse, and Philips. That makes it a versatile option if you have multiple TVs in your home. The only thing to note is that it doesn’t work with Roku sticks or boxes, so make sure you’re using a Roku TV before buying. Other than that, it’s a reliable and budget-friendly replacement option. Overall, a great purchase if you need a simple, no-fuss Roku TV remote replacement.

reviews.body: This 6 ft surge protector power strip has been very practical for everyday use. The size is compact enough to fit easily along a wall or under furniture, but it still provides plenty of outlets thanks to the 8 widely spaced plugs and additional USB ports. One feature I especially like is the low-profile plug, which saves space when plugging it into an outlet behind furniture. It sits flatter against the wall, making it much easier to place a TV stand or couch close without bending cords. The outlet spacing works well for larger adapters, and having both standard outlets and USB ports helps reduce the need for extra charging bricks. I can’t personally verify the level of surge protection it provides over time, but as a functional power strip for organizing multiple devices, it has worked well so far and feels like a good value. Overall, a convenient and space-efficient option for home offices, entertainment centers, or anywhere you need extra outlets.

Unfortunately, I didn’t see the review about the high frequency noise until after I bought it :( This power strip emits a high pitch electric frequency, I can hear it whenever it is on and it hurts my ears and makes me clench my jaw instinctively. The pressure is unpleasant, and even if I hide it behind or under something, I can always hear the buzz/hum. It should be noted I am also autistic, and am very sensitive to higher pitches. It’s a constant ringing I hear and feel and makes it virtually impossible to have anything plugged in without the electrical frequency.

name: JBL Vibe Beam - True Wireless JBL Deep Bass Sound Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.2, Water & Dust Resistant, Hands-Free Call with VoiceAware, Up to 32 Hours of Battery Life (Black)
description: "Bring your sound everywhere. With bass you can feel, up to 32 total hours of battery life, and a secure, comfortable stick-closed design, the splash and dust resistant JBL Vibe Beam are designed for your daily entertainment".
highlights: JBL Deep Bass Sound: Get the most from your mixes with high-quality audio from secure, reliable earbuds with 8mm drivers featuring JBL Deep Bass Sound

Comfortable fit: The ergonomic, stick-closed design of the JBL Vibe Beam fits so comfortably you may forget you're wearing them. The closed design excludes external sounds, enhancing the bass performance

Up to 32 (8h + 24h) hours of battery life and speed charging: With 8 hours of battery life in the earbuds and 24 in the case, the JBL Vibe Beam provide all-day audio. When you need more power, you can speed charge an extra two hours in just 10 minutes.
Hands-free calls with VoiceAware: When you're making hands-free stereo calls on the go, VoiceAware lets you balance how much of your own voice you hear while talking with others
Water and dust resistant: From the beach to the bike trail, the IP54-certified earbuds and IPX2 charging case are water and dust resistant for all-day experiences

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 21, 2023
reviews.title: Top quality audio after a few adjustments
reviews.body: This review is about the audio features of the JBL Vibe Beam earbuds. I'm an audio engineer so audio quality is very important to me. As such, I use wired connections as much as possible to maintain the integrity of the audio signal. However, many newer consumer-level products no longer have audio jacks, so I finally decided to invest in some wireless earbuds as a backup to the other earphones and headphones that I use. I didn't want to spend a lot of money as I didn't foresee myself using this product very much, and since this is my first foray into wireless audio earphones, if they were just ok or they broke after a while, then I wouldn't feel too bad about the little money that I spent. Also, I wanted to focus on finding wireless earbuds with excellent audio quality first as I already have an excellent high-end bluetooth earpiece for cell phone use, and I wouldn't be using this product as a bluetooth headset for my phone. After doing much research, I chose the JBL Vibe Beam's as they were at my price point, I'm familiar with JBL products, and I could reasonably expect that they were likely deliver the high audio quality that I've come to expect from JBL products. In my research, I read reviews that complained about the harshness of the high end with high frequency instruments like cymbals or the lack of bass, so I was a bit concerned. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 7, 2026
reviews.title: Easy to use
reviews.body: The JBL Vibe Beam earbuds are truly impressive for their price. The sound is clear with deep, punchy bass that JBL is known for, making music more enjoyable. They fit comfortably in the ears, even during movement or daily activities. The battery life is solid, and the connection is stable without interruptions. Overall, they offer great value and are an excellent choice if you're looking for quality wireless earbuds without spending too much.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
reviews.title: Excellent product for the price
reviews.body: For the price point, these earbuds have been awesome!! Instead of getting overly expensive earbuds that I might lose or not like, I bought these as starter Bluetooth earbuds and haven't opted for anything else since. I mainly use them to watch shows or listen to music (especially when walking). I sometimes use them for phone calls. For me, the sound on calls is clear but I have gotten the feedback that it can sometimes be loud with background noise for the person on the other end of the line. I have small ears and the buds are a surprisingly comfortable fit. I did separately buy a silicone lanyard that I use every time with the earbuds so that I don't lose them. Without the lanyard, I am confident I would have lost the buds. The charging capacity is also great. The buds last a long time before needing to be charged. The control buttons on the buds are very easy to use and work reliably. These sync well to my phone and tablet but I've had issues syncing them to airplane screens. I also bought a silicone case for it, which has been useful.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 25, 2025
reviews.title: JBL Vibe Beam vs Soundcore Liberty 4 NC vs Soundcore P40i
reviews.body: I ordered 3 different earbuds all within a similar price range(+\- $30) to figure out which one I liked the most - Soundcore Liberty 4 NC, P40i, and JBL Vibe Beam. Here are my notes and comparisons. The Liberty 4 NC is my favorite but there is one aspect I don’t like. They Liberty’s are very clear with good bass and their app allows you to customize EQ settings which is deal breaker for me. (All three pairs have custom EQs within their respective apps). The EQ settings and customization from the JBL app are far superior as they give you unlimited options. The JBL Vibe was less comfortable but more so than JBL Tune(ordered these a while back, the Tunes fall out constantly!). The JBL Vibes are not noise canceling and I knew that before hand so I’m not knocking it. The JBLs are fine for the price point but not the best for quality as they use 8mm drivers vs 11mm drivers for the Soundcore. The one thing I don’t like with the Liberty’s is the slightest white noise I get when between songs or silent parts. The P40i’s and Vibes don’t have that problem. The P40i and JBLs have great bass and fit well but they lack in higher frequencies and that’s important for audiophiles. The P40i also have a much friendlier app interface with many more preset features. BUT the Liberty’s have an option to set up your “ideal listening EQ” and that is a great feature as it adjusts to your preferred sound through samples and takes the guess work out. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 12, 2026
reviews.title: Survived washer and dryer cycle!!
reviews.body: Guess who I bought this for? Yes, the husband. Guess who did the laundry and forgot to empty the pockets and left these JBL ear buds in his jeans pockets? Yes the husband. The sound quality of these are nice, especially for the price point. But what blew me away was what happened last week. It got thrown into a regular wash cycle and then a high heat dry cycle and survived and still works! Well, one of them worked right away.. the other one, took two days but works like normal, I guess it had to dry out? Yes, they were in the charging case when they were washed, but bothe the ear buds and charging case still works perfectly fine! I guess they were telling the truth when they said "water resistant". I bought another pair anyway because I thought the other earbud was never going to work, well I was wrong! The second order is worth keeping as this might be the Nokia of earbuds, lol! *If you know what I'm talking about, you're my people, lol 😆) Happy husband said "see, nothing to worry about, they were water resistant".. says the man who washed his earbuds.. I'm glad JBL offers good quality earbuds, highly recommend!

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 28, 2026
reviews.title: Boom goes the bass and great quality
reviews.body: I’ve been a lifelong Apple earbuds listener. I never thought I’d be so impressed with a set of ear buds. I tried my gf earbuds and after hearing hers I was hooked. Bought these and have been very happy 2 months now and zero issues. Great value for what you get. Several ear pieces to choose the right fit for you. Setting up the Bluetooth was easy and simple to figure out.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 23, 2025
reviews.title: Decent, but far from perfect (for me).
reviews.body: I've had these for nearly two months, using them at my job working with dogs. Typically, I listen to long-form videos on YouTube or music while working outside in a yard full of dogs. Having come from using some relatively basic ear buds bought at Target, I wanted something a little more durable and with stronger sound. I found these JBL buds for a decent price and decided to go for it, knowing that just about anything from JBL would sound better than what I had. All in all, they sounded great for the price and I enjoyed the easy touch controls for the most part, but there were some significant problems with how I was trying to use them on the job. For one thing, I'd never had ear buds in this design, with an Air Pod style handle or whatever and I worried that it would be unwieldy. Fortunately, it actually made getting them out of my ear or out of my pocket easier - so that was good. However, the touch controls were a little too sensitive for me, as I'd often find myself fumbling to grab one of the buds from a pocket and accidentally hit that touch spot in the process, either restarting my media or skipping onto something else entirely. Read more Read more
