reviews.title: Solid Earbuds for the Price, Just Don’t Stay in Perfectly
reviews.body: These earbuds were great overall. The sound is amazing and gets really loud, and I was honestly impressed with the quality. The app is solid and easy to use pairing was smooth, and I like that you can turn on ambient sound and mess with different settings to customize how they sound. The only issue I had is the fit they tended to fall out of my ear sometimes, especially if I’m moving around, so I found myself adjusting them more than I wanted. I also got them at a really good price because they were on sale, so for what I paid, I’m definitely happy with them and would recommend them.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 6, 2026
reviews.title: Great Power Strip for Electronics and Everyday Use
reviews.body: I’m using this Yintar 6ft power strip behind my couch, and it’s been working out great so far. I have my electric couch plugged in, along with a heating blanket, a Google Nest, and I’ll plug my laptop in on occasion too. It handles everything without any issues. The six outlets give me plenty of room, and the USB ports are nice to have so I’m not constantly swapping chargers. The 6-foot cord is a little longer than I really need for this spot, but that’s what I ordered, so no complaints there. It actually makes it easier to tuck everything neatly behind the couch without cords being stretched. The power strip feels solid and well made, not flimsy, and I like knowing it has surge protection for the electronics I have plugged into it. Overall, it does exactly what it’s supposed to do. Simple, clean, and useful for everyday home use. I’d buy it again.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 7, 2026
reviews.title: Reliable six outlet surge protector for a tidy workspace
reviews.body: I bought this to replace a tangle of cords cluttering my desk. With six AC outlets and three USB ports, it handles my laptop charger, monitor, lamp, speakers, and a couple of phone chargers all at once. I looked for solid surge protection and the 1680 joules rating, plus the ETL listing, and it checked those boxes. The outlets are widely spaced, so I can fit bulky adapters without blocking neighbors. A six foot cord gives me flexibility to place it where outlets are easy to reach. In daily use it sits on my desk, and I can reach everything easily without bending down. The USB ports charge steadily while the main device stays plugged in, with two USB-A ports up to 2.4A each and a USB-C port up to 3A for faster charging. The three ports total 15W. The flat six foot plug and the included cable ties help keep the setup neat and the cord tucked away. What stood out is how quickly protection kicks in when there is a surge and how quiet it is in normal use. The wide voltage design means I can use it in spaces with different outlets without worrying about compatibility. The six outlets stay accessible even with larger plugs in use, and the overall build feels solid enough for everyday use. One small caveat is that when all three USB ports are drawing power, the total charging speed can dip a bit, but I still get reliable everyday charging. Other than that minor note, the experience is consistently smooth and confident. Overall I am happy with this unit and would rate it five stars for keeping my devices safe and organized. name: Oura Ring 4 Sizing Kit - Size Before You Buy The Oura Ring 4 - Unique Sizing, Not Standard US Ring Sizes - Receive Credit for Purchase
description: The Oura Ring 4 Sizing Kit allows for you and those around you to find the ideal fit before purchasing. With a sizing kit purchase, a 10 credit will automatically be applied to your Oura Ring 4 at checkout. A properly fitting Oura Ring 4 will result in the highest possible data accuracy, comfort, and overall experience. Oura Ring 4 sizes are slightly different from standard ring sizes, so it is important to size yourself using our tools and to follow our sizing instructions. Oura Ring 4 works on all fingers and can be worn on either hand. For optimal accuracy, we recommend wearing your Oura Ring 4 on your index, middle, or ring finger.
highlights: OURA RING 4 SIZING KIT - The Oura Ring 4 Sizing Kit is the ideal way to find the perfect size. It’s a custom sizer that allows for you, and those around you, to find the perfect fit before purchasing
SIZE BEFORE YOU BUY - Oura Ring 4 sizes are different from standard ring sizes. Size yourself with the Oura Ring 4 Sizing Kit. For additional details, see “From the brand” section below

NOT STANDARD SIZING - Oura Ring 4 sizes are slightly different from standard ring sizes, so it is important to size yourself using our Oura Ring 4 Sizing Kit. Oura Ring 4 is available in sizes 4-15. There are no half sizes
COMFORT AND ACCURACY - A properly fitting Oura Ring will result in the highest data accuracy, comfort, and overall experience

CHOOSE A FINGER - Oura Ring 4 works on all fingers and can be worn on either hand. For optimal accuracy, we recommend wearing your Oura Ring 4 on your index, middle, or ring finger. Size the exact finger you plan to wear your Oura Ring 4 on

HOW IT SHOULD FIT - Oura Ring 4 should fit securely and comfortably at the base of your finger. Your ring should not rotate as you go about normal daily tasks. We recommend wearing your ring sizer for at least 24 hours to confirm your best fit

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 7, 2026
reviews.title: Accurate Sizing, Helpful Trial Period, and Worth It Before Buying the Ring
reviews.body: I purchased the Oura Ring 4 sizing kit because I didn’t want to guess on sizing for a $300+ health device, and I’m very glad I didn’t skip this step. The sizing kit is well-designed and closely mimics the look and feel of the actual ring. Oura recommends wearing a test ring for at least 24 hours. Still, I found it helpful to wear mine for several days to account for normal fluctuations in finger size (sleep, activity, temperature, etc.). This made a noticeable difference in choosing the correct size. One of the biggest benefits is accuracy. I typically wear a size 7 in rings, but after testing, I found a size 6 fit best for the Oura Ring. Without the kit, I likely would have ordered the wrong size. The fit of the actual ring matched the sizer exactly. I also appreciated being able to test the ring during real-life conditions. I wore it during work shifts that involved frequent handwashing, hand sanitizer use, and repeated glove changes, and it held up well. That gave me confidence in both the sizing and the practicality of wearing the ring daily. Another advantage is saving time. Purchasing the sizing kit separately allowed me to determine my size right away and order the ring without waiting for the standard sizing process (which can add a couple of weeks). For anyone eager to receive their ring sooner, this is a helpful option. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 26, 2026
reviews.title: Works perfectly. Love it!
reviews.body: I absolutely loved this Fire TV Stick 4K Max. It is fast, easy to use, and the picture quality is very clear and in high definition. The setup was very simple, and within minutes I was already enjoying my favorite shows and movies. It is definitely an excellent product, practical and modern. I totally recommend it for turning any TV into a Smart TV and having instant entertainment.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 14, 2026
reviews.title: Fast, fluid and excellent 4K quality - totally worth it
reviews.body: Amazon Fire TV Stick 4K Max is the best I have ever tried to turn any TV into a fast and complete Smart TV. It works super fluid, opens the apps fast and the quality in 4K looks excellent with very sharp colors. Controlling with Alexa is very practical, you can search for content or open apps with your voice alone. I also really liked that it hardly gets stuck and the WiFi is very stable, ideal for watching movies or IPTV without interruptions. The only "negative" thing is that the main screen has a lot of advertising and content from Amazon, but outside of that the performance is excellent. Overall, it is a powerful, fast and very easy-to-use device. It is totally worth the price 👍

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 11, 2026
reviews.title: Incredible Performance and Quality in a Compact Device
reviews.body: The Amazon Fire TV Stick 4K Max is, without a doubt, one of the best purchases I have made to enhance my home entertainment experience. From the very first use, you can tell just how fast and fluid it is: apps open almost instantly, and navigating through the menus is incredibly smooth. As for content, it offers access to practically every major platform—such as Netflix, Prime Video, Disney+, and more—so you won't be missing out on anything. In short, it is a powerful, fast, and comprehensive device—ideal for anyone looking to transform their TV into a modern entertainment hub. Highly recommended

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 23, 2026
reviews.title: Fire Stick 4K Max is excellent, a backlight on remote would be welcomed.
reviews.body: This Fire TV Stick 4K Max works seamlessly. Connectivity was a breeze. Buffering and responsiveness of control remain excellent. It works instantly switching from one streaming app to another and is compatible with our new Samsung 65” 4K (2026) TV. The one feature that would be welcomed is to have a backlight available on the remote for night use. Otherwise for speed, dependability, efficiency, responsiveness and ease of use, this Fire Stick is 5⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 18, 2026
reviews.title: Great Streaming Stick – Fast, Feature-Rich, and Easy to Use
reviews.body: Great Streaming Stick – Fast, Feature-Rich, and Easy to Use I'd give the Amazon Fire TV Stick 4K Max a solid 4 stars. The Wi-Fi 6E support delivers noticeably fast and stable streaming performance, and the 4K picture quality is sharp and impressive on a compatible TV. Setup is quick and intuitive, the interface is responsive, and having access to thousands of streaming apps plus free live TV without a cable or satellite subscription makes it a genuinely great value. The only reason I'm holding back a star is the heavy push toward Amazon's own content and ads in the interface, but overall it's a capable, fast, and easy-to-use streaming device worth having.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 24, 2026
reviews.title: Great App Organizer!
reviews.body: Plug and play in minutes! Organizes all my apps like Amazon, HULU, and whatever I subscribe to all in one place. It also downloads the free apps I get with my cable company automatically. If you’re all confused about what you’re subscribed to or what you have this is a great way to organize everything. No ongoing costs. A lot of free apps are just included and it has links to Amazon if you shop there etc. No trouble with buffering unless your modem is the issue. The remote is great and functions to turn on the TV and also controls volume, it’s the only remote I use.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 9, 2026
reviews.title: Fast, smooth, and very easy to use
reviews.body: The Fire TV Stick 4K Max is an excellent streaming device. Setup was quick and simple, and the performance is very fast and smooth. Apps load quickly, and streaming quality is great in 4K. The Wi-Fi 6E support makes a noticeable difference in speed and stability. Alexa voice control is very helpful for finding shows quickly. Overall, a great upgrade and definitely worth it!

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 24, 2024
reviews.title: Great device, but may not be for Plex users
reviews.body: For various reasons, I decided it was time to at least try a different streaming device, and I figured a FireTV Stick would be the obvious solution. I wanted a responsive device, and to try to ensure this, I figured I might as well go for the top model of the FireTV Stick, so I ordered the 4K Max. This device does not at all disappoint in that regard! It is very snappy while navigating through the UI and the various streaming apps I utilize, so I'm very pleased with that. It comes with a short HDMI extender, which is a great touch because it makes using a "stick" form device such as this not interfere with being in the way of your other HDMI ports. Make sure you use the power adapter it came with (or a USB adapter capable of outputting 2 Amps) because you will run in to problems quickly if it doesn't detect enough amperage. I rather wish it came with a longer cable, as it is not uncommon these days for a TV to be wall mounted, but that's just my own opinion. Setup is very easily done and has nice guidance every step of the way. Even getting the remote to work with my TV was super easy and included in the first time setup guidance. The remote ended up only being able to control my TV's power and volume, which is not a big deal, and I'm sure most individuals who buy this intend to use it as the main source of media to play on their TV. reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 8, 2026
reviews.title: Affordable but performs like higher-end earbuds
reviews.body: I bought the TOZO A1 Wireless Earbuds Bluetooth and honestly didn’t expect them to be this good for the price. They’ve ended up being some of the best earbuds I’ve owned. The sound quality is great, and I really like that you can control volume and skip tracks directly from the earbuds by tapping them instead of needing to use your phone. Setup was super easy, and they connected quickly without any issues. The battery life has also been impressive — they charge fast and last for many hours, easily getting through a full day of use. What I liked: Great sound quality for the price Touch controls for volume and track changes Easy setup and fast connection Long battery life with quick charging Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 20, 2026
reviews.title: Perfect fit, fantastic value, great sound
reviews.body: I honestly love these. The ear seal/fit is much better than many of the other inexpensive true wireless earbuds I've used and the battery life is perfect for my gym sessions which last around 2-3 hours. They're also great for noise suppression when I mow grass. As far as sound quality goes, they're not going to beat high-end IEMs in a quite room, but they're better than or at least on par with earbuds from a certain influencer marketed brand and are a third the price. I've bought two pairs which I've used daily for close to two years. Unfortunately, on of the pairs died recently, one earbud just ran out of battery and putting it in the case to charge results in the case getting very hot, but the remaining earbud is technically still usable. I'm thinking of getting a third pair because of how much I like the fit though. Just to touch on a few recommended topics, I did try wearing them in the shower when I first got them but the water resulted in the volume getting cranked in one direction as a long press on the ear bud acts as a volume control. I actually have the same issue when sweat puddles on an ear bud. A quick pat dry resolves that issue though. It doesn't have active noise cancelling, of course, but the passive noise cancelling is very good. These are cordless/true wireless so no cord length issues. And that should cover all the recommended topics. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 28, 2026
reviews.title: Honest Review
reviews.body: Great wireless headphones! Pro -Pretty noise canceling -Connect quickly after you set them up -Sound quality / bass is pretty nice -Clear -Great for music! Con - They begin to hurt my ears pretty quickly after putting them in 😞 - It may be user error but I haven’t had much luck with calls. If you touch the earbud while on the phone it will hang up. Also, when I answered a call with these the person on the other line couldn’t hear me or I couldn’t hear them. (But I don’t make calls thay often so it’s not a massive issue for me) Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 1, 2026
reviews.title: good quality earbuds!
reviews.body: These earbuds are really good for the price. They’re lightweight and sit comfortably in my ears without feeling bulky or like they’re going to fall out. Love the fact that they are Bluetooth and wireless, they instantly connect to my phone with i take them out the case. The sound quality is solid, especially the bass, which is actually pretty strong and sounds nice for everyday listening. I literally cannot hear anything if I have these in at maximum volume. I’ve seen some people mention that the battery life isn’t great, but in my experience, I can listen for hours without having any issues. They connect easily and the case is compact, which makes them convenient to carry around. Overall, these are a great option if you want affordable wireless earbuds that still perform well

They last all day while im working on a full charge, sound quality is great, AND they have survived going through the washer a few times because I forgot they were in my pocket... so basically they are idiot proof and we'll worth the money.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 25, 2026
reviews.title: Love the earbuds but not the case
reviews.body: I just received these earbuds today and I absolutely love them 🙌🏼 love that the A1 are smaller and fit perfectly in my ears without hurting them. So far I had them in for about an hour and no issues yet. I will update if that changes. Usually my left ear always hurts when using earbuds so I am these are different. Love the app and being able to change the sound effects. LOVE LOVE the translation option, super handy. The color pink is super cute. Definitely recommend Update: I have used these earbuds everyday since I got them. I haven’t had any issues so far, they are still very comfortable. The only issue I have it’s with the case lid, the lid it’s not secure enough and opens way too easily, if the lid gets hit or shaken a certain way it will open. Because of that I lower my stars. I don’t feel that my earbuds are really secure in the case and that I can loose them at anytime if I throw my case in my backpack or purse.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 21, 2026
reviews.title: 10/10, no notes
reviews.body: these little things are bulletproof. probably alien technology the government doesn't want you to know about, I don't know. but let me tell you a little story about these things. I ordered my first pair over two years ago. I had just gotten a new big kid job, and wanted some inexpensive headphones to get me through my days as I work outside as a horticulturist. in Florida. where I may as well be in a sauna all day. you expensive Beats are not surviving that amount of heat and moisture. I had a $25 gift card, and went searching for something under $20 that was gonna do the job, but not be catastrophic if I accidentally dropped them into the compost pile. and then I found these. cute colors, within budget-perfect for a girlie getting free headphones with girl math. I didn't expect much, I just needed something functional that would play my true crime while I decapitated flowers at work all day. and let me tell you, the sound on these little things was incredible. I was expecting the speaker system of an '06 Nissan altima passing you at 60mph in a residential area, but no. the sound was crisp, clear, and issue free. the Bluetooth connection was as automatic as needing the bathroom after your first sip of coffee. flawless. and then last week I finally did it. I forgot old faithful in my cargo pants pocket. it went through a wash cycle, and two dry cycles-if you know you know. and I didn't realize until 4 am Monday morning when I'm frantically looking for them, that I realized what I had done. I still took them with me to work, for emotional support, but to my utter disbelief these things still connected like they always did, and played my newest podcast episode like I hadnt tried to make them one of the victims the podcast hosts talked about. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 9, 2025
reviews.title: Wireless - Waterproof - Great Sound - Phone App - Mind Blowingly low price point for this quality!
reviews.body: I had been searching for a decent pair of wireless waterproof earbuds where the battery would play music for at least 4 hours on a full charge. I wasn't going to even consider any pair fitting my needs priced below $40, until my daughter told me about these, saying she really loved them and used them until she lost one of them after only a few weeks of use. So I bought them, and have all positive things to say about them, except for one thing. Here goes: PRO's: - They come with 4 pair of different sized rubber fitting tips - Using the pre-installled earbud tips, I was shocked in surprise that all it took was one quick finger press and they fit snugly and better than any other pair I have ever worn. As opposed to having to fiddle with them, twist and turn with a bit of force to make them stay in position. Not these - perfect instant snug fit that stays in place! - near continuous run time playing music or listening to audio books is at least 4 hours! Are you kidding me? For under $15, having 4-5+ hours battery time? Awesome! Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in Italy on November 2, 2025
reviews.title: (Le ho ancora dopo 2 anni🤯) Eccezionali per rapporto qualità - prezzo!
reviews.body: Acquistate il 31 ottobre 2023 (Halloween Shop🎃) esattamente 2 anni fa ho acquistato queste MAGNIFICHE cuffie! Vi posso assicurare che a distanza di 2 anni funzionano ancora a meraviglia! Da talmente sono piccole si possono indossare addirittura sotto le cuffie di protezione anti-rumore a lavoro😂 (non fatelo a lavoro, seriamente, state attenti! L'ho provato per testare la dimensione). Addirittura queste cuffie hanno battuto (in durata nel tempo) le Airpods 3ª Generazione (pagate ben 209€ e usate dal 03/01/2023) queste qua vi posso assicurare che in qualità sonora ovviamente battono le tozo.. ma in durata no!😂 Mi son durate fino maggio-giugno di quest'anno (è una vergogna assoluta che delle cuffie che costano 209€ durino di meno delle cuffie che costano 22,99€🤯). A distanza di 2 anni la batteria È IMBATTIBILE! Quando faccio palestra l'allenamento mi dura circa 2h e usando le cuffie 2h la batteria a fine allenamento è ancora a metà!!😍 Invece per la custodia di ricarica dura completamente 3-4 giorni (e ripeto, dopo 2 anni di uso quotidiano, visto che sono una persona che ascolta musica ogni santo giorno, funzionano ancora da dio, senza difetti né niente) anche di quella sono sbalordito! Funziona ancora meravigliosamente bene! Per me è uno dei miei prodotti migliori per qualità-prezzo in assoluto! Merita tantissimo questo prodotto!❤️

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 18, 2026
reviews.title: Great Sound, Affordable, and Surprisingly Comparable to AirPods
reviews.body: I bought these after losing my Apple AirPods and not wanting to spend that kind of money again—and I’m honestly so glad I took the chance on a cheaper option. After using these daily for a couple of weeks (commuting, walking, and at home), I’m seriously impressed with the quality. The sound is clear with good volume, and for the price, it feels very comparable to my old AirPods. They connect automatically to my phone every time, which makes them super convenient—no messing with settings or reconnecting. What I like: Great sound quality for the price Quick, automatic Bluetooth connection Comfortable fit (I can wear them for long periods without issues) Feels like a solid, reliable alternative to more expensive brands What could be better: Read more Read more

reviews.title: Amazing product
reviews.body: I was skeptical about the product but these air buds are great, the reviews are accurate. I would say the sound quality compared to Apple AirPods are a close 95%. The playing time between charges seem to last longer than the Apple AirPods. I’ve been using these airbuds for 3 days now averaging 8 hours a day. My charging case has yet needed to be charged and it’s showing 68% battery life left. The airbuds fit well and they are comfortable and light. GREAT GREAT product. The price is a steal

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 24, 2023
reviews.title: Amazing buds/price/service... buy these!
reviews.body: Yes, my subject line sounds like that trash hyperbole you get on annoying pop-up ads on your Smartphone! And ironically, it usually has to do with ear buds. There are tons of them on the market, and everyone claims to be the best. Now, first off, I am just a customer… never got any gifts or money or anything from this company, nor was asked to “click on a review” for a discount or whatever. No… the reason for all this hallelujah chorus, is that I’m really totally both jazzed, and a bit in awe of these earbuds. I think the main mind-blowing thing about them is the price. I mean, I have a bit of experience and knowledge with this kind of technology, and frankly, these days, what “guts” that are needed to be used to create Bluetooth ear buds is not that complex… nor expensive. How company’s like Sony, Apple, Bose, etc. can charge upwards of $300 for these boarders on theft and fraud! I’ve tried those “Rolls Royce” buds, and I urge you to do the same, and then try these Tagry Bluetooth Wireless Earbuds that are literally 90% less expensive. Yes, they retail for about $35! They come in an excellent quality charge base, are extremely easy to put on, (and have three extra size buds to give you a custom fit), and most important, their sound quality, and I mean crisp highs, solid middles, and bone- rumbling base, are outstanding. I cannot understand what more you would want either using them for talk radio, podcasts, YouTube, streaming movies, or TV, or playing your Amazon Music or Spotify or iTunes playlists. And yes, if connected to your iPhone, you can make and receive calls, and the microphone in the buds are top quality. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 13, 2026
reviews.title: Very good set of buds for good price
reviews.body: Overall very good product for a good price. Great sound and functionality. Volume is bit weird, as at 2nd lowest setting on my galaxy 23, it still sounds loud then I lower one level and i cant hear anymore but typically i only have one in at a time so not a deal breaker. Really good battery life, lasts all week at work listening at least 50% of the day each day. I have a pair of beats studio buds and it kept dropping connection at work. I got these to have at work to augment the beats and I have really liked them. I usually end up taking them home and using instead of the beats now. I just miss the beats function of noise canceling then going to transparency mode.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 14, 2023
reviews.title: Great value for both phone calls and music
reviews.body: Sometimes I'm amazed by the advancements in wireless and micro technology, especially when it comes to audio. These popped up in an email from Amazon a few days ago. Though I usually ignore those Deal notices this one intrigued me. I have a JBL Everest over-ear headset that I love for indoor phone calls and they're pretty good for music too (though I don't use them that way). I often like to go outside when I'm on the phone and the JBLs aren't convenient to use with a stocking cap, so I'd thought briefly about getting something in-ear to wear under my hat. These were $35 and the reviews were good. I liked that the case wirelessly charges the ear buds, and the case can be charged wirelessly too. My Samsung phone has wireless charging so I already have two wireless chargers and they're very convenient (especially given that the USB port on my phone was getting sketchy). So I figured I'd give them a try. I played a variety of my favorite music. I first asked for Jeff Buckley live music and the first song that started playing as I stepped outside was "Hallelujah." I walked around the property listening to my favorite music and before I knew it I had been listening to music for about three hours. I had really only cared about the sound quality for phone calls and whether they would stay in my ears (which is usually a problem for me). They never came close to falling out and they are comfortable. For phone calls the sound quality is very good on both ends. And though they're rated as having a 33' range I walked all around the house and got considerably farther than that from my phone with two walls separating the devices and I had no dropouts. Overall I am so happy with them that after a week of using them I ordered a backup pair. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 26, 2026
reviews.title: Great earbuds.
reviews.body: This is the first time I have had a set of these at all. I really like them and wish I had bought some a long time ago. I like this set but as I said I have nothing to compare them with. They are affordable and I have had no problems with them. Very clear sound and stay well in my ear. All in all I am very pleased with these.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 8, 2026
reviews.title: There is a Reason they are Rated so High
reviews.body: I have had the apple earbuds before and I actually like these better. They come with 3 different sized ear pieces., so you can adjust the size to match your ear canal. This is good for me because I have abnormally small ears. The actual discomfort I experienced from the apple earbuds literally makes the actual comfort of the item more important to me than the sound quality because the apple ones not only were painful, they would repeatedly fall out. Comfort aside, the sound is on par with what I got from the apple earbuds. They do seem well made for the price. I bought this specific set because a friend of mine already had them and spoke highly of them. They connect right away and stay connected, so they work exactly as advertised. There is a reason so many people have given these high marks. They are worth it.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 24, 2026
reviews.title: Good Wireless Earphones Option for Price
reviews.body: Works well compared to similar products. For around two years I was using a similar, different brand of wireless earphones also purchased off of Amazon, and they worked okay. I had originally switched over to wireless because my old headphone jack finally stopped working, unfortunately; now, of course, my new phone doesn't even have a headphone jack in the first place. I started buying these when I was no longer able to purchase from the previous brand off of Amazon. They were both around the same price and looked relatively similar. After a few months of using these, I can say that these are better than my previous brand, which I will refer as P.B. for short moving forward. As an avid music listener, these get the job done. For starters, they pair to my phone much faster (3 seconds max) as opposed to P.B. (7-12 seconds), they're also much better at keeping debris, junk, and other foreign materials out of the inner parts; I'd have to buy new earphones from P.B. every 3-5 months for this very reason. Perhaps my biggest compliment is the fact that the interactive "buttons" on the top sides of these earphones are far less sensitive than P.B. I'm personally not a huge fan of these buttons in the first place, but I can confidently say that I've accidentally pressed those sensors on these earphones relatively far less (maybe 2 or 3 times a week) then P.B. (at least 10 times a week). The case is also pretty durable; if I were to have dropped P.B.'s case with the earphones inside, there would have been a 100% chance that they would have gone flying; these, however, from the few times I've dropped the case, have stayed inside the case, undamaged. Battery life for both is about the same (about 4-5 hours but haven't really noticed a whole lot), as is/was audio quality. Overall a good product for the price; my only complaint is with the occasional, accidental, button press, pausing my music, which can't be disabled as far as a know. Frustrating, as this was a problem that I had with P.B. as well, but not a deal breaker. For casual music listing, this shouldn't be a problem. Originally had to return my first pair for issues with the hinge on the case breaking, but was sent a new product at no cost and haven't had any problems with the new one. 10/10 for price, 8/10 for audio quality, and 7.5/10 for battery life. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 9, 2026
reviews.title: Beyond impressed!
reviews.body: I am beyond impressed with these earbuds! From the moment I paired them, everything just worked seamlessly. The sound quality is incredible—crisp highs, rich mids, and surprisingly deep bass for such compact earbuds. Whether I’m listening to music, podcasts, or taking calls, the clarity is outstanding. The fit is also super comfortable. They stay in place perfectly, even when I’m on the go, and I can wear them for hours without any discomfort. The battery life is a huge win too—I love that I can go days without worrying about charging, and the case is sleek and easy to carry. Another thing I really appreciate is how easy they are to use. The connection is quick and reliable, and the controls are intuitive. Plus, they look great—simple, modern, and not bulky at all. Honestly, these earbuds exceeded my expectations in every way. Amazing quality at a great value. I would absolutely recommend them to anyone looking for reliable, great-sounding wireless earbuds!

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 25, 2026
reviews.title: Good, inexpensive
reviews.body: Bought these to replace my last pair that I accidentally dropped in my oven. JLab is reliably inexpensive and reliable. I wear them in a hot sauna and they always work. Sounds quality is good enough. Transparency mode is not good.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 15, 2026
reviews.title: Quality and affordability
reviews.body: I purchased these earbuds in November of 2025 and they still work and sound like new. I’ve purchased more expensive earbuds in the past, and the price of these earbuds are well worth it. I’ve never had a problem with them connecting to my phone. They fit perfectly in my ear and the sound quality is on point. I didn’t know what to expect from these at this price point, but I’m glad that I gave them shot. I would definitely recommend them for anyone looking to same money, but also want to have quality sound and reliability.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 24, 2025
reviews.title: After 6 months, I'd say it's a good choice
reviews.body: Good wireless earbuds for the price! Would probably buy again if need be, or order for a family member. ⟢ Pretty straightforward to use, even without the app (which was a concern of mine)! I'm sure the app would add to my experience in some way, but I haven't really used it, so it isn't accounted for in this review. ⟢ Very compact! Fits in the average patch pocket on a denim jacket or jeans or in a small cross-body bag. ⟢ I'm pretty pleased with the sound. Nothing much to say. I don't really use the other modes (bass boosted and balanced), but JLab Signature has 2 channel audio and sounds clear enough. I'm debating whether or not I hear a faint buzzing or if I'm overthinking it when I use it on my laptop, but it's overall isn't a big problem for me. ⟢ They seem durable enough. They've definitely stumbled out of my bag several times, gotten through some sweat, and I lost one of them on the sidewalk and in someone else's lawn. I take a ceramics class and my hands are often covered in clay dust, and my apron pockets tend to collect some of it too, so these usually end up with at least some dust on them even when I try to be careful. Each of these times I've cleaned it with 70% isopropyl alcohol wipes and small brush, and they haven't lost sound quality as far as I notice. I do wish there were a carrying case available for it, but that is something else. Read more Read more

reviews.title: Great but with a catch
reviews.body: Pros: really good sound quality, bass boost mode pretty awesome, ambience mode dope, battery life, the price, doesn’t fall off the ear. cons: you have to really read the manual to know how to use and switch modes from the headphones by tapping, connecting via bluetooth can be a challenge but not a tough challenge, the charging port could be better. But overall, really great earbubs! Highly recommend.

reviews.title: Great Quality & Price
reviews.body: Excellent headset! It pairs effortlessly with my phone, and I really like the charging case with the built-in cord. The sound quality is great and perfect for my workouts.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 27, 2026
reviews.title: Love these
reviews.body: I'm only on day 3 of owning these, but so far I am blown away that these are only ~$20 The fit and comfort is excellent, the touch controls work perfect and are intuitive, the app appears up to date and works great (where you can adjust the EQ to you desire, change pass thru volume, etc) and sound is great. I really don't know what a more expensive set of buds would offer. Maybe ANC?...which I personally don't want because it gives me a headache I'm really reaching here, but these are the only two things I could find that could be better: 1. When turning the pass thru audio on and off, it interrupts whatever you are listening to and has an audible voice that tells you it's turning it off/on. Unnecessary and kind of annoying when listening to books/podcasts. Just turn it on/off, no voice notification needed 2. The case has a really short, built in usb-c cable (see pic) - why not just have a usb-c port where I can plug it in instead of this goofy thing? Expect an update in 6-12 months

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 31, 2026
reviews.title: Meh
reviews.body: These ear buds are okay. Not great, not terrible, just okay. But that’s to be expected from a 20 dollar set of ear buds. I originally bought them for work so I could take calls if I needed to while I was driving, but due to the lack of single bud controls (I can’t use two at the same time) I just ended up giving them to my son to use for his tablet. I ended up settling with a pair of Beats Studio buds+, which are a bit more expensive but well worth the price. Overall, the Go Air Pop+ earbuds are pretty much what you would expect for twenty bucks. The sound quality isn't bad, the case feels a bit flimsy but it’s held up fine so far, they look decent but feel cheap (because they are), they fit pretty well but after a while they tend to try and swan dive out of my ear.

reviews.date: Reviewed in Italy on May 30, 2025
reviews.title: Ottime cuffie in-ear economiche
reviews.body: La qualità dell'audio è molto buona per il prezzo, come da aspettativa per JLab. Packaging spartano, ma contenente due dimensioni alternative (oltre a quelli già installati) di gommini per adattarsi a varie dimensioni di condotti uditivi. L'autonomia è buona, non eccezionale, mi durano circa tre/quattro ore in videochiamata. La custodia esterna consente diverse ricariche complete. Le tempistiche di ricarica degli auricolari nella base sono accettabili, un po' meno quelle della custodia. Molto comodo il cavo USB-C integrato, che consente una ricaricara comoda anche con un telefono o un power bank dotato di tale uscita. La funzione Be Aware è utile e molto efficace per rumori vicini. Personalmente mi è stato possibile condurre una breve conversazione senza rimuovere le cuffie, ma è meno efficace con rumori distanti o quando si riproducono contenuti a volume alto o medio-alto. Comoda l'applicazione, di cui utilizzo principalmente la limitazione del volume a 80 dB. Mi ritengo generalmente molto soddisfatto dell'acquisto.

reviews.date: Reviewed in Belgium on November 12, 2025
reviews.title: Vraiment top
reviews.body: Qualité au top Durée d'écoute très longue Batterie top J'avais le modèle en dessous Que j'ai garder très longtemps et ceux ci sont mieux.. qualité prix parfaite

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United Kingdom on March 5, 2026
reviews.title: Tiny Earbuds But Surprisingly Big Performance
reviews.body: Ordered the JLab Go Air Pop+ earbuds because I wanted something simple, portable, and reliable for everyday listening or travel. After using them for a while, I have to say they perform better than I expected for their size. The first thing I noticed is how compact the case is. It is small enough to fit easily in a pocket, which makes it great for travel or just carrying around during the day. The case uses USB C charging, which is a nice upgrade since most of my devices already use the same cable. No hunting around for some outdated charger. Ear support is good. ✅ Battery life is one of the standout features. With up to 35 hours of playtime between the earbuds and the charging case, they last a long time before needing to recharge. I have used them for several listening sessions throughout the week and still had plenty of battery left in the case. For anyone who hates constantly charging devices, this is a big plus. ✅ Pairing them with Bluetooth was quick and easy. Once connected the first time, they automatically reconnect when you take them out of the case. They also support Dual Connect, which means you can use either earbud on its own if you want. That is handy if you are taking calls or just want one ear free while walking. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in France on August 9, 2025
reviews.title: Great value, reliable everyday earbuds
reviews.body: I’ve been using the JLab Go Air Pop+ for over a month now, and for the price, they’re incredibly reliable. The sound quality is good—not on the same level as my Bose headphones, of course—but perfectly fine for casual music listening, phone calls, and especially audiobooks. The earbuds are small and lightweight to the point that I sometimes forget I’m wearing them. I’ve even fallen asleep with them on and woke up without any ear discomfort. The companion app is a nice bonus, allowing you to customize tap controls and tweak EQ settings. Battery life is impressive: after 3 hours of audiobook listening, I still had over 40% left, and the case charges them quickly. The integrated charging cable on the case is a thoughtful touch—it saves you from hunting for a cable. If I had to nitpick, the case feels quite light; a little extra heft would make it feel more premium. But given the price point, that’s hardly a dealbreaker. Overall, an excellent 5-star buy for anyone looking for affordable, comfortable, and functional earbuds.

reviews.date: Reviewed in Germany on February 20, 2026
reviews.title: Quality
reviews.body: These ear headphones are excellent for everyday use. The sound is clear and well-balanced, with deep bass and crisp highs. They fit comfortably in the ears and stay in place even during workouts. The cord is durable and tangle-free, which is a big plus. I also appreciate the built-in microphone for calls—it works really well. Overall, great value for the price, and I would definitely recommend them.

I’ve used them for everything from cable management in my office to securing heavier gear in the garage, and not one has snapped on me. The UV resistance is a huge plus for outdoor projects, and having the assorted sizes (4", 6", 8", and 12") means I always have the right length on hand. Definitely high-quality and a great value.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 27, 2026
reviews.title: Black Cable Ties for Reliable, Everyday Organization
reviews.body: When it comes to keeping your cables, wires, cords, and everyday clutter in perfect order, sometimes you don’t need magic—just solid, dependable performance. That’s exactly what the ANOSON 400-Pack Assorted Zip Ties deliver: no frills, no disappointments, just reliable, consistent functionality that earns a well-deserved 4 out of 5 stars in real-world use. These zip ties come in a thoughtfully assorted bundle of four essential sizes—4-inch, 6-inch, 8-inch, and 12-inch—giving you the flexibility to tackle a wide range of tasks, from bundling delicate electronics cables behind your entertainment center to securing heavier-duty outdoor wiring or gardening projects. With 400 pieces total, you'll have more than enough for home, office, workshop, or even outdoor maintenance jobs. Crafted from high-quality nylon, each tie is durable, resistant to abrasion, and built to withstand tension—locking securely with a satisfying click. More importantly, these are UV-resistant, meaning they won’t degrade, crack, or become brittle under prolonged sun exposure. This makes them ideal for both indoor and outdoor applications—whether you’re organizing holiday lights on the porch, bundling extension cords in the garage, or tidying up router and modem cables in your home office. Use two-way talk to hear and speak through the Blink app. | Set up in minutes — Easily install wire-free or connect Video Doorbell to existing doorbell wiring. reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 2, 2026
reviews.title: Dependable, Clear, and Easy Security — A Smart Doorbell That Just Works
reviews.body: If you’re looking for a straightforward, reliable smart video doorbell that delivers quality performance without fuss, the Blink Video Doorbell (newest model) is a fantastic choice. I’ve been using this doorbell for a while now, and it consistently impresses with clear head‑to‑toe video, long battery life, and simple setup—everything you want in a smart home security upgrade. 📹 Excellent Head‑to‑Toe HD Video One of the first things I noticed was the 1080p HD video quality. The head‑to‑toe view really makes a difference—whether someone is standing close or farther away, you get a full picture from top to bottom. I can clearly see packages on the step, visitors’ faces, and even pets wandering by. The night vision also performs very well; it captures clear and usable footage after dark, which is reassuring for overnight monitoring. 🔋 Two‑Year Battery Life Really Delivers The promised two‑year battery life is not just marketing — this doorbell really goes a long time between battery changes. I installed it months ago and haven’t had to recharge or replace the batteries yet. That long runtime is a huge comfort compared to many other wireless doorbells that need attention every few weeks. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 11, 2026
reviews.title: Finally, a sense of security in a busy NYC building!
reviews.body: Living in a 100-year-old building in the Bronx, security is my #1 priority. This Blink Doorbell is reliable and has been a total game-changer for my peace of mind. Pros: • Easy Install: I set this up myself without a handyman. • The View: The head-to-toe view is incredible. I can see packages on the floor and the faces of people clearly, even in my narrow hallway. • Alexa Integration: It connects perfectly with my Echo Show 8. I just say "Alexa, show me the front door" and I see everything instantly. • Motion Alerts: It’s very fast. I get a notification on my phone the second someone steps near my door. Battery Life: I am shocked at how long the batteries last. I don't have to worry about charging it every week. • Night Vision: Even when it’s pitch black outside, the infrared night vision is crisp. I can see exactly who is moving around. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 9, 2026
reviews.title: No fuzzy view very clear and reliable!
reviews.body: Great view super easy to set up and once we got it on it was easy to watch what’s going on around my place with the motion detection feature we leave it armed almost at all times and it’s great at updating whenever anything is near our place!

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 14, 2026
reviews.title: Good devices when you have adequate wifi
reviews.body: I purchased 2 of these added them to my blink system.. they work well I dont like the curved appearance of everything in them however... I cant complain it captures almost everything. These cameras work on 2.4ghz wifi. Which is good and bad. Lots of co interferance devices... I live far enough from most possible issues it wont be a problem. I created a second SSID on my wireless for these and other IOT devixes. So they do not interfere with speeds of my more capable devices.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 17, 2026
reviews.title: Decent but could be better
reviews.body: Rating: 7/10 👍 Excellent value and battery life 👍 Easy setup and Alexa integration 👎 Weak video/audio quality and lag 👎 Limited features without add-ons 👉 Best for: budget buyers and basic home monitoring 👉 Not ideal for: high-security needs or premium smart home setups 📦 Key Features Video & Viewing 1080p HD video (newer version: up to 1440p square view) � Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 8, 2026
reviews.title: High Quality Video and Audio
reviews.body: The Blink Doorbell is quick and easy to set up. First, scan the QR code inside the Doorbell with your phone before physically setting it up. This will link it with your system, making theft of the Doorbell useless. Once activated, it will only work with your account-specific system. Placement: Optimal Height is generally 40–48 inches from the ground. This is the typical height of a doorbell. You can buy an optional no-drill Blink Vinyl Siding Clip on Etsy to avoid putting holes in your siding. The clip slides under and between the siding. Intercom: to speak to someone through the Blink Outdoor 2K camera, open the “live view” in the phone app and then tap the microphone icon (unmute), at the screen bottom. The mic icon will turn green, allowing you to speak through your phone. Tap the mic icon again to mute, allowing the person at the Doorbell to speak. The audio on the Blink Doorbell operates like a walkie-talkie; only one person can speak at a time. There may be a 1-2 second delay. Night Vision: works fine, showing a clear view of the immediate surroundings. Also check that your phone’s Blink app has permission to use the microphone. Go to the phone settings > apps > blink > permissions > microphone > and select allowed. That’s all there is to it. This is an easy to set up, effective security device. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 7, 2026
reviews.title: Very good.
reviews.body: I ordered the white model because my trim and shutters are white. It took a few weeks to arrive but it was worth it. It looks really attractive on my house. I bought this to replace a Ring doorbell as the subscription price seems to go up every year for not impressive video quality. Not only is the video on the Blink doorbell much sharper but it’s a wider angle that takes in my entire front yard - granted it’s a small urban front yard - and most of the front porch as well. Setting it up in the Blink app was simple as I already have some Blink cameras on the property. I set the ring setting in the Alexa app and it’s integrating flawlessly. Glad I made the switch. The camera looks nicer, the video quality is much better and if they ever take away our grandfathered status I can still do local storage in the sync module.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2026
reviews.title: Clear images, local storage, with minor issues
reviews.body: We had been using a different brand video doorbell, but decided we didn't want to continue to pay an annual fee and have our videos stored in the cloud. This doorbell allows us to store up to 256gb of video in a flash drive in the sync module. It works well, but there are a few issues with it. It advertised that it can be hard wired which is true. However, that wiring only connects it to our physical doorbell ringer, allowing it to ring when the button is pushed. It still runs on batteries that need to be replaced eventually. Our last doorbell was hard wired with no batteries to worry about. There's also no way to set it up to work with a chime when it detects motion. It can use a Blink Mini camera as a chime, but that, or any other 3rd party chime, only work when the button is pushed. The only alert for motion is from the app. Our last doorbell had a chime that alerted us any time there was motion. When it does detect motion and we click on the notification to open the app, it shows a blank screen saying a recording is in progress. We either have to wait until that recording is done to see what the motion was, or click open live view to see it in real time. It should automatically show what's being recorded when the app is opened. Read more Read more

name: Soundcore by Anker Q20i Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling Headphones, Wireless Over-Ear Bluetooth, 40H Long ANC Playtime, Hi-Res Audio, Big Bass, Customize via an App, Transparency Mode (Black)
description: Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling: 2 internal and 2 external mics work in tandem to detect external noise and effectively reduce up to 90% of it, no matter in trains or offices. | Immerse Yourself in Detailed Audio: The noise cancelling headphones have oversized 40mm dynamic drivers that produce detailed sound and thumping beats with BassUp technology for your every travel, commuting and gaming. Compatible with Hi-Res certified audio via the AUX cable for more detail. | 40-Hour Long Battery Life and Fast Charging: With 40 hours of battery life with ANC on and 60 hours in normal mode, you can commute in peace with your Bluetooth headphones without thinking about recharging. Fast charge for 5 mins to get an extra 4 hours of music listening for daily users. | Dual-Connections: Connect to two devices simultaneously with Bluetooth 5.0 and instantly switch between them. Whether you're working on your laptop, or need to take a phone call, audio from your Bluetooth headphones will automatically play from the device you need to hear from. | App for EQ Customization: Download the soundcore app to tailor your sound using the customizable EQ, with 22 presets, or adjust it yourself. You can also switch between 3 modes: ANC, Normal, and Transparency, and relax with white noise. | Hear Your Surroundings: Switch to Transparency mode on your noise cancelling headphones when you need to be aware of your surroundings, such as transportation announcements, crossing the road, or just staying connected to the world around you.
highlights: Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling: 2 internal and 2 external mics work in tandem to detect external noise and effectively reduce up to 90% of it, no matter in trains or offices.

Immerse Yourself in Detailed Audio: The noise cancelling headphones have oversized 40mm dynamic drivers that produce detailed sound and thumping beats with BassUp technology for your every travel, commuting and gaming. Compatible with Hi-Res certified audio via the AUX cable for more detail.

40-Hour Long Battery Life and Fast Charging: With 40 hours of battery life with ANC on and 60 hours in normal mode, you can commute in peace with your Bluetooth headphones without thinking about recharging. Fast charge for 5 mins to get an extra 4 hours of music listening for daily users.

Dual-Connections: Connect to two devices simultaneously with Bluetooth 5.0 and instantly switch between them. Whether you're working on your laptop, or need to take a phone call, audio from your Bluetooth headphones will automatically play from the device you need to hear from.

App for EQ Customization: Download the soundcore app to tailor your sound using the customizable EQ, with 22 presets, or adjust it yourself. You can also switch between 3 modes: ANC, Normal, and Transparency, and relax with white noise.

Hear Your Surroundings: Switch to Transparency mode on your noise cancelling headphones when you need to be aware of your surroundings, such as transportation announcements, crossing the road, or just staying connected to the world around you.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 20, 2026
reviews.title: Best headphones ever! You won’t regret these bad boys!
reviews.body: These Sound ore by Anker Q20i headphones are absolutely amazing. I am an earbud girlie all the way, but let’s be real, the noise cancelling effect is only in play when you’re playing music on your ears buds. In the other hand, these headphones, as soon as they just connect to any of my devices, provide the noise cancelling effect I was looking for! I wanted noise cancelling headphones to block out the noise of home - I have 3 kids that always have some sort of device and my fiancé who ALWAYS has the tv on and loud. I love that I can pop these on if if I’m not playing anything through them at the moment. - I also love the fact that they can be connected/paired to multiple devices. They are currently paired to my phone, iPad, and switch lite. I use these a lot to play music through my phone or while gaming on my switch lite. BONUS- you can simultaneously have these connected to 2 devices at once. Using them while gaming but also connected to my phone so I won’t miss an important phone call. The headphones do have specific sides R/L that are marked accordingly. If you out them on backwards, they still work BUT if you are trying to use the mic, it will drastically impact the ability for the other person to hear you- so just make sure you put them on the right way. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 14, 2026
reviews.title: Great sound and effective noise blocking
reviews.body: These Soundcore Q20i headphones work really well and do a great job blocking out noise. The active noise cancelling makes a noticeable difference, especially in busy or noisy environments. The sound quality is solid with good bass and clear audio, making them great for music, videos, or just everyday listening. They’re also comfortable to wear for longer periods, which is a big plus. Battery life has been impressive, lasting a long time between charges, and the Bluetooth connection has been reliable. Overall, these are a great set of headphones that deliver both good sound and effective noise cancelling. Suggestions for improvement: Improve ANC performance for higher-pitched noises Add more padding options for an even better fit Include a carrying case for travel protection

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 11, 2026
reviews.title: The best money can buy for an affordable price
reviews.body: 100% recommend these headphones to anyone wanting amazing sound with great comfort involved. It gives you the option to use a cord, or go full bluetooth, not too bulky but still has a nostalgic feeling with the ear padding. The headset itself feels quite sturdy. Also, Soundcore has an app you can download to your phone that gives you some Equalizer options for more sound variables, and even comes with the option of having noise cancelling on or off, incase you want to still be aware of your surroundings if you are out on a walk with them on. It has the perfect use for those who... say, you use chrome tv or a tv firestick or something with your tv that uses bluetooth. Perhaps you want to watch something loud at night, but don't want to wake people up in the house due to the volume.. well, these give you that option. Kind of wish there were more color options, but traditional black is always a perfect choice. Honestly, if you are looking for headphones that will have an amazing life in 1 full charge, with perfect sound quality, versatile in its use, and won't break the bank even at full price, this is the headset for you.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 3, 2026
reviews.title: Good headphones for the price, some minor cons.
reviews.body: TLDR: good quality and sound for price, minor inconveniences in setup. ANC mode works well. These cause my ears to get very hot quickly, so if you have sensory issues this might be a dealbreaker. These headphones have good sound, especially for the relatively low price. The app is not user friendly, but not really required to use the item. The extremely tiny "user manual" included in the box is impossible to read if you are over 40, so you will need to scan the QR code to get directions online. All that being said, they are pretty easy to pair to IPhone. Also the noise cancellation is superb! My husband was sitting right next to me talking and I couldn't hear him at all with the headphones on in ANC mode. Battery seems good, I don't know for sure if these will auto shut off, but after a day or so of non-use you will need to re-connect in Bluetooth settings. Have used near water, so I can't address water resistance, but I bought them for an international flight soon, time will tell about durability as it's only been about a week so far.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 27, 2026
reviews.title: Good quality at a great price
reviews.body: These are very comfortable and the sound is great. I use them while I am working in my flower beds. They feel very lightweight, with a good fit. They stay charged for a long time. Hopefully they hold up well. The price was great for the quality of these headphones.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 20, 2026
reviews.title: Great quality earphones for the price!
reviews.body: I got these on sale and figured for the price I would try them out for my kids. They were complaining that they couldn't hear the protective style over road noise and I tried those out and I also couldn't hear well. I warned them that these could get loud, but with the noise cancelling tech, they don't have to turn the volume up high. You immediately feel the noise cancelling when you turn them on. It almost feels like a pressure change and then the world goes quiet. My kids can now each listen to their tablets and don't complain about difficulty hearing when my friend and I chat in the front seat. It's a nice break for me and a nice break for them. These are extremely soft and comfortable. They're technically too big for my 4 yr old's head, but she works with them anyway because of how much better the sound is. My 6 yr old does great with them. They pair very easily. I got a set in pink and navy blue. The blue is nearly black, but again, the quality is so good my youngest doesn't care. I wanted to get them both pink, but I knew we'd have difficulty matching them to the correct tablets. This way we have pink with the pink tablet and blue with the purple tablet. It'd be cool if they made a nice purple color. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 6, 2026
reviews.title: the under dogs
reviews.body: where to start.... first off, i write this under having experience with allot of speakers and headphones, i am what i guess one could refer to as an " audiophile ". i have used beats, Bose, skull candy, sony and many more. Anker is in my mind what id call the under dogs in audio, they are not a very well known brand, but boy are they good. i currently only use Anker soundcore and skull candy. about these head phones, this is my 3rd pair, i had 2 x in ear, the first was so good i bought a back up pair as use them with my mobile phone, my toddler threw my first pair in my coffee and broke them. but these come with great sound, mid and highs very clear and very well balanced, they have the ability to connect up to multiple devices and flip between the 2 with a push of a button. the transparency mode is awesome a very nice caveat to have, specially when using them in office environment, which these were purchased for, they switch between noise isolation and transparency mode with a push of a button. the mobile app opens up a whole new world, with 20 studio grade sound options as well as programmable buttons and features for the headphones. i can honestly say that these soundcore by Anker as a company are great value for money with no disappointments and can quiet easily compete with any other reputable and well know brands, be it with regards to audio quality or battery life, these wont disappoint ! The quality is great and the customer service is even better. My first pair had an issue with an odd, unexplainable, sound that would come out of nowhere. It would hurt my ears and scare me because it was unexpected. I contacted the company directly through their website. I explained the issue and offered to send them back for testing. They agreed and sent me return postage. I sent them back and then they sent me a new pair. Product Review These headphones are fantastic! I purchased a pair for the noise cancellation feature and the ability to connect to 2 devices at the same time. Connecting to multiple devices, an switching back and forth, was seamless. ANC I connect to my work laptop and my phone at the same time and the switch over is seamless. The noise cancellation is good. You can still hear a little but I’d say these block 95% of sound. So here's my comparison against a pretty premium pair of headphones- my wife also has Bose QC35's and I've used them plenty too. Pros: - Sound Isolation is amazing, it has a very good seal over the ears (I have a big head and ears too) - Very comfortable, and suprisingly light. Ear cups feel memory foamish - noise cancelling is pretty great, and it doesn't cause discomfort like some cheap NC Read more Read more

Ich habe nach einer preiswerten Alternative zu den teuren High-End-Modellen gesucht und mit dem Q20i einen Volltreffer gelandet. Meine Highlights: • Ruhe auf Knopfdruck (ANC): Die Geräuschunterdrückung ist für diese Preisklasse phänomenal. Das monotone Brummen in der Bahn oder der Lärm im Büro werden fast komplett geschluckt. reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 13, 2026
reviews.title: Great Sound and Battery Life — Make Sure You Get the Right Head Size
reviews.body: ⭐ Rating: 5/5 These are amazing overall, but sizing is something you definitely want to pay attention to before ordering. 🔧 What I used it for: Daily use for music, calls, and general listening around the house and while working. ✅ Pros: Very comfortable once you get the correct size Excellent battery life for how compact they are Bluetooth range is strong — works across the house without dropouts Good sound quality for everyday listening Lightweight and easy to wear for long periods Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 20, 2025
reviews.title: I can’t believe I haven’t already left a review! Love them!
reviews.body: You alone will know why an open ear headset might be something you’d want. So let me just focus on the function of these. Battery life is epic. I mean all day. I use them all day a lot of the time. Comfort is excellent. As I said, sometimes I wear them all day. I barely know they are there. In fact, sometimes I forget they are there. sizing is something we need to talk about. I am 6 feet tall and have a normal sized head I suppose and the mini ones fit me perfectly. I also have short hair so keep that in mind. The reason this is important is because the standard ones might give me a little more freedom without getting tight. I really don’t care about that because the minis allow me to sit in a chair without being uncomfortable when I put my head against the pillow. They can also be used in bed. In fact, sometimes I sleep all night long with these on, and they are still there when I wake up. If you can wear the mini and have a little bit of distance between your neck and head and you don’t have to look up a lot, get the mini! Otherwise get the standard ones and be aware that you probably won’t be able to put the back of your head against anything without removing them. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 25, 2025
reviews.title: Great general purpose headset - good for comfort, audio and battery life
reviews.body: This is the one to get, for general purpose Bluetooth headsets. The combination of bone conduction and speakers in front of your ears, not pointed into them, works great. My previous one was a single ear, in-ear type, which became annoying and uncomfortable. This one is comfortable and doesn't get in the way of environmental sound to my ear. The voice on the phone just appears in my head. It's great being able to work with my hands while I talk on the phone or listen to music, podcasts, etc. Music - I'm a sound engineer, and this is fine for casual music listening. You're not going to get deep bass or audiophile detail in music, and it's no substitute for good closed-back headphones, but it's mostly balanced and good enough. I use the Standard setting in the app. The Volume Boost setting doesn't seem to do much at all. The Classic setting, which is bone-conduction only and takes more battery power, doesn't sound as good. I tried other EQ settings, including the 5 band custom one, and I usually stay with Standard. If you need to use the bass or treble boost settings, there's probably something wrong with the source audio. Volume - music volume maxes out just a little before I wish it did. It's not super loud, but it's plenty in a quiet environment. Using it on a bicycle or with significant wind, you'd probably want to put a headband over it, and even then, it may not be loud enough. For podcasts or phone calls, volume is better; no issues with it not being loud enough yet. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 15, 2026
reviews.title: Simply Marvelous
reviews.body: Super happy with these. A bit on the pricey side, but totally worth it in the long run. I work 8 hour shifts often listening to music the whole time and on medium volume I can happily report the 14+hr battery to be accurate. Charges quick and pairs even quicker. Multi device pairing is a breeze too, only being made easier by the app where you can toggle on and off devices that the headphones will try to pair to. So, basically you can pair a bunch of devices and turn the ability to connect to them on and off. For me, I usually pair to my laptop and phone, but have my watch and streaming stick saved and toggled off so the headphones prioritize my phone and laptop so they don't fight for pairing power. I had the minus volume button deteriorate and come off rendering the rocker almost useless. I reached out to support and they sent me a new pair free of charge since it was still under warranty. Really nice and easy and now I have two pairs, just one I can't turn the volume down on nor rely on its water resistance. I'd consider myself an average sized dude 5'10" @160lbs and the "mini" size fits perfect and I barely know it's on me until I get a notification and hear it IN STEREO! Stereo audio is also a big plus for me and one of the main downsides to using just one in-ear earbud. Plus I get normal surround sound of the world around me in addition to stereo music. I think it's just one of the best daily-wear inventions yet. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 11, 2026
reviews.title: Great headphones!
reviews.body: I primarily use earbuds but I also fish and enjoy hearing nature. Got these to bridge the gap so that I can Kayak and make/receive calls while doing it without blocking out the outside world. The app didn't initially want to play nice, now that I have it working I love the EQ settings. Well worth downloading and changing to "standard" as the default on mine were set to "vocal." The audio quality is surprisingly great when stacked up against traditional buds/headphones. Call quality is pretty good, although it can be difficult to hear the caller if in a noisy environment. Why 4 stars? I guess I'm a spoiled techbaby. There is no battery life indicator in the app, in 2026, with the latest and greatest in bluetooth technology. The app functionality is dubious as of now. I'll see how it holds on.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 15, 2026
reviews.title: Excellent product. Bone conduction has great sound.
reviews.body: A great product. Bone conduction worked very nice... on my right side. However, I seem to have cochlear, or nerve-related damage on my left side, so I can't get sound on my left side. I tested the headphone left side by turning them around, and they work fine. They are truly as described. If you have outer or middle ear issues, these will work very well. Sound was fully adjustable with with the Shokz app. Connected very fast and easy. I bought the "mini" version, and they fit nice. Very light weight. The carrying case holds the headphones very well, also. It would fit nicely in a gym bag. Charging cord is included, but NOT the AC adapter.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 19, 2026
reviews.title: Throw Away All Your Old Headsets - THIS IS THE ONE YOU WANT!
reviews.body: The first thing I need to say is just get it!! This is such an awesome product and it is worth the price. The quality and functionality are superb, plus I love the simplicity because it's so easy to operate with the multi-function button. The sound is outstanding and the app gives you the capability to increase the base, the treble and overall volume among other things. These bone conduction headphones are the only way to go because your ears are left uncovered so you can hear other things going on around you, but at the same time doesn't really detract from the music you're listening to or even a phone conversation. Speaking of phone conversations, the built-in microphone is excellent and your phone calls are clear and crisp without any outside interference, whether you're talking or listening. This feature is just as good or better than the overall sound quality when music is playing. I was especially drawn to this device because I have an issue with the anatomy of my ears. For whatever reason I could never get earbuds to stay in my ears. Much as I tried they would always fall out which was extremely frustrating. Finally my doctor told me I fell into about 10% of the population that has this same problem, so the only solution for me was the over ear style. One last thing I need to mention is the sizing. Specifically the standard versus the mini. I'm a male, 5'11" and weigh 180lbs with basically a normal head and short hair. Based on some other reviews I got the mini and I'm glad I did. It's a perfect fit without too much play in the back of my head. I can easily move my head up, down and all around and the headphones stay in place with a great deal of comfort. Therefore, I would only recommend the standard size to someone with a larger head or someone with a lot of hair in the back of their head because you need the extra space provided by the standard size. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 14, 2026
reviews.title: Good quality, lightweight.
reviews.body: I bought these because I wanted something I could leave on my head through my entire workout the gym. I have a pair of Bose noise cancelling headphones, which I love. They were $300+ and I accidentally left them at the gym twice because, once I took them off while on the decline bench because they are heavy and will fall off and the other time I forgot them when I took them off on the treadmill, because I was sweating. Thankfully someone turned them. I bought my husband the regular SHOKZ Open Run headphones as a gift and he likes them. He was using individual earbuds and went through at least 3 pairs in the past 5 years and he couldn’t always hear me when I was trying to talk to him. I like these headphones because they are lightweight, they stay on through ALL of my workouts and the sound quality is amazing, just like my Bose headphones. I also like that the are smaller than my Bose headphones and don’t take up space in my backpack. I have not used the phone mic yet. Battery life is good too. When I turn the headphones on, it will state “battery low, medium or high”. I like this feature but my Bose headphones will state the actual percentage of battery life left. I can check the percentage of battery life left in the SHOKZ app though. It took awhile to get used to not having the noise canceling feature, but I don’t mind it now. I bought the mini size because I went to Best Buy and tried the two different sizes on. Only suggestion I would like to make is that SHOKZ will make an adjustable headband to fit the head better. The mini is still too big and I use my hair jaw clip to clip the back of the headband into it, to bring it up higher, so that the earbuds will sit better, improving the amplitude quality. If I don’t do this, I just keep the volume up higher. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 7, 2026
reviews.title: Finally, My Cables Have Pants
reviews.body: I bought this with low expectations and higher cable shame. What I got was the ability to look at my desk without wincing. This thing instantly transforms a chaotic spaghetti monster of cords into a single, respectable, well‑behaved cable snake. It’s like telling your wires to “get it together” and they actually listen. Power cords, HDMI, Ethernet — all quietly bundled and no longer plotting against my sanity. The split design is genius. Open it up, shove wires in (technical term), and boom — organization. No tools, no instructions, no life coaching required. It even cuts cleanly, which made me feel oddly accomplished for someone who just wrapped cables in plastic. It’s flexible, durable, and sturdy enough to protect against pets, chairs, and your own feet when you forget where the cords are (which you will). Bonus: it instantly makes any setup look more professional, even if what’s plugged in is absolutely not. Highly recommend if you want fewer visible cords, less chaos, and wires that finally behave like adults.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 11, 2026
reviews.title: Clean, Effective Cable Management
reviews.body: I bought this 3/4" x 10 ft loom to improve cable management behind my desk, and it worked exactly as intended. Before installing it, I had multiple loose cables running behind my PC and monitor setup. Once the loom was in place, the wiring was much more consolidated and the overall setup looked significantly cleaner. I’m including before-and-after photos with this review, and the improvement is very noticeable. The loom was easy to install, accommodated several cables without issue, and provided a cleaner routing solution without adding much bulk. It gave the cable run a more organized, controlled appearance and made the area look far more refined. For the price, it was a worthwhile purchase and I’d buy it again.
