reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 4, 2025
reviews.title: Perfect protection for oxygen tubes
reviews.body: My mom is currently on oxygen 24/7. She isn't quite capable of managing all the maintenance & accessories by herself, so I take care of it all. I had two serious & pressing issues in need of a quick solution. I'm highly allergic to the dye in the mainline oxygen tubing that our local supplier provides. So, I couldn't touch it without covering my hands or the tubing. The alternative, clear tubing our supplier provides is unbelievably fragile & subpar to say the least. I attempted to create a homemade cover using old damaged tubing, shrink wrap, & tape while I searched for a better solution. As you can imagine, my makeshift cover wasn't ideal & certainly not sustainable. I stumbled across several types of PET covers & tried quite a few. I liked the expendable covers at first. I thought they'd be the better option, assuming they were lighter weight than the others. However, I soon realized that it was entirely too difficult & time consuming to snake 50+ feet of tubing through the cover on a weekly basis. Not to mention, they aren't very flexible & that's kind of a necessity when dealing with O² tubing. I tried these & immediately decided they were the best invention ever! I have nerve damage in one hand which makes it tough to deal with things that require more than minimal dexterity. The split sleeve made applying this cover SO easy. The material these are made from are a visibly higher quality than the others I've used while still managing to be lightweight & flexible. Read more Read more

reviews.body: Der Alex Tech 13mm-3.1m selbstschließende Kabelschlauch ist eine hervorragende Wahl für alle, die ihre Kabel ordentlich und geschützt halten möchten. Hier sind die Gründe, warum ich dieses Produkt besonders empfehle: Effektiver Kabelschutz: Der Kabelschlauch bietet einen ausgezeichneten Schutz für meine Kabel, sei es für TV, Computer, Heimkino oder Auto. Die robuste gewebte Struktur schützt die Kabel vor Abnutzung, Abrieb und möglichen Beschädigungen, was besonders wichtig ist, um die Lebensdauer der Kabel zu verlängern. Selbstschließend und einfach zu verwenden: Der selbstschließende Mechanismus macht die Handhabung des Schlauchs sehr unkompliziert. Man muss ihn einfach über die Kabel ziehen, und er schließt sich von selbst. Dies spart Zeit und Aufwand bei der Installation und sorgt dafür, dass die Kabel sicher und ordentlich im Schlauch verstaut sind. Einfache Anpassung und Schneidbarkeit: Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 5, 2024
reviews.title: I love my Kindle! Update!
reviews.body: I have had my Kindle for over a month, and I am still excited about this Kindle. However, I just saw that it is on sale (Black Friday Deal), and I wanted to cry. It is now $45 less than what I paid (excluding the cost of the charger and case; add another $60 to that $45 (Ouch!)). Then, I had to have a talk with myself, and it went like this: Trinigal, stop being upset! You are fortunate to have purchased a Kindle that works well, and doesn’t have issues (like others who have shared the dissatisfaction with their Kindle). You are still enjoying your reads and always looking forward to your next read. I then chuckled to myself, and said that the title of the next book I select should be: When is the right time to buy a Kindle? My original review is below: This is my first Kindle, so I don’t have any other Kindle to compare it to. I usually read a physical book, books on my Computer, IPad or iPhone. This Kindle makes a difference in my ability to relax while reading a book. It is an awesome device that is portable, light weight, and convenient. Although I like reading on my IPad, it will no longer be my go to device for reading (When you know better, you do better). Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 24, 2026
reviews.title: Super little Kindle packed with great features!
reviews.body: I absolutely love this little Kindle. It is perfect to take with me anywhere and fits in my purse or pocket. It charges super fast and the charge lasts for weeks! Downloads my new books very quickly and loads my book in the blink of an eye. Read without glare even in the car in full sun. My big IPad used to hurt my hands to hold and read at the doctors waiting room, not this little gem. I can not say enough about this Kindle….it is great and I highly recommend it.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 11, 2026
reviews.title: Feels worth it! (So far)
reviews.body: First off this purchase was definitely an impulse purchase but so far I am very happy with it I do like the black color (even though I do wish there was a few more color options but still very satisfied). Next I do want to speak about the size, fit, design, and feel its an overall small design and at first I was like wow this is much smaller then I thought it was going to be but I instantly realized that it was a great size the screen I can tweak the text size so reading isn’t an issue but it’s small enough to just fit in my pocket and go and I love that about it makes it easier to keep on me and read when I can! Next the design it’s sleek and simple and for a device that you use to read book it feels like it’s a simple solid design! Now the functionality of it I struggle with reading and focusing in general so that the reason I bought this I could have downloaded an app and read the books on my phone or iPad but struggling to read like I do I get distracted easily so I decided to try this out and so far I have been extremely satisfied with it’s for sure worth it if you want to read more but get distracted easily this will help you focus more on the book and the read then the distraction that come with other devices. Lastly the price this one I’m a little torn on I did go for the bigger size which was more and then the surcharge for the version with no ads seemed a bit unnecessary but I did get 3 months of the kindle subscription so that will help me decide over then next few months if I really deem it worth the extra money and I will update later down the road but so far I am satisfied and I have started The Hobbit book I am loving it so far! (Oh yeah also you can hook up Bluetooth headphones and listen to audiobooks as well which I also love that feature as well cause same thing as before I get distracted easily notifications can make me stop my book and then I’m lost this there is no interruptions so that is another great feeling about this device!) One thing I will say is I wish I could simultaneously listen to the audiobook and read along side the text at the same time but it’s okay I still love it! Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 12, 2026
reviews.title: Great buy for an avid reader!
reviews.body: The display is so easy to read - it does flash black every few pages but I learned it’s to prevent the words from the previous pages to still show. It’s quick to flip pages, I’ve noticed it lags in the store slightly when downloading books but nothing too crazy. All in all it’s a great device that’s so easily portable and makes reading in your spare time so easy! I’ve had this a week and have already finished 4 books, I should’ve got this sooner!
reviews.variant: Color: Metallic RaspberryOption: Without Kindle Unlimited

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 20, 2024
reviews.title: Excellent clarity and blazing speed; a worthy upgrade.
reviews.body: The screen has excellent sharpness and contrast, the best of any ereader I've ever seen in person. It brings to mind another device that featured strikingly good sharpness and contrast despite the layers over the screen: The Voyage. I don't have one on hand to do a direct comparison, but it certainly seems to have that same sort of secret sauce going for it. I'll be very interested to see some direct comparisons from people who still have a Voyage on hand. It's not as apparent in photos, but I'm surprised at how much sharper it is than my 2021 PW. I knew from the early reviews that it would be better, but I wasn't expecting it to be so noticeable. Even my beloved Oasis comes up short in a direct comparison! Much has been made of the speed of this iteration of the PW, and it's not just empty hype. I never thought the word "fast" would ever really apply to an e-ink screen due to the limitations of the technology, but the Kindle team has made that a reality. Pages turn as quickly as I can tap the screen, and while I've never cried out to the heavens about page turns being too slow, it's impressive to see such responsiveness on an e-ink screen. The true benefits of this speed come into play when navigating menus, adjusting settings, and especially when typing. I honestly did not think it was possible for an e-ink screen to be so fast and responsive. This is truly impressive. The speed in itself is not a reason to upgrade to this model, but if you're already considering an upgrade, it is definitely an extra enticement. Read more Read more

reviews.title: Add to cart
reviews.body: I was debating between the paperwhite and the color soft and i can say i don’t regret decision. It’s the perfect size for on the go it fits in my purse no problem. It’s light weight so it feels good in your hand while holding. I was skeptical of the black and white color display but after using it doesn’t really bother me like i thought it would. The screen display is nice with no glare even after putting a screen protector on top. All in all it was worth purchasing.

I also like that the brightness adjusts automatically. The battery lasts a long time, so I don’t have to worry about charging it often. And the 32GB storage is more than enough for all my books. Overall, I’m very happy with this purchase. If you enjoy reading, this is definitely worth it.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 29, 2026
reviews.title: Great extension cord/surge protector
reviews.body: I love this extension cord/surge protector. My house is almost 100 years old and most of the rooms downstairs only have 1 outlet for the entire room, surge protectors with extra long cords are a must and WAY cheaper than re-wiring and installing more outlets. I liked this particular one because of the ample electrical slots and also because the USB and C charging ports are extremely handy. I liked it so much I bought a second one and took it with me while attending to my mom in the hospital. I am able to setup my laptop to work plus charge my phone and any other devices even though the closest outlet is by her bed and not close to the chair/couch.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 3, 2026
reviews.title: Perfect for Tight Spaces & Multiple Devices
reviews.body: The 10Ft Extension Cord with Multiple Outlets and Flat Plug Surge Protector is exactly what I needed. The flat plug design is a game changer — it fits perfectly behind furniture without bending or damaging the cord. The 10-foot length gives plenty of reach, and having multiple outlets makes it easy to plug in everything at once without needing extra adapters. It feels sturdy and well-made, not flimsy like some extension cords. I’ve been using it for lamps, chargers, and small electronics with no issues. Definitely a practical and reliable buy.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 26, 2026
reviews.title: Great extension cord!
reviews.body: I like the flat to the wall plug. The size of it is perfect with the numerous plug-in spaces and the type of charging ports available. The easy on off switch makes it super convenient. I bought the 10ft cord and I've been very happy with my choice. I'm actually going to buy another one. For sure a good choice at a good price

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 21, 2026
reviews.title: Handled everything I threw at it and more! Great value and works perfect for all my needs.
reviews.body: I got this cord to take to the hospital when I had surgery. I knew from past experience that it is sometimes hard to get outlets close to the bed and enough to charge all my devices. I took my phone, an old Apple Touch that had a lot of my Audio books on it, and my Kindle to read and watch videos. It worked great! Not only was the cord long enough to reach the bedside from a slightly distant wall, but I was able to recharge everything at once. Plus, I was even able to plug my C-Pap machine into it as well. There were more than enough outlets to accommodate my needs, but the USB outlets made it so much easier than having to have so many of those 'cube' adapters. The cord was more than long enough, and the device itself was compact and didn't take up valuable table side space, yet still able to handle everything I threw at it, but my husbands' devices as well. It more than paid for itself in sheer convenience and peace of mind. I now have it handling many of my devices in my home office. I am very happy with this purchase.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 26, 2026
reviews.title: Perfect for giant Apple MacBook Pros &c huge power bricks!
reviews.body: I have a frustratingly-shaped flat plug surge protector, and a tower-shaped one (goes with my decor). I ordered another tower-shaped one, but that one was sadly DOA (I haven't even shipped it back yet due to bad weather). I told the Amazon returns guy that called me that while the first tower design was nice and it is still working, I have a specific use-case that may make a "boxier design" fit my needs better (I needed to replace the silly first surge protector, which I still have, and will use, but at the time I got that one, I had Windows; now I need a flat one for Apple). I got this one to replace the DOA one that otherwise would have been fine, and it's WAY better for Apple stuff. First, the Apple MacBook Pro's battery brick is ridiculous, but this surge protector handles it fine (I don't need to "prop it up" like I had to before). Apple plug-ins need more space, period, including the phone charger(s). I like the 2 USB As and 2 USB Cs on this too. But the configuration is just the best! I hope it lasts a long time!

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 14, 2026
reviews.title: Nice device but doubtful on fast charging.
reviews.body: Overall a decent product, but after further testing, things do not charge fast with this extension cord/power strip. When i compare the speed of charging between this and plugging into the wall, it is noticeably slower at charging devices. I probably should have done a better job at reading the description.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 22, 2026
reviews.title: Super Functional & Versatile - Perfect Charging Setup!
reviews.body: This power strip is a great addition to any space. It’s super functional and versatile, with plenty of outlets and a great mix of USB options - including USB-C, which is a huge plus. I love that it’s lightweight and easy to move around, but still feels sturdy and well-made. The flat plug design is perfect for tight spaces, and the long cord gives you flexibility on where to place it. Another bonus - there’s no noise at all. No buzzing or humming, which I’ve had issues with on other power strips. Overall, it’s a really convenient and reliable charging station for home, office, or even a dorm. Definitely recommend!

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 25, 2026
reviews.title: Side-facing outlets make a big difference for bulky plugs
reviews.body: This has been a solid extension cord, but the standout feature is the outlet layout. The plugs are oriented on the sides, which makes it much easier to use multiple bulky power adapters at the same time. With standard power strips, large “brick” chargers usually block adjacent outlets. With this design, you can rotate how things are plugged in and actually use all the ports. The cord length is also useful for reaching where you need it without having to stack multiple extensions. Overall performance has been consistent with no issues so far. Pros Side-facing outlets allow better use of space Works well with large power adapters Good length for flexible placement Reliable performance Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 19, 2026
reviews.title: Amazon Fire TV Stick
reviews.body: I’ve been using the Amazon Fire TV Stick for a while now, and it’s honestly one of the best upgrades I’ve made to my home entertainment setup. The setup process was incredibly simple—just plug it into the HDMI port, connect to Wi-Fi, and you’re ready to stream within minutes. The interface is smooth and user-friendly, making it easy to navigate between apps like Netflix, Hulu, and YouTube without any issues. What really takes the experience to another level for me is how seamlessly it integrates with my Amazon Alexa device. Being able to control my TV with just my voice feels incredibly convenient and futuristic. I can say things like “Alexa, open Netflix” or “Alexa, play my favorite show,”

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 13, 2026
reviews.title: Reliable Service
reviews.body: Reliable function. Portable, (can take with you when traveling/visiting a VRBO, AirBNB, or in your RV). Primary use in our home. We wanted a basic operating Fire TV Stick WITHOUT Alexa features. Have not had one issue since we purchased our first Fire TV Stick years ago. Fair price, quality product, universal use, fast delivery, arrives synced to your account if you choose. A product you can count on at home, or wherever you may go. Thanks, Amazon.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 20, 2026
reviews.title: Amazon Fire TV Stick 4K Select (newest model per it's one title).
reviews.body: Sadly it surprised me how this performed. - Someone who's acclimated to installing stuff like this is okay, but, for those who are not - they'll need a friend. Many in my family would not have gotten this installed and they don't do tech phone call well. And, so gage that for yourself. - Once it "installed" I presumed that my long standing account, as-is would be there. No! - I spent 2.5 hours attempting to guess what was up, saw parts of my account online I never knew existed, and traversed many Setup Menu items in the FireTV menu that fixed nothing (stop, clear cache, restart). The whole restart thing becoming a few x's a day ritual is what caused this purchase - ironic. Described as may go away since "better". No. - So, finally giving in - clicked HELP here and figured out how to fool the menu picks into "other help" needed, not listed some may relate to having to do. So, got to a Chat with CS. - As someone who's engaged development of devices like this on global platforms I consider that Product Support IS part of the PRODUCT itself. What else would it be? Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 28, 2026
reviews.title: Great device for older TV's, keeps them with all the smart options newer TV's have built in.
reviews.body: This is my 6th or 7th Firestick over the years, this one is 4K, but also works in 1080 HD for an older tv in the garage. Most people know what these look like and if not the picture in the listing is accurate. It comes with the Firestick a usb power cable and the power brick to supply power to the Firestick. Super easy to install, Easy to add your own app to the menu. Connects to your wifi quickly so the set up doesnt take too long. All of the controls for the Firestick and most for the TV are on the remote the Firestick comes with there are many options like everything now days, you can configure your setup on the tv how you like with many options for apps and channels to watch. Storage capacity for other apps is good, not sure what the memory capacity is, but I've never had one tell me it was full or to check storage. Rarely have any issues with the device either, they always work as expected, buffer rarely so the stability and usability of the device is strong.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 7, 2026
reviews.title: Easy 4K Streaming Upgrade with Smooth Performance
reviews.body: The Fire TV Stick 4K Select is a simple and effective way to upgrade any TV into a full streaming hub. Setup takes only a few minutes, and the interface is easy to navigate even for people who aren’t very tech-savvy. Streaming in 4K looks sharp and smooth, and apps like Netflix, Prime Video, YouTube, and others load quickly without noticeable lag. The AI-powered search feature is helpful for finding shows across multiple platforms, and the included voice remote makes browsing much faster than typing everything manually. It also offers access to plenty of free and live TV options, which is a nice bonus. Overall, it’s a reliable and affordable streaming device that delivers strong performance for everyday use.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 30, 2025
reviews.title: Limitations beyond sideloading, but probably still the future.
reviews.body: Preface: I've used FireTV devices for quite a while now, and have learned to appreciate what separates them from their competition, but also some of the annoying quirks that they struggle with sometimes. This device, with VegaOS manages to do away with most of the quirks, but also takes away some of the reason why I buy FireTV devices in the first place. Hardware: I'll get this out of the way quickly, since it's basically unchanged and is perhaps the least-impactful aspect of any Fire TV device. Yes, it has a micro-USB port for power, get over it. Amazon probably has a warehouse full of them, and for a connector that you plug in once in a device's life... it's not really impactful. People will always fuss about the lack of USB-c, but they're missing the point. The remote is nicer than my previous remotes, seems more solidly built and feels better in the hand. Unsure how it'll last, but maybe it'll make less creaky noises after a year of service than previous remotes. Software: Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 2, 2026
reviews.title: Works great! Remember to do update when first installing!
reviews.body: Review Edit: I initially had issues with this new Fire Stick 4K and gave it 2 Stars. It was so frustrating because it wasn’t taping shows properly on Hulu. After my review was posted a very nice and knowledgeable Amazon customer service rep reached out to me to see if she could help with my issues. With her help we were able to do an update to the device and now it is working just as it should. ******************* I purchased this to replace an older version Fire Stick TV. I purchased this one because it said it was the latest 4K model. We have others that are working great on other TVs. This new one however is not so great. I use it with my Hulu subscription and I DVR a lot of shows to watch. I noticed immediately there was an issue when recording a show that was 2 hours long, the new Fire Stick TV would only tape the first hour and stop. It never taped the rest of the show, this happened two days in a row. It doesn’t even show up as a full show then next day on my TV list. But ….. it would tape the whole two hours on my Hulu account on my laptop, so I knew it was a Fire Stick TV issue not a Hulu issue. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 1, 2026
reviews.title: Great Upgrade for Slower Smart TVs, Smooth and Easy to Set Up
reviews.body: I bought this item to use with older Roku smart TVs that had become sluggish and unstable over time due to software updates, and it’s been a very effective upgrade. Setup was quick and straightforward, and performance has been smooth from the start. Navigation is responsive, apps load quickly, and it feels like a significant improvement over the built-in software on older TVs. In my case, this ended up being a much more cost-effective solution than replacing the TVs themselves. The displays were still fine. The issue was just the software. The Fire TV Sticks solved that problem completely. Overall, this is a great option if you want to extend the life of an older TV or improve performance without buying a new one.

reviews.body: You know that weird, awkward gap behind your dresser or desk, the one that exists just to make room for clunky plugs and chunky surge protectors? Yeah. This little powerhouse ended that era. The flat plug sits nearly flush with the wall (seriously, check the photo, it’s barely thicker than my finger), which means I can finally push furniture all the way back without sacrificing outlets or sanity. 👏🙌 It’s perfect for tight spaces or anyone who’s sick of their furniture floating two inches off the wall like it’s haunted. 👻 But it’s not just pretty, it’s practical. With 8 AC outlets, 3 USB ports, and a USB-C port, I can charge literally everything in my life, phones, iPads, baby monitors, humidifiers, night lights, and a few mystery cables I refuse to trace. 🔋📱💻 No more fighting over plugs or playing outlet roulette. The 5-foot cord gives you plenty of reach without feeling excessive, and the cube design keeps it all tidy without becoming a tangled disaster zone. I keep one in the bedroom and one in the home office, but honestly… I’m tempted to grab a third just so I never have to deal with a janky strip again. 🤷‍♀️ It’s slim. It’s smart. reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 30, 2025
reviews.title: Modern Power Hub With Smart Ports — Just Not for High-Draw Appliances
reviews.body: I really like this surge protector and ended up buying a second one after owning my first for a while — that alone says a lot about the quality and value for money. The layout is excellent: 8 outlets, plus 3 USB ports including a USB-C, which still feels refreshingly modern. The plug-in capability is great, and charging phones and accessories is fast and reliable with good charging speed. It’s also very flexible in terms of versatility. I use it for everyday electronics, chargers, and desk setups, and the mountability makes it easy to keep things neat and off the floor if you want a cleaner setup. The only reason I didn’t give this 5 stars is power handling. If something with a higher draw gets plugged in — like a hairdryer — the unit will shut itself off. On one hand, I appreciate the safety aspect, but on the other hand, it means this isn’t ideal as a true all-purpose room surge protector for mixed appliances. Overall, it’s a well-designed, modern power strip that’s perfect for electronics and charging stations — just be mindful of its limits with higher-power devices.

reviews.title: The Power Strip that's Slim, Smart, and Just a Little Show-offy!
reviews.body: Oh, dear flat power strip, where have you been all my overloaded, messy-cable life? This sleek beast has turned my single socket into a charging octopus with 8 AC outlets and 3 USB ports, and it's doing the multitasking tango without breaking a sweat. Firstly, let's talk about its physique – a svelte 0.31 inches in thickness. This power strip could sneak into places James Bond wouldn't dare. It's so flat; it makes your other power strips look like they've been on a donut binge. Need extra space for other plugs? Fear not! The 45° right angle design is here to play Tetris with your sockets, making sure everything fits snugly without the drama of blockage. Now, USB aficionados, rejoice! Dual USB A ports, featuring a respectable 2.4A, and a USB C port flexing with 3A. It's like having a VIP section for your devices. And don't get me started on its detective skills – these USB ports can detect charged devices faster than Sherlock Holmes solving a case. Phones, tablets, earphones – they're all welcome to the charging party. But wait, there's more! Surge protection, because this power strip is not just a pretty face. Overload protection, short-circuit protection – it's like having a personal bodyguard for your electronics. The on/off switch is the bouncer, ensuring only the well-behaved gadgets get through, providing a charging environment safer than a library on a Sunday. Read more Read more

I don’t recommend that, and I wouldn’t make a habit of it. I was just stress testing it. Bought a similar thing from Walmart recently. Was less than impressed. The usb ports barely charged my phone. This unit has more charging power from the usb ports. Very high quality. Unbeatable price. Get this one! 8 plug ins, 2 usb ports, and 2 type c ports. This is the one.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 18, 2026
reviews.title: Def worth it
reviews.body: Perfect size & is very lightweight. I absolutely love physical books, but I don’t have enough space for more in my home (plus packing all those books up for moving is a chore & very heavy). I purchased mine in the green color along with the matching fabric case & recommended charger (added up to around $175). It came about a month or so ago, & I haven’t been able to put it down since. The battery is incredibly reliable; the charge lasts FOREVER (realistically—about 5-7 days with daily use). I take it everywhere with me as it fits easily in my bag & the charge holds so well. As far as cost, I save so much money on physical books, primarily because of the kindle unlimited subscription which is roughly $12/month after the trial. I can read as many books as my heart desires for that price alone. *Mwah* Chef’s kiss. The setup was a breeze, but the ‘controls’ were a little difficult to figure out at first, especially on the swipe to get back to the home screen. But everything else is pretty easy. The brightness, text size & other settings can all be changed easily. If you’re going back & forth on the ads vs. no ads feature like I did: CHOOSE ADS. It’s more or less just book suggestions. They only occur on the screensaver, plus the advertised cover art is usually pretty awesome & you get to explore new reads if you want! Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 8, 2026
reviews.title: I READ A BOOK IN 4 DAYS! I haven't read a complete book in years until I bought this Kindle!
reviews.body: I've been trying to read books again for the last couple of years without much success. In 2025 (and probably 2024), I never finished a single book that I started. That includes self-help, college textbooks, and friend's books! Fumbling with books, turning pages, trying to remember to carry it with me, all of that made it difficult for a working adult with a family to sit down and read, even for a few minutes. Not to mention my arthritis, needing to wear reading glasses, needing a light just bright enough to make out the words, and more. I ordered this Kindle on New Years Day and fully expected to get bored of it in a week and want to get my money back. I downloaded my first book (Included in KindleUnlimited), by an author I never heard of (even though she's critically acclaimed and has had books turned into movies) and started reading. I was INCREDIBLY SURPRISED how easy it was to read on this thing. It's MUCH better than a physical book, by leaps and bounds. I started reading the book on Monday and finished it by Thursday. I can't wait to start my next book! The book was good... and that kept me interested, however... a few things about the kindle made it easy to finish the book so quickly... 1) Mainly, it was how easy on the eyes and the size of the text. I didn't need my reading glasses at all and the text was clearer than any book I've opened in the last few years. Read more Read more

reviews.title: Great mount for the price
reviews.body: Works really well. Had a 40 inch TV on it and upgraded to a 65 inch tv. Was a little nervous about its ability to hold it since I was pretty much red-lining the rating for it. After about four months with the 65in TV, the mount is holding up surprisingly well. For the price, it's pretty strong, easily adjustable, and easy to mount.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 6, 2026
reviews.title: Great product, easy install
reviews.body: This was quite slick to install. Only thing to be aware of, it’s about 16” wide, so if your studs are not centered right around the spot you want your TV mounted, you may want to either hit 1 stud and drywall anchor the other side (not what they recommend but probably ok if your TV isn’t very heavy) or do what I did and run a couple 1x4s across a few studs to give you a solid base to mount this into. But mounting it and hanging the TV really was very easy. I am really happy with it - it’s rock solid, has a really wide range of motion, pulls out from the wall quite a long ways, and can turn quite far, too, which makes it easy to reach all the sockets and ports in back.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 17, 2026
reviews.title: Great producy
reviews.body: Very easy to install. Very solid. Not Chincy at all. Comes with everything needed to hang your TV. Works perfectly the first time. Made for a wall with studs 16 inches on center as it is only 16 inches wide at the base. If you can’t line up on your studs probably not right for you.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 29, 2026
reviews.title: Fantastic mount
reviews.body: This mount is excellent. It perfectly fits a standard 16 inch stud spacing, but no more than that. The outer most holes for the stud mount go out to only 16 1/4 inch, and have slotted holes for the smaller stud spacing. More specifically, I bought and mounted this for an LG G5 Series 55” OLED. Not only does it fit for this specific TV, but it also has room for adjusting it up and down, sliding left and right, and allows the TV to come out and inwards using the extendable hinges. I do like the little bubble level it comes with, it’s nice to keep and save in my office desk, for little nuance projects. Only thing I wish it came with, were 4 washers for the lag/stud bolts. The surface area that the bottom of the head of the bolt, doesn’t seem to have much contact area for my liking. So I added 4, 1 inch wide washers to give the bolt more area to clamp the mount and spread the load across the slotted hole. Other than that, with a little measuring and thought, this mount was extremely easy to install. Do take the time to see how far up or down you want mount the long black mounts that actually attach to the TV. Other than that, it’s still holding up great. Before I mounted my TV, I grabbed a bathroom scale, and while i stood on it, I pulled more of my weight onto the mount, and I wanted to confirm it would hold 100 or so pounds. It did so very well, felt and sounded sturdy at about 140 pounds. This manufacture does state 100 pound limit if I can remember correctly. So just keep that in mind.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 19, 2026
reviews.title: Excellent Full-Motion Mount with Strong Build and Helpful Support
reviews.body: This Mounting Dream full-motion TV wall mount has been a great purchase overall. The mount itself feels very solid and well-built, and once installed, it holds my TV securely with smooth movement. It has plenty of adjustment range and feels sturdy even when fully extended. The instructions are mostly clear, but they do seem a little out of order in places. That said, the included template is extremely helpful and makes placement and drilling much easier. One thing to note is that the vertical mounting bars may feel slightly short for some larger TVs, but they were a perfect fit for my 75” LG, so no issues in my case. Customer service really stood out. They were quick to respond to questions and even followed up afterward to make sure everything was working well. That kind of support adds a lot of confidence to the purchase. Pros: • Very strong, heavy-duty construction • Smooth full-motion adjustments Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 6, 2026
reviews.title: **Peak Frugality Meets Solid Engineering**
reviews.body: I’m always hunting for that rare creature where smart design, solid materials, and a completely reasonable price all shake hands and agree to work together. This mount is exactly that. Installation was refreshingly simple. The hardware is stout, the instructions actually make sense, and once it’s lagged into studs the thing feels like it could hold a small rhinoceros, not just a TV. The tilt function is smooth and useful without adding unnecessary complexity or wobble. In other words, it does exactly what good tools should do: disappear into the background and quietly do its job for years. If you believe in spending money *once*, installing it properly, and then forgetting about it forever, this is a fantastic buy. Made from heavy and thick steel but yet simple, affordable, and engineered with just the right amount of practicality. Five stars from the Department of Sensible Spending. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

reviews.body: This thing is exactly what i needed! My baby’s crib is right next to an outlet, and we recently had to lower the mattress, exposing the outlet so our child could reach it and possibly unplug the video monitor or sound machine. This is the only outlet in his room (which is more like a large closet) so I tried to find a big box thing to cover the plugs. Nothing was working until i stumbled on this! It is so flat that i can put the crib right up against the wall, and all of the plugs are safely underneath the crib (with covers on the outlets that aren’t being used because he has crawled under the crib on occassion 😂). I got one that even has usb ports that will be useful later. This is the perfect solution, and it looks pretty too! Highly recommend!

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 7, 2025
reviews.title: Good for those with hard to reach outlits
reviews.body: I’m one of those people with very few outlets in my room, one of them behind my bed. Now, I have a HUGE fear of fire, so haven’t dared to plug anything in back there because of fear of something sparking or overheating. This was exactly what I was looking for! The plate covers the remaining open plug, protecting it, and I was easily able to set it up. Two other features not mentioned on this: it has two hanging holes in the back you can use to hang it on large-head nails, and it has a built in surge protector (according to attached instructions and device information). I was able to use the holes to put it up on my wall (see pic) with some screws I had. If I had one concern, it’s that it sparked once when plugging something in. Only once. Happens occasionally on some outlets, but just in case you’re terrified of fire like me, flip the switch to turn the outlet off first, and then no problem!

reviews.body: The Anker Soundcore 2 Portable Bluetooth Speaker is an impressive speaker that delivers excellent sound quality at an affordable price. Despite its compact size, it produces surprisingly loud audio with clear mids, solid bass, and minimal distortion—even at higher volumes. One of its standout features is the 24‑hour battery life, which easily lasts through a full day (or more) of listening on a single charge. This makes it a great choice for outdoor activities, travel, or all‑day use at home without constantly worrying about recharging. The IPX7 waterproof rating is another big plus. Whether it’s by the pool, at the beach, or caught in the rain, the Soundcore 2 handles moisture without issue. The durable design feels sturdy and well‑built, especially for a speaker in this price range. Bluetooth pairing is quick and reliable, with a strong connection that doesn’t drop easily. The simple button layout makes it easy to control playback and volume, and it also supports dual pairing, allowing you to connect two Soundcore 2 speakers together for a wider stereo sound experience. Overall, the Anker Soundcore 2 is an excellent portable speaker that punches well above its weight. reviews.date: Reviewed in France on May 5, 2025
reviews.title: Bon produit
reviews.body: Cette enceinte Bluetooth Anker SoundCore 2 est une option intéressante pour son prix. La qualité sonore est correcte pour une écoute d'appoint, et les basses sont étonnamment présentes pour sa taille compacte. L'autonomie de 24 heures est un vrai atout, idéale pour une journée entière sans se soucier de la recharge. La portée Bluetooth de 20 mètres et l'étanchéité IPX7 sont également des caractéristiques appréciables pour une utilisation en extérieur ou près de l'eau. Le microphone intégré est pratique pour les appels mains libres. Cependant, si vous recherchez une puissance sonore élevée pour animer une fête ou sonoriser un grand espace, cette enceinte risque de montrer ses limites. Elle est parfaite pour une écoute personnelle ou dans un petit groupe, mais ne vous attendez pas à un son qui remplisse une pièce. En résumé, un bon rapport qualité-prix pour une enceinte portable et polyvalente, mais la puissance sonore pourrait être un peu juste pour certains usages.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 17, 2026
reviews.title: Awesome ear set -- can't be beat at this price point
reviews.body: Outstanding! For the low price (~$20), this is an amazing ear set. I have been using Apple Airpods and then SamSung Buds for the past 6 years. As expected, this set does not have all the features like Apple's or Samsung's, but having used this set for several months, now I am wondering if I ever really needed those features (for a price of $150-200!). Great sound, long battery, light but solid build quality, coupled with no fear of losing! The only thing is that when I drop it on the floor, it opens up. Each pod is made of two pieces, which separate as it hits the floor. After having to click them back together several times, I added superglue before click them back on both sides. Now they don't open up. Definitely recommend this set.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 17, 2026
reviews.title: best buds for the price. look no further.
reviews.body: I never write reviews, but I have to for this product. Just as most have said, these are AWESOME. I have had nothing but bad experiences with other wireless earbuds so I stayed away from them. But as my over the ear headphones of 8 years stopped working, I had to buy new headphones so I decided to give them another chance. I kept hearing good things about Soundcore so I trusted the reviews and they live up to the hype completely. The audio quality of course isn't the best, but it's clear enough, loud enough, and has enough fidelity not just for the pricepoint, but in my honest opinion exceeds it. But the most important thing of all, connectivity. That is the number one factor when looking for wireless earbuds. Many earbuds have decent quality, but if they don't stay connected, who cares. These always stay connected. Previous earbuds, the right would connect but not the left. This is because with other earbuds the right is the primary connected device, while the left is connected to the right bud. With these, it seems both buds are connected simultaneously. Also, these aren't just good for music and movies, even in games they don't lose their synchronicity. Only one problem, when I tried playing a PC game and watching a movie at the same time, the buds did cause significant lag and input delays in my game, but that is totally forgiveable. When the buds are dedicated just t your game and not to another app they work very decently. Most wireless earbuds I tried in the past always had this problem of synchronizing to games. I am absolutely blown away at the quality of these for the price. The biggest downside I have heard is they only last about a year before they stop being functional. But still, for only $20 these are unbeatable if you're on a budget. I recommend the a20i over the p20i as the bass is a little too loud, but you can easily fix this by downloading the soundcore app and choosing the "reduce bass" EQ setting. Also, these are bluetooth only. They don't utilize a USB dongle. So if you have an older laptop or desktop that doesn't have bluetooth, you will need to purchase either a bluetooth dongle or a bluetooth/wifi PCI-E card for your desktop. Read more Read more

reviews.body: Anker makes really good stuff all the time. For a really good price no less. They are a real bang-for-buck type company in the best way meaning possible. These earbuds are genuinely very impressive. I’d say they are most definitely better than Airpod gen 1’s. The cups are excellent and cut off any outside noise. The bass is very smooth for this price point, the vocals are clear, maybe the treble is a little overpowered by the bass, but besides the bigger charging case and slightly bigger appearance and fit, these are superior to gen 1 airpods for sure and for $20 on sale you literally cannot beat this with any other option. The charge hold time is excellent too and the case charges fast.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 6, 2026
reviews.title: Great sound and battery life for the price
reviews.body: These earbuds are impressive for the price. The sound quality is clear with strong bass, thanks to the 10mm drivers, and they work well for music, videos, and calls. The app with EQ presets is a nice bonus—you can easily customize the sound to your preference. Battery life is excellent. With the case, they last a long time, and I don’t have to charge them frequently. Bluetooth 5.3 provides a stable connection without drops. They’re lightweight and comfortable to wear, and the fit stays secure even during movement. Call quality is also decent with the dual mics.

Easy connection to bluetooth. Easy to carry. Nice feeling to the ear. Adjust well. Nice sound. Water resistant ( I put one by accident in the washing machine. reviews.title: These headphones don’t have location only sound so if they die it won’t play anything
reviews.body: These earbuds are really good the sound quality doesn’t sound cheap at all and the noise cancellation is really good and can’t hear anything with both earbuds on and they are crisp and really good the battery life is also really great I tested the battery life on the earbuds without the case full charge it lasted the whole night intell close to afternoon and the case too lasted days by one charge and I also dropped these in water it also been in rain and they are perfect the control are really easy and can customize them in the app it feels cheap but they are really tuff wean it comes to drops and for 19 dollars they are worth it probably better then any cheap headphones I have ever used

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 30, 2026
reviews.title: They’re fine but not my favorite.
reviews.body: I gave these ear pods three stars because I really haven’t had big issues with them - they are just a basic, mediocre set of ear buds in my opinion. I bought these to use until I can afford new AirPods. They are fine, but there are definitely things I dislike about them. The sound quality is just ok, and sometimes it’ll do this thing where the sound goes in and out. That doesn’t happen often, thankfully. Also, they do not stop playing the audio if you take them out of your ears and don’t put them right in the case. My least favorite part, though, is the way they fit in my ears. I have small ears, so I have a hard time finding ear buds that won’t work their way out of my ears as I wear them. These ones are too big and when I touch them to adjust them back into my ear, the touch control is initiated. All I’ve noticed the touch control doing, though, is making beeps when I touch the earbuds to adjust them. I’m not sure what else is supposed to happen, but I haven’t noticed anything changing when I touch the ear buds while I’m listening to them. It’s mostly just annoying. I also haven’t tried to swap out the rubber parts of the ear buds with smaller ones. I can’t remember if this set even came with replacements, and I don’t remember where mine are if they did come with them. So that may help the size issue if I were to find them.

reviews.date: Reviewed in Australia on March 11, 2026
reviews.title: Incredible for the price
reviews.body: Excellent product. Great sound. Easy to connect, great battery life, not a massively long range connection (although my other earbuds are Bose so can't really compete) so you need to stay near your phone, in saying that... They're still well worth it for the price. This is my second pair as I like having multiples as I use them so much. So obviously it shows they're great. Anyone who is on the fence to buy, they're very similar to Sony link buds which are 10x more expensive, these are absolutely comparable to those. And for the price, excellent.

reviews.title: Excellent replacement
reviews.body: I picked up this Universal Remote for my Samsung TV, and it ended up being a great replacement. Setup was incredibly easy—no complicated programming or pairing needed. I just put the batteries in, and it worked right away with my TV. That alone made it worth it. The compatibility is excellent. It works perfectly with my Samsung Smart TV, and all the main functions respond quickly without any lag. The buttons are laid out well, so everything is easy to find and use. The build quality is also better than I expected. It feels solid in the hand, and the buttons have a nice responsive click to them. It doesn’t feel cheap like some replacement remotes do. Overall, this is a simple, reliable, and affordable solution if you need a replacement remote. It works just as well as the original, if not better in some ways. Definitely recommend.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 19, 2026
reviews.title: Good value - quirky operation
reviews.body: 2-pack is a great value. Using to control my 43-inch Samsung TV. Not sure if issue is with remote or TV, but response to button presses are often "delayed" causing me to press more than once thinking the command didn't register. Then it acts as a double-press (or sometimes "triple-press") when it finally does respond. Just waiting to see if the button press registers is the best answer I've found -- if it works, great; if it doesn't, then I can press the button again. Ergonomically, I wish the primary control buttons (up/down/select/left/right and settings) were higher up on the panel. Being on the lower end of the remote, it feels like I'm always gripping just the edge of the remote when using it. But it works, albeit in a manner that I consider "quirky"

reviews.title: Works as it should and all you need are 2 AAA batteries
reviews.body: This pack of 2 replacement remotes came packaged nicely in plastic bubble wrap and in a box so even the postal service wasn’t able to really do damage when they just tossed it into the rain filled ditch at my house and called it delivered. I was very pleased to find they were dry and undamaged thanks to the plastic bubble wrap. Really all I had to do was take the back off and put the 2 AAA batteries in, put the back on and point it at the TV. It was as simple as that. And I appreciate the buttons being softer than others, they don’t make a clicking sound. Worked immediately with no programming, no codes, no fumbling around with small print instructions. Just simple. I’m glad it came in a pack of 2, I put the second one up incase the first one gets lost of damaged due to the fact that I have 2 very careless teens that forget to put the remote back in its designated spot when they are done with it and several chew happy pups that gladly will take advantage of said forgetfulness. All in all, this remote works very well, I can push a button once and it does what it is suppose to do. Very simple and straight to the point. If by chance my teens and dogs manage to lose or chew up both of these, I will purchase this pack again.

name: Life360 Tile - Bluetooth Tracker, Keys Finder and Item Locator for Keys, Bags and More. Phone Finder. Both iOS and Android Compatible. 1-Pack (Black)
description: Tile keeps stuff safe—and people and pets protected. Attach Tile to your important things like keys, bags, and backpacks—no accessories required. With a loud ring and long range, find what you’ve Tiled by ringing the device or viewing its last location from your phone. Double-press the Tile button to find your phone, even when silenced. Plus, Tile can work as an SOS button to send discreet alerts to your Life360 Circle in unsafe situations. Track everything and everyone you love on one map, all from one app.
highlights: THE EVERYTHING TRACKER: Protect lost or stolen stuff with the all-in-one family safety app. Attach to everyday things like wallets, keys, bags, and beyond
STAY SAFE WITH SOS: Keep stuff safe, and people and pets protected. Discreetly trigger an SOS to keep your loved ones safe in any situation
FIND YOUR THINGS: Ring your misplaced Tile, or track it down in the free app
FIND YOUR PHONE: Phone hiding under a cushion? Use your Tile to make it ring — even when silenced
USE WITH LIFE360: Track everything—and everyone you love—a top family connection and safety app. Add your Tiles to see all you love on one map
WORKS WITH iOS AND ANDROID: The free Life360 app works for all households and individuals
TECH SPECS: 350-foot / 105m Bluetooth range. Up to 3-year battery. Water resistant (IP68 rated)

reviews.title: Super useful little trackers 🎀📍 worth it for keys, bags, anything
reviews.body: Okay these are legit helpful, I got 3 in pink and honestly they’ve already saved me from the “where are my keys??” panic more than once 😂📱 They’re lightweight, easy to attach to anything (keys, purse, wallet, etc.), and pair super quickly with the Life360/Tile app. The Bluetooth range is solid for everyday use, and I love being able to ping my stuff from my phone, plus the pink color is so cute (makes them way easier to spot). 💕 If someone in your life is always misplacing things, these are the perfect practical gift that actually gets used. Only tiny thing: the battery is replaceable but I’ll be curious how long it lasts with heavy use, so far though no complaints whatsoever. Honestly, loved these and I’d buy them again... perfect for holidays, gifts, or just because someone loses everything like me 😭✨ Pros: ✔️ Easy to set up Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 25, 2026
reviews.title: Way more useful than I expected!
reviews.body: I thought these just track our keys, but they do so much more than I realized. Have been using since Christmas and LOVE. My favorite feature is that you can actually use the Tile to find your phone. If I lose my phone in the house (which happens all the time), I just hold down the button on the Tile and it makes my phone ring, even if it’s on silent. That alone makes it worth it. It also pairs with the app so we can see each other’s locations, which has been really nice for keeping track of each other and our child. I love that it sends alerts when we arrive home too. I also got the little blue and yellow one and it is so cute , I love that they have fun color options instead of just plain ones. Overall, it’s way more than just a key tracker and has been super convenient for everyday use. Definitely one of those things you don’t realize you need until you have it.

reviews.body: I really like these tiles. They work great! My sister and I went on a cruise last month and we each put one in our luggage. It helped to know our bags were close. I also had enough to put one on my 85yo mom's car keys. We already had a family circle using Life 360, but now, if she forgets her phone or it dies, we can still locate her. No Silver Alerts for us! 🙏🏽 And we can use the tile to locate her phone when she lays it down, or use the phone to locate her keys. Definitely a win-win situation. Easy to set up, and connect and worth the money.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 7, 2026
reviews.title: Title: "Game-Changing Tile Tracker: How It Helped Me Recover My Stolen Wallet!"
reviews.body: Tile by Life360 Mate – Bluetooth Tracker (Green Surf) – My Personal Take When I say a product is worth five stars, I don’t hand it out lightly. For me, a 5-star rating means the item not only works as advertised but transcends expectations—like finding a lifehack you didn’t know you needed. The Tile Mate by Life360? It just earned that rare, well-earned five stars. Let me tell you why. Seamless Setup, Instant Peace of Mind First off, I was blown away by how easy it was to program these little devices. Hooking them up to my phone via the app took under two minutes—no complicated steps, no frustration. I slapped one on my keys, another in my wallet, and bam—instant confidence I’d never lose them again. The app lets you name each Tile, set reminders, and even get notifications when you leave your house without an item. For someone chronically bad at remembering where they put their keys, this is pure gold. Real-Life Lifesaver: Recovering My Stolen Wallet Fast-forward to the real test. I had the Tile Mate in my wallet when it was stolen at a restaurant. Panicked (and rightfully so—credit cards, ID, everything was inside), I launched the Tile app. The tracker pinged to the last known location, and using the “Community Find” feature (where thousands of Tile users anonymously help locate lost items), it showed me a nearby address. I flagged it to the police within 10 minutes, and—miracle of miracles—they tracked the thief down and recovered my wallet (minus the cash, of course). The cards? All intact. The relief? Priceless. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 27, 2026
reviews.title: First Mac, really impressed.
reviews.body: Honestly kind of blown away by how much I like this thing. This is my first Mac and it's insane how much power is crammed into such a small form factor. Not only that, but just overall build quality, fit and finish, everything just feels very premium. I've had several Windows laptops and they all just feel pretty cheap, even at this same price point. That being said, this is Apple's "middle ground" laptop for a reason. If you plan on doing a lot of video editing or intensive gaming, just get the Pro. But everything shy of that runs amazingly well on this, and in a much more compact form factor. You can honestly still play plenty of games on the Air. But if you want to play FPS or something like Cyber Punk, definitely get a Pro. Lite stuff like Balatro, MTG Arena, Hearthstone, Stardew Valley, etc run perfectly on the Air. Also, if you plan on this being your one and only computer, get the 15 inch, a Pro, or connect it to a monitor. For portability, 13 inch is king. But if you are going to be on this computer for 3+ hours a day, get the extra screen real estate.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 7, 2026
reviews.title: Great MacBook for the price!
reviews.body: I needed a new laptop for school and I wanted a MacBook. So, I was pleased when Apple released the Neo as a more affordable option for those who are fond of Apple products. I love that I was able to set it up with my iPhone and it works seamlessly with my iPhone when needed. It’s pretty fast and it’s durable. It’s very lightweight compared to its other Mac counterparts. The screen is crisp and clear. It has great sound quality. I got the Indigo Blue color and I enjoy it thoroughly. I also like that it has a headphone jack to it as well. It’s easy to use like all other Apple products. It’s definitely worth it for the price.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 29, 2026
reviews.title: Impressive web PC, first time mac user, massive upgrade over a 4 year old Lenovo Yoga 13
reviews.body: The neo is really impressive for $600. It has a nice screen, an incredibly rigid aluminum chassis, loads web pages and videos super fast, boots quick. Impressive Bluetooth and WiFi range and performance (Bluetooth is so much better then my old laptop), good speakers(for a laptop), good battery life, a decent keyboard, excellent trackpad, decent web cam, looks nice, low heat output and no vents to get blocked, compact and light. Highly recommended for anyone shopping for a compact web machine. The only negatives on the hardware are: lack of keyboard backlight, the interior edge of the chassis is sharp, not enough to cut you, but enough to be annoying when typing in some positions, not many ports just a normal USB C and a 2.0 speed USB C (both can be used for charging) and screen does not fold flat, so the laptop cannot be stood up against the wall. I was a bit unsure about the Neo because of it using a cellphone chip, but that was not a problem at all. The Neo is super snappy, as fast as my Workstation/Gaming PC when loading web pages and videos. Also I have not noticed any issues with the amount of RAM, 8GB seems sufficient for running a web browser, just as long as you do not go crazy on tabs in Chrome or if you use Firefox that actually manages tabs properly. As a web browser machine I doubt that this will have any performance issues. I am sure that you would hit limits of the mobile CPU if you do did something serious like video editing, but it is more then enough for more casual use. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 9, 2026
reviews.title: Great laptop
reviews.body: The Neo has quickly become one of my favorite pieces of tech, and a big part of that comes down to two things Apple consistently gets right: MacOS and build quality. This is my first MacOS product and it is a joy to use. Everything feels smooth, intuitive, and thoughtfully designed. From the seamless integration with other Apple devices to the clean interface and reliable performance, it just works in a way that lets me focus on what I’m doing instead of fighting with the system. Then there’s the build quality, which is honestly second to none. The Neo feels incredibly solid the moment you pick it up. The aluminum chassis is sleek yet durable, with no flex or creaking, and the attention to detail is obvious in everything from the hinge to the keyboard. It has that premium feel that makes it clear you’re using a quality device. The trackpad is large and precise, easily the best I’ve used on any laptop, and the keyboard strikes a great balance between comfort and responsiveness. Overall, the Neo delivers a polished experience that’s hard to beat. Between the reliability and elegance of MacOS and the exceptional build quality, it’s a laptop that feels worth the investment.

reviews.title: Great first MacBook
reviews.body: My first MacBook, bought a month ago, and I love it. Affordable way to learn about the Apple OS. Love the blue color, it works well with my four year old external monitor that I use with my 15 inch PC, and a small Logitech mouse - I cannot stand touchpads. I don’t mind this mechanical touchpad too much, though, yet I prefer my mouse. The only thing you need to be aware of, is that it might get a bit warm while charging. I use the Neo on top of a laptop cooling fan, mostly because this laptop is a bit too “slippery” on my lap due to its light weight , just over 2 pounds. My grandson will pick one up to use at college in the fall. It will be a great laptop for him to take notes in class. And it is a great first MacBook for those of us who want to eventually change to all Apple - I already have an iPhone, iPad and Apple Watch. So, enjoy it - I sure do.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 10, 2026
reviews.title: Same Apple quality for less $
reviews.body: I’ve had my Neo about a week now and I love it. I was hesitant because it uses an iPhone chip instead of an M chip like other MacBooks. But my 2020 MacBook Air M1 started having display screen issues (vertical and horizontal lines, flickering) so I needed to get something before it failed completely. I didn’t want to spend $1,100+ for a new Air, so I researched the Neo. It’s actually faster than my old Air and I’ve had no problems at all using it for everything I always use my laptops for, which is mostly writing books, email, web browsing, Zoom meetings, and video streaming. I haven’t had any performance problems with a dozen or more tabs open at the sand time. I got the 8Gb, which is plenty even for the thousands of documents and photos I have saved. I have entire book drafts of 200+ pages saved, a doctoral dissertation, and hundreds of research articles saved for my dissertation. It’s more than adequate for my needs. It’s very light weight. The citrus color is pretty. The keyboard is very responsive and feels even better than the Air. The battery doesn’t last as long as the Air before needing recharged but it’s still long lasting. No complaints.

reviews.title: Lovely for people in the Apple ecosystem alteady
reviews.body: This is a lovely little notebook for work or school. It comes with Apples office programs for free and will serve you well for most light computing and even some light gaming. It's integrated in Apple's ecosystem and you can use your iPad as a second monitor and mirror your iPhone screen and all sorts of other integrations. Safari makes it effortless to add your webapps to the dock and it runs iPad apps. Despite costing half what my i7 laptop did and having half the ram the processor is 60% faster at running Powder Toy than the i7. It does this with no fan, while emulating the x86 Powder Toy with Rosetta. It uses a lot less battery than the i7 laptop does to do this. Performance is really, really good. Battery life is really really good. The build quality is excellent. This thing is made of metal and it's hefty. The base stays on the table when you lift the lid. It feels like a much more premium device compared to my expensive plastic windows laptop. One downside compared to my Intel laptop is that only 33 watt charging is supported over usb-c, and in fact the included charger is only 20 watt! That makes it take a bit longer to charge the battery full when depleted. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 31, 2026
reviews.title: A Great Alternative to Ear Buds!
reviews.body: I LOVE these headphones! I was initially hesitant to buy this headset because I already had AirPods, but i always struggled to find ear buds that fit in my apparently small ears and didn’t constantly fall out. After some encouragement from my fiancé who already had these headphones, I decided it was worth a shot to try something different. Boy I’m glad I did! The sound quality and noise cancellation are top notch, and the battery life is frankly amazing. I rarely have to charge them even after using them for hours at a time. Comfort-wise, it isn’t perfect as long usage can result in some very mild discomfort in the form of ear squishing. However, overall the comfort is pretty great, especially in comparison to ear buds which always caused me a lot of discomfort and even pain when wearing for 30 minutes or more. So far, they’re also pretty durable and robust. Highly recommend!

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 17, 2026
reviews.title: Beats Solo 4- Pink
reviews.body: This is my first pair of Beats headphones. Besides loving the fact that they were available in pink, the sound is great. The only thing that is a con for me is that I thought that they would have more bass in them, but they don’t, which isn’t a takeaway for me. They also support Spatial Audio if you have that feature set on your phone for iPhone. When you turn your head left or right, it sounds like the music is following whatever direction your head is turned, and that’s really cool. The ear support is really comfortable, and when I have them on for a while, they don’t make my earlobes hurt compared to the last headphones I have owned. The quality is great, they can fold when not in use, and can be placed in the case that they come with. The volume control is decent, depends on what your phone volume settings are set to especially if you have a phone that detects high volume and suggest that you turn your volume down to keep from getting hearing loss or damage. Noise cancellation is decent also.

reviews.title: Good sound quality
reviews.body: Lightweight, good sound and connects easily. Love the color. It did make my ears sweat some but I was working outside in the yard.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 10, 2025
reviews.title: Pros and cons got me on the fence about keeping
reviews.body: So today is the last day before my return window closes and I am on the fence about keeping them. Straight up there are features I really like. But there are two major downsides that cause me to think twice. I have been wearing them everyday since the day I received them, for hours a day, in multiple scenarios : work, workout, yard work, housework, etc. Especially as I really wanted to give them a fair chance. Right from the get go just a few hours in they began hurting my ears. So I did what I usually do with other like-headphones: I turned them around. At first when I did this they seemed to be unidirectional but after a few times they became bidirectional and it really helps with the longevity of wearability, esp if I keep flipping all day on long days. But still nothing like my old headphones that I have had for years and paid $50 for, which have outlasted multiple other pairs. With my old ones I can go 8 hours before feeling the need to flip em around due to discomfort. But staying on the longevity side what I do love about them is the battery, which I can go three to four long days of wear without needing to recharge. Love love love that!! Esp when needing to run out of the house and didn’t have time to charge my headphones. Plus’s the ease of connecting to WiFi! Love!! Instead of having to go into systems and go thru the whole manual routine of connecting, these you just press the button and the screen pops up to connect! Plus they do let you know when you have a potential important message, whether text, email or fb and asks if you want it read. Then it seems to have smart capability to filter according to your interests. Even not to interrupt you again if you say no. One of the biggest drawbacks, though, esp when working in the heat, like yard work or cleaning out garage, is they cause my ears to sweat. And I’m typically not a sweater! Hate that! And they really sweat! They are attractive and I got the “pink,” which as some have said don’t look pink, except in certain lighting. Which honestly after using fur a month I do think of these as my “pink” headphones. If the ear cuff piece wasn’t so small they’d be perfect! Sound quality and all that is good but I’m not an expert on that, as I’m typically listening to audio books or podcasts and not necessarily music. And they did come in a really nice packaging. But so today I’m opting to wear my old headphones, which are finally, after ten years, starting to lose their charge-ability, but so that’ll be the test of whether I just need to look for another version of those or decide to keep the Beats Solo. I do like them but like a pair of shoes, you gotta have comfortably! Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in Canada on December 17, 2025
reviews.title: Iconic style, modern performance
reviews.body: The Beats Solo 4 represents a major technical leap for one of the most recognizable pairs of headphones on the planet. While the iconic, foldable design remains familiar, the internal hardware has been completely overhauled to meet modern standards. The Gold and Black aesthetic provides a high-end, sophisticated look that feels more mature than earlier iterations. One of the most impressive upgrades is the staggering 50 hours of battery life, which allows most people to go an entire week without reaching for a charging cable. Even if you do run out of power, the Fast Fuel feature provides five hours of playback from just a ten-minute charge, and the addition of a 3.5mm port means you can still listen to music even when the battery is completely dead. Sound quality has also seen a significant refinement. These headphones now feature custom 40mm transducers designed to reduce electronic interference and distortion, resulting in a much cleaner and more balanced sound profile than the bass-heavy Beats of the past. They also support Personalized Spatial Audio with dynamic head tracking, which creates an immersive, theater-like experience for movies and music. It is worth noting that these are "on-ear" headphones, meaning they rest directly on your ears rather than surrounding them. While the new UltraPlush cushions are incredibly soft, they prioritize a secure fit that stays put during movement, which some users may find a bit snug during very long listening sessions. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in Canada on April 23, 2025
reviews.title: Worth the price, good sound enough, perfect for workouts
reviews.body: I'm a huge headphone collector always looking for good quality sets. A very long time ago I used to own the first beats generation whose quality was amazing... over time I started using other sets and brands like Sony, Bose, etc.. I have a lot of sets for particular purposes. Yes is right that these don't come with noise cancelling and that music doesn't stop when you take them off... but I was not looking for any high end/premium headphones, but instead something more simple, classic, that could deliver good sound quality. It is also true that its bass is not what it used to be, but this is to help to provide a better and more crisp sound. Apple indeed made a downgrade and I'm not a full fan of downgrading bass, but being able to use the spatial sound feature in these is great, it makes music much more enjoyable with clear vocals and you can actually distinguish instruments better than just having a full blast on the bass. Again, I wasn't going for a noise cancelling set, and its "passive" noise cancelling is good enough (not for very loud places like airplanes though) but that's why I have other sets in case I want noise cancelling. This stays on pretty well while working out and even though is not specified it does feel sweat resistance and they feel pretty comfortable. Love their size btw, small, compact enough to fit in a my already bulky backpack. It came with a very nice case/pack where I can fill with a few more stuff, so that's a big plus. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 18, 2025
reviews.title: 10/10 thank god for amazon
reviews.body: i was gifted airpod pros 2nd gen as an early christmas present, but they didn’t come with the box so i was stuck with the massive eartips they come in the box with. they kept falling out my ears and i had to keep pushing them back in, which is really annoying. then i bought these ear tips and im really glad they came with multiple sizes because as it turns out, my ears are different sizes. these new ones are much more comfortable and they are a much better fit for my ears, as now everything sounds much more crisp and the noise cancelling is much better now thanks to the seal from having eartips that actually fit my ears. my one complaint is these ones i had to practically pry off the little paper thing they come on, and i was worried that i would accidentally tear them, but after much yanking and pulling they did come off completely in tact, so i assume that means they’re good quality. i haven’t needed to use the cleaner yet because my airpods are still basically brand new, but it feels sturdy and well made. all in all, worth every penny.

reviews.title: Great for price
reviews.body: These ear tips completely saved my AirPods Pro. I’d been fighting with my original tips for a while—constant slipping, weird seal, and honestly I was worried I’d have to replace the whole earbuds eventually. I decided to try these replacement tips and I’m really glad I did. Fit & Comfort: Super comfy. The silicone feels soft but secure, and once they’re in, they stay in. No more constant readjusting or feeling like one is about to fall out when I move or chew. Noise Isolation: The seal is actually better than my original tips. Noise cancellation feels more effective, and even without ANC on, they block a lot of ambient noise just from the fit alone. Sound Quality: Bass feels a bit punchier and everything sounds clearer, which I’m guessing is from getting a proper seal again. Music, podcasts, and calls all sound great. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 15, 2026
reviews.title: Good buy!
reviews.body: These replacement ear tips worked perfectly. I’ve had my AirPods for years and one of the original ear pieces finally tore, so I bought these to replace it — and I couldn’t be happier. They fit just like the Apple ones and seal really well, which keeps the noise-canceling working properly. They also look identical to the originals, so you’d never know they weren’t Apple brand. I’ve been using them for a few months now and they’ve held up great with no signs of wearing out. They’re very comfortable and stay in place well. If you need to replace worn or damaged AirPod ear tips, this is an excellent and affordable option that performs just like the originals.

reviews.body: Ear pieces were comfortable, perfect size, and did not poorly affect sound quality. The cleaning products were not the greatest.

reviews.body: Perfect fit and everything works exactly as Apple. Easy to put on and the variety of sizes was really helpful.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 6, 2026

reviews.body: Great assortment is sizes for all ears. They work great and stay in place in your ears. Noise cancellation and the seal works great.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 4, 2026
reviews.title: Perfect
reviews.body: These were super easy to put on, the cleaners that come with them are very helpful to clean the case. They are comfortable, still allows noise cancellation, and are a great quality. I got one for my husband and for myself.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the Netherlands on January 10, 2026
reviews.title: Just as original
reviews.body: Perfect!

reviews.date: Reviewed in Canada on January 16, 2026
reviews.title: Perfect fit and sound quality restored
reviews.body: These replacement AirPod ear tips are excellent and honestly just as good as the originals. The fit is snug and secure, and they stay in place even during longer listening sessions or while moving around. Installation took seconds and felt intuitive — they snap on properly without feeling loose or fragile. Sound quality improved immediately after swapping out my old tips. Bass feels fuller, audio is clearer, and passive noise isolation is noticeably better, which makes a big difference for calls and music. Comfort is also a big plus — no pressure or irritation, even after extended use. The material feels high quality and durable, not cheap silicone, and they hold their shape well. I also appreciate that they match the original look of the AirPods, so once they’re on, you can’t really tell they’re replacements. Overall, this is a simple, affordable upgrade that restores both comfort and performance. If your original tips are worn out or lost, these are an easy five-star replacement and well worth it.
