reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 13, 2026
reviews.title: Perfect last minute table runner!
reviews.body: I used these for a last minute table runner for Easter dinner. I twisted the fabric down the middle of the table, spreading open every few inches to place small centerpieces and Easter eggs. I placed another runner on both sides of the center runner and added place settings on top (for a total of 10 place settings). Took some stretching because I made random loose twists around each plate. Sorry I forgot to take a final photo- the one shown is when I first started playing around with just one piece of the material, as I had no idea how I was going to use it. I also purchased the matching napkins which turned out to be a nice unusual twist - I placed one on each plate and placed the silverware on top. So simple & easy and pretty. Perfect dusty rose color. Nice fabric - just the right amount of thin while not being flimsy. Packing was creative and protective while also not using unnecessary packaging. Highly recommend. Hope this helps someone!

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 20, 2026
reviews.title: 3 years later, still chopping like new!
reviews.body: I bought this in May of 2023 and as of February of 2026, this little chopper is still going strong! It's super easy to use, makes chopping anything super fast, and the blades have stayed sharp as anything. I only use the two square blades, so I can't speak on the others, but if they are of the same quality, then I assume they would be great too. It cleans super well in the dishwasher as long as you brush out any bits of food in the top grid and on the blades first. Otherwise they might not wash off and end up dried to the blades, making it much harder to clean. I've had to clean the dried bits off more than once when my kid loaded the dishwasher and didn't make sure there were no food chunks on it. I especially love this thing for chopping onions. Thanks to the pieces being contained in the bin after being chopped, they don't have the opportunity to make my eyes water. I leave it all in the bin until it's time to dump it in the pan. I have recommended this thing to everyone I know, plus in posts on Reddit. It's probably my most used kitchen gadget, getting used almost every single time I cook. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 16, 2026
reviews.title: If you cook, you need this.
reviews.body: I don't write many reviews any more, but I had to offer my opinion for this one: It's the best thing since sliced bread. I am eating way more salads and vegetables because chopping is pretty much effortless with this little gadget. It's simple to use and does exactly what it's supposed to do--not something you find in many products today. I regularly chop cucumbers, onions, tons of peppers, and even cherry tomatoes (which it chops easily without smashing them). This is particularly well suited for people with arthritis or other hand issues. It takes all the work out of chopping and cleans up easily. So... if you cook, get you one of these. This little guy is a power house.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 29, 2026
reviews.title: Great for Certain Veggies but the Cleanup is a Chore
reviews.body: I purchased this vegetable chopper in mid-2023 to make meal prep more efficient and slightly more fun, but after over a year, my feelings are mixed. The assembly is straightforward as the different blade options snap and lock into the base easily, and the overall fit of the components is secure. It has a clean, professional appearance on the counter, but the actual functionality depends entirely on what you are trying to chop. While the machine offers versatility with several blade options, it doesn't meet the bar for everything in my kitchen. Soft vegetables like tomatoes are not good candidates as they tend to get smashed, and very hard vegetables like carrots are too difficult to push through. For me, it really only works well for bell peppers and onions, and even then, you have to cut the vegetables down significantly first unless you want to throw your entire body weight into it. The biggest drawback for me is the cleanup. The blades are not the easiest to clean, and even using the provided plastic claw, removing debris is rarely effortless. Since I only regularly use it for two types of vegetables and then freeze them in batches, I don't feel it provides great value for the money for my specific household. However, I can see where someone who chops a much wider variety of moderate-density produce daily would get more use out of this. Read more Read more

name: KitchenAid Classic Multifunction Can Opener and Bottle Opener Easy to Use, Razor Sharp Stainless Steel Cutting Wheel, Soft Ergonomic Handles, Black
description: Open any sized can or bottle with the KitchenAid Classic Can Opener. The durable, razor-sharp stainless steel cutting wheel easily pierces and smoothly slices through cans, while the large knob provides a secure grip for effortless turning. The oversized, ergonomic handles provide maximum comfort and control for simple, safe use. This 2-in-1 tool features an integrated bottle opener to remove caps with ease. Hand wash with warm water and a mild detergent; rinse and dry immediately. One year hassle-free replacement and lifetime limited warranty.
highlights: EASY, SMOOTH OPENING EVERY TIME: Equipped with a durable, razor sharp stainless steel cutting wheel, this can opener pierces cans easily and slices smoothly for a hassle free experience.
EFFORTLESS TURNING KNOB: The oversized, easy to turn knob offers a secure grip, allowing you to open cans with minimal effort and maximum control, ideal for users with limited hand strength.
SOFT, ERGONOMIC HANDLES FOR COMFORT: Designed with oversized, ergonomic handles that provide a comfortable and secure grip, minimizing hand strain and making it easy to open cans of any size.
2 IN 1 MULTIFUNCTIONAL TOOL: This tool is equipped with a built in bottle opener, allowing you to easily remove caps in one convenient design, saving space in your kitchen.

VERSATILE FOR EVERYDAY USE: Suitable for a wide range of can and bottle sizes, this opener is designed to be a reliable, go to tool in any kitchen, whether you’re opening soup cans or beverage bottles.

Very quick cutting. We had one for about 10 years, switched to another brand that stopped cutting after 2 years, and we are back to this awesome kitchen aid opener.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 30, 2026
reviews.title: Solid, reliable can opener
reviews.body: This KitchenAid manual can opener has become my favorite kitchen tool for opening cans. The sharp stainless steel cutting wheel glides smoothly through lids and the large ergonomic handles provide a comfortable, non-slip grip, just as many reviewers have praised. I like that it doubles as a bottle opener and the easy-turn knob requires little effort to use. Some reviewers note that the metal parts can rust if left soaking, so I make sure to hand wash and dry it immediately. Overall it's a sturdy, reliable opener that feels solid in the hand and does the job well.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 4, 2026
reviews.title: Must hand clean and dry - will rust.
reviews.body: Purchased Jan of 2025 literally lasted me less than a year. It was working really great and was easy to use until it broke. Mainly due to user errors It says not to put in the dishwasher and hand wash. PLEASE FOLLOW those instructions. Getting lazy and just throwing in the dishwasher, yea it might come out okay the first dozen times but eventually it will RUST. Which I don’t think the rust resistant feature is accurate (hence the 4 star) I saw some reviews saying it was fine after going in the dishwasher don’t take that as absolute truth. Other than that, if you take care of this thing the right way, it will last a good while.

reviews.title: The best facial (not dish) washcloths!

After much research i ordered these (even though they are supposedly for dishes) and they are PERFECT! I have been using Italy towels for years but stopped because i think they’re too rough for my aging skin. These leave my face feeling soft, clean and exfoliated without being too rough and damaging. They are so much better than a regular washcloth which doesn’t dry out due to thickness and harbors bacterial growth, the loop is perfect for drying and for the price you can get several packs so you can always have a fresh one.

I might from time to time rate something five star, but I never rave about it. Happy to rave about these, though. 100% cotton, no microfibers or plastics. If you buy these you will be happy. Highly, highly recommended.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 13, 2026
reviews.title: Best organizer with a water spout to keep them counter dry!
reviews.body: I am thoroughly impressed with this product. I tried a product that goes over the middle of the sink and it blocked everything off and would always fall off in a funny ways. I had no room for my pots and pans and started getting irritated with it. This is up and out of the way has a little spicket to drain off the water and as you can see I got all of my sponge and soaps in the same spot which is wonderful cleaned off my counter beautifully. I chose the color black to blend in with my black countertop and also to look nice and clean on the counter. One of the best organizers I have ever found for a countertop. It's sturdy metal so far seems rust resistant, but I haven't had it long enough to know for sure. Mobilize easily to clean. Fabulous little sink caddy. I highly recommend to those of you that like to be organized and keep your counter nice and dry, and still arrange things so that it can be out of the way.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 17, 2026
reviews.title: Great organizer for money, measure ahead of time.
reviews.body: So far we love this sponge holder. It can hold a sponge in its own area, a large dawn dish soap container and a wide handsoap bottle. The little drain allows excess water to drain into the sink. So far, the black coloring is still there and there are no signs of rust, but it is still early in our using it. The organizer is easy to assemble. The price point is a good value for the quality of the organizer. My one warning is that this was too large for the area we originally wanted to place it (our faucet and soap dispenser were in the way) so we moved it to the left of our sink where the “to be washed” dishes typically sit. So near the front of the sink but to the side. Make sure to look at measurements before purchasing.

name: Reynolds Kitchens Unbleached Parchment Paper Roll, Non Stick Parchment Paper for Cooking and Baking, Oven Safe up to 425 Degrees, Chlorine-Free, 45 Feet x 12 Inches, 45 Total Sq. Ft.
description: Reynolds Kitchens Unbleached Parchment Paper is a versatile and dependable choice for your cooking and baking needs. Designed to fit most standard cookie and sheet pans, this 12-inch wide parchment paper easily lines your sheet pans for easy clean-up—no trimming or extra folding required. This non-stick parchment paper for baking is oven-safe up to 425 degrees and can be used underneath foods placed in the microwave or oven to help eliminate leftover food residue. With our non-stick parchment paper, your food effortlessly slides off so you can prepare chicken tenders, bread, pizza, pretzels, or your favorite holiday recipes with confidence. This unbleached parchment paper is made with 75 percent unbleached fibers and 0 percent chlorine bleaching. The packaging is fully recyclable, and the parchment paper is made from responsible sources as certified by the Forest Stewardship Council (FSC). With quality you can trust, Reynolds Kitchens Unbleached Parchment Paper is the reliable choice to make your kitchen tasks easier and your clean-up quicker.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 19, 2026
reviews.title: Acceptable for the Price
reviews.body: It seems absurd to spend $50, or even $100, on a meat thermometer. But if you want a good thermometer that works quickly and accurately, then the price is just a bullet you'll have to bite. I tested four meat thermometers: this one, the Thermopro Lightning ($45), the Thermopro TP620 ($30), the Thermopro TP19H ($15), and the Thermoworks ThermoPop 2 ($47). Some meat thermometers have some lag between the first displayed reading and the final accurate reading. With the TP19H, this lag is the most pronounced, as it takes between 10 and 20 seconds in many cases to reach an accurate reading. The TP620 and the ThermoPop 2, have less lag, but still take between 5 and 10 seconds to reach an accurate reading. The Thermopro Lightning, however, is a true instant-read thermometer, as it provides a fast initial reading, and the final accurate reading takes no more than two seconds to appear. The Lightning is the only one that I know that I can trust to quickly provide an accurate reading. I find this to be surprising given Thermoworks' reputation, but perhaps their Thermapen ONE, which I did not retest since it retails for $125, is what merits their reputation. For $45, however, the Thermoprop Lightning appears to be mightly close in performance to the much more expensive Thermoworks and is certainly better than the ThermoPop 2 that I tested. The ThermoPop doesn't even have a magnet or hook. All of these thermometers are waterproof under running water. I probably wouldn't put them in the dishwasher, however. The displays on all of these are fine, and I think that the Thermopro Lightning's display stands out quite well. If you're looking for a quality meat thermometer that will do exactly what you expect it to do, get the Lightning. But if you need the cheapest meat thermometer out there, the TP19H here will suffice. But be warned: the lag of the thermometer in providing an accurate reading will likely be frustrating and uncomfortable, especially over substantial heat, which is obviously the circumstance in which you're likely to be using this product. I've used all of these products, and I didn't want to spend $45 on a meat thermometer. At $45, the Thermopro Lightning isn't cheap, but if you can swing it, you'll have a for better meat thermometer that truly provides three times the value compared to this cheaper model. reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 26, 2026
reviews.title: very good Olive Oil Dispenser
reviews.body: At first I wasn't sure how something could have 2 spray patterns without doing some kind of nozzle adjustment but it does. Plus it has a pour spout by pushing a button that then pops up to expose the pour tube. I have several other olive oil sprayers and bottles and such. Most of them come with tiny funnels to fill them and when using a gallon container to fill them would be best as a two person job... but with just me filling them it becomes a real balancing act that didn't always balance... This oil sprayer bottle is wide enough that it doesn't need a funnel to fill it. In fact I fill this first then use this pour spout to fill my 32 ounce pour bottle (it's not a sprayer), yes it takes two fills of this bottle to fill that bottle then another to fill this bottle, but it does avoid that balancing act. The two spray types does work. Slow press for stream and fast press for for mist. To pour push the slide button on top to flip the pour sprout open. It's the slow and fast press that I had to see to believe but it really does work. I've had and tried many types of olive oil sprayers from ones with a stainless steel bottle with a plastic spryer that the bottle would fall off while using to ones with large handles that break to ones that would not spray no matter how much try pumping it. reviews.body: I’m super happy with this lunch box. The design is sleek and low-key, so it doesn’t look bulky or awkward to carry around. It’s definitely more stylish than most lunch boxes I’ve owned, but not it a bad way. Size-wise, it’s pretty much perfect: not huge, but not cramped either. I can fit four frozen ice packs in it without any trouble, along with my food. Insulation is been impressive. If I put the lunch box in the freezer overnight or start with frozen packs, everything stays cold for around 8 hours during the day, which is exactly what I was hoping for. It feels well made, the zippers are smooth, and it holds its shape nicely. Cons: If you pack a lot of containers or bulky meals, this may feel kind of small (for me, it holds a canned soda, water bottle, a large Tupperware along with ice packs) Best for: People who want a clean-looking lunch box that fits a normal workday meal, uses ice packs, and doesn’t take up a ton of space in a fridge or bag. name: STANLEY Quencher ProTour Flip Straw Tumbler with Leakproof Lid 40 oz | Built-In Straw and Handle | Cup Holder Compatible for Travel | Insulated Stainless Steel Cup | BPA-Free | Prickly Pear
description: Can you feel it? Those good vibes that carry you through the day. From fresh Lichen to Prickly Pear, and Agave to Pink Mesa, it’s about being present now. Going where you want. And getting exactly what you want. This is your Summer Edit. The leakproof 40-ounce Quencher ProTour Flip Straw Tumbler makes hydration simple. Flip up to sip, then snap it shut when you’re done. Thanks to double-wall vacuum insulation, your drink stays cold for hours. It’s ready to toss in your bag and fits most car cupholders.
highlights: SEEK SOMETHING DIFFERENT THIS SUMMER: All you gotta do is choose - discover our new Summer colors (Lichen, Prickly Pear, Agave & Pink Mesa). The leakproof Stanley Quencher ProTour Flip Straw Tumbler makes hydration simple. Flip up to sip, then snap it shut when you’re done.

LEAKPROOF HYDRATION: The ProTour Flip Straw lid lets you sip without worry. Just flip up the built-in straw for a quick drink, then snap it shut to prevent spills. This leakproof tumbler is ready for all your adventures.

ICE-COLD REFRESHMENT, ALL DAY: Thanks to double-wall vacuum insulation, your drinks stay ice cold for hours. Whether you're at the gym, on a hike, or at your desk, the Quencher ProTour keeps your water refreshingly chilled.

BUILT TO LAST, BUILT FOR YOU: Made from 18/8 recycled stainless steel, this BPA-free tumbler is as durable as it is sustainable. It's dishwasher-safe, meaning no hassle when it’s time to clean. Enjoy your water worry-free, wherever life takes you.

PERFECT FIT, EVERY TIME: With a textured comfort-grip handle and a narrow base that fits most car cup holders, the Stanley Quencher is as easy to carry as it is to store. Ideal for your commute, gym bag, or next road trip, this tumbler is always right by your side.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 9, 2026
reviews.title: Well made, easy to clean, and no leaks.
reviews.body: It’s very good. Keeps ice for at least 18 hours. Stays super cold, doesn’t leak, easy to clean. Works great for hot liquids too but I would be worried about the straw as I become more aware of microplastics and begin to eliminate them from my life they are literally everywhere if you actually analyze what they are in and how the break down over time we have a really tough uphill climb. I would like to see this straw replaced with something that will never break down and get into our system period. We would ideally have to use only a product with steel insulated walls and no plastic contact, not even the o-ring. Even those break down and get into our liquids over time and that amount of time is very short. It doesn’t fit in all cup holders because of the cup holder depth, if it’s a deep cup holder then the handle gets in the way. Its versatile to an extent I guess, but it becomes cumbersome quickly depending on the application. I think it’s good for hangout at the pool or beach but beyond that its size and shape start to annoy pretty quickly. It’s also heavy if you’re loading with ice and liquid and if you add chopped fruit or veggies then you’ve got yourself a pretty weighty jug rapidly. Its also very tall because they try and accommodate standard cup holders so keep that in mind, it’s easy to knock over and if it’s full of ice, liquid and chopped fruit and veggies for flavor enhancing it’s quickly like carrying a small carryon or cinder block with a handle. Overall though it’s a tank, keeps liquids cold as heck, doesn’t leak, sturdy, and easy to clean but quickly can feel like you’re carrying a 5lb infant around, that’s top heavy, not stable in many standard cupholders becoming a cumbersome dumbbell quickly. Speaking of dumbbells it can be used as one in a pinch Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 23, 2026
reviews.title: Diehard Yeti/Rtic user but....
reviews.body: I always stick with yeti and rtic but I needed something that fully closed and still had my beloved straw. Yeti/Rtic just didnt make something in any size that meets these requirements. I was honestly really skeptical I was even going to like this cup but so far I really like it. Ive had water in it for about 8hrs and its still cold. The lid doesn't leak. The straw is sadly not my normal straw but I do like this one - its a good shape and not too bulky. I do like the newer handle design. I have arthritis and limited hand function at times and I find even with water in it I can hold it fine and it's not too heavy. I think my only 2 complaints are related to the lid. Its difficult to screw on and off (hopefully that will ease up the more I use it) and its not as thick and rigid like other lids ' the edges flex just a bit and that is enough to have be a little worried how it will hold up. Also the straw is a little difficult to pull up and push down but again I am hoping that over time that will become easier. Overall I do think its a good cup and the color is fantastic and bright so that makes me super happy!

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on October 25, 2025
reviews.title: Great little product! Hard-boiled eggs are no longer a challenge!
reviews.body: Bought this about two years ago because the egg-timer I had that actually sits in the boiling water with the eggs wasn't working as consistently as I had hoped. I saw this Dash Rapid egg Cooker and decided to give it a try. Love it! Mostly the eggs are cooked to satisfactory doneness, although occasionally a smidge under (but not grossly) or a tad over, but definitely cooked and edible. I found that using the 4+ level on the measuring cup for 6 eggs and filling it to just above that mark is enough water to cook the eggs to the required doneness. I've used the omelet feature a couple of times and it's okay, but mainly I use the DASH Rapid Egg Cooker for making hard-boiled eggs. My husband loves hard-boiled eggs so this was a great way to make sure he had his weekly quota. I would recommend this product! I've read other reviews where the purchaser said there's no way to store the trays and other paraphernalia, which is not exactly true. The trays (there are three; one for boiled eggs, one for scrambled, and a split tray for maybe omelets) fit conveniently inside the unit, with the stem and measuring/poking cup nestled in the split tray. I've not had any issues with them not fitting. And there is just one way to use the stem (I rarely use it, though) and it fits just one way, too. Doesn't take a rocket scientist to figure out (and I am not a rocket scientist!). If I can figure it out, anyone else can, too. :-D Read more Read more

reviews.body: I absolutely love this egg cooker! It makes preparing eggs so quick and effortless. reviews.title: Works perfectly!
reviews.body: I wish I had this little gadget earlier in life. I love hard boiled eggs but 9 times out of 10 they are either overcooked, undercooked, green, etc....plus difficult to peel. I have watched so many videos describing how to make hard boiled eggs turn out correctly. Turns out all I needed was this device. Follow the directions, put the finished eggs directly into ice water for 10 minutes, and you will have perfect hared boiled eggs that are easy to peel. Love this product. Worth 10 times the price.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 9, 2026
reviews.title: Simple, inexpensive Egg Cooker makes perfect soft-boiled/hardboiled eggs!!!!!
reviews.body: I love poached eggs but using 2 or 3 cups of water for 2 eggs and waiting for the water to boil was annoying, not to mention having to clean a teflon-free pan with egg glomming onto the sides.... until I found this little thing. I don't actually use the little plastic poaching pan that comes with this, I just stand them up on the grid and steam them. Then I do like the Brits do, whack off the top and either eat it right out of shell or spoon it on to a plate. Perfect every time! Uses a couple of tablespoons of water compared to a whole pan and cooks in about 5 minutes cutting my consumption of electricity and water! I And no cleaning egg residue out of the saucepan! I concede I'm not as handy as the Brits whacking off the top but bought an egg cracker that does the job quite well. And it would be a good idea to have at least one egg cup to hold the egg while you open it because it's VERY hot right out of the steamer and could be more than a little messy without the egg cup! It also does a great job hard boiling eggs too, also saving time and water. I've used this little gizmo 4 or 5 times a week for 2 1/2 years and it's still steaming away. The tray that heats the water needs to be wiped down pretty often as the steam/water removes the "bloom" from the eggshell and, combined with the chemicals in the water, can get a little crusty. However, that crusty stuff stays on the bottom and doesn't touch the egg inside the shell and comes off easily with a little soapy soak. I don't use a micro-wave and won't cook food in plastic, but since these eggs stay in the shell and nothing plastic touches the inner-egg it's about as pristine a process as I can make it, conserves energy, cuts the wait time considerably and makes perfect soft-boiled and hard-boiled eggs! It's a genius little cooker! (I gave 7 to friends and family for Christmas! ) So simple and inexpensive! Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 31, 2025
reviews.title: Reliable and Powerful Amazing Blender!
reviews.body: I originally purchased this product to make smoothies, but it turned out to be much more versatile than I expected. I’ve been using it regularly for over six months now, and it has become a permanent part of my kitchen routine. When the product arrived, it was well-packaged and securely protected. The first impression was very positive. The materials feel solid and well-made. Visually, the product looks exactly like the photos shown on the listing, which I appreciated. In daily use, I haven’t had any issues with setup or operation. It’s very easy to use, and the blade is much sharper than I anticipated. I’m honestly careful not to touch it directly because it feels extremely sharp. I use it around two to three times a week, mostly for smoothies but also for sauces and quick food prep. Overall, the performance has been excellent. One of the biggest advantages is that it comes with multiple containers. This is very convenient, especially when preparing larger portions or when I don’t want to wash a container immediately. The containers are dishwasher-safe, which makes cleanup easy. I’ve washed both the blades and containers in the dishwasher many times, and there has been no dulling or visible wear. The blade handles ice easily and blends very efficiently. It really feels powerful for its size. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 16, 2026
reviews.title: Powerful Upgrade: My Experience with the Ninja Blender
reviews.body: After years of using my Magic Bullet, I decided it was time for an upgrade and chose a Ninja blender because I’ve always known Ninja to be a higher-quality brand. One of the main reasons I switched was that I wanted a larger cup size so I could make bigger smoothies without having to split them into multiple batches. The price of this blender also felt very fair for the quality and brand reputation, which made the decision even easier. One thing I really appreciate about this blender is how straightforward it is to use. There is essentially no assembly required and no complicated buttons to figure out. You simply screw the blade topper onto the cup, place it onto the machine, and press down to start blending. The only downside to this design is that you can’t operate it hands-free—you do have to hold it in place while it blends. That said, it works incredibly fast. It didn’t even take a full minute to completely blend frozen fruit, ice, and the other ingredients in my smoothie. Another feature I liked is that this set came with three cups and multiple lids, including tops that make it easy to drink your smoothie on the go. Having the extra cups is convenient if you’re making drinks for more than one person or prepping ahead. Overall, I’m really happy with this purchase and feel like it was a worthwhile upgrade from my Magic Bullet. Read more Read more

I also tried it out on ice and it’s amazing it crushes the ice like a camp! Also extremely easy to clean. Love the tall cup size. No leaking anywhere: the unit and the mugs, all have been stable. Been using it multiple times daily for over a month now. Well worth it.

description: All the power you need to: Mix, puree, chop, crush ice, and more - with only 5 simple buttons. With 700 watts of peak blending power in this milkshake blender. reviews.title: Excellent Blender — Great Power and Value!
reviews.body: I’ve been using the Hamilton Beach Power Elite Wave Action Blender (58148A) for a few months now, and I’m genuinely impressed. It’s powerful, easy to use, and works exactly as advertised. The 700 watts of power handle ice and frozen fruit with no problem, and the Wave Action system really does pull everything down toward the blades so you get a smooth, consistent blend every time. The 40 oz glass jar feels sturdy and high-quality, not flimsy like cheaper plastic ones. It’s also easy to clean, and the parts come apart quickly for washing. The 12 blending functions are simple to use and clearly labeled — perfect for making smoothies, purees, sauces, and even crushing ice for frozen drinks.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 15, 2014
reviews.title: Great blender, so far - and a recipe for a delicious smoothie!
reviews.body: I picked this up after being unable to find a blender under $30 in any local stores that had decent reviews (I always check Amazon.com for pricing and reviews before purchasing anything). I had purchased a VERY similar model (same brand, and similar features) on impulse at a local store for right at $30, but decided not to even take it out of the box after reading the reviews on Amazon (my phone was dead when I purchased it, or I would have checked the reviews before pulling the trigger). After a bit of research I came across this unit which was a few dollars less, and is the same brand with almost identical features - so I was surprised that it had so much better reviews, but I figured it was worth the gamble and I needed a new blender for my morning smoothies ASAP (my old one was not working at-all). I never took the other one out of the box, so I can't compare this one to that one - but I can say that I'm very happy with this blender - it works perfectly for what I need it for, and although I have only had it for a few weeks, I haven't had any problems yet and it seems sturdy enough to last for a while. The problem with my last blender was that the gears the connected the motor in the base to the blades in the pitcher were made of rubber or soft plastic, so over the years they eventually got stripped until they were unable to catch each other enough to make the blades spin. During my search, I came across several models that had metal pieces connecting the blades to the motor which seems like it would be ideal. This model uses plastic, however it seems thick and sturdy enough to last a while. The pitcher is also very wide all the way to the bottom. Many blenders have a 'funnel' shaped pitcher that is smaller at the bottom (by the blades), sometimes causing ice or frozen chunks to get caught in the blades which can cause problems with the motor and/or the gears that connect the blades to the motor. Because the bottom of this is so wide, it seems like this happens less frequently because the blades will simply push the frozen chunks aside rather than getting them stuck between the blades and the glass. I rarely used the lid on my old blender, as I don't usually have issues with the ingredients splashing out - however, the lid of this blender has a couple of features that I really like. One is the spout that allows you to more easily pour your smoothies into your cups with less mess/dripping, and the other is a little 'flap' that allows you to easily pour in ingredients while blending without worrying about splashing. reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 28, 2023
reviews.title: Wish I had returned this
reviews.body: Updating from Oct 2024 (purchased July 2023): This blender really isn’t good, or at least maybe I got a dud. Currently my blender only works 1/5 times and I have to finagle it around to get it to start. A weird smoky burning smell happens when it’s running as well. It also leaves odd black marks all over my (white!) countertops, and I can’t figure out if this is the rubber on the bottom or if literally something is coming out of it. It’s basically just a paper weight at this point. And all I use it for is making smoothies. It rarely even blends up all the fruit. Not a great use of my money even if it was a ‘good price.’ It’s a loss if you have to replace it every year. Ugh. Original: I don't know about this blender. I bought it during Prime Day because my old blender (a Black & Decker one I got in college and probably got on a Black Friday deal) had recently broken. There are so many blender options available that it is quite overwhelming to pick one especially with all the features they have, but since my old B&D was "basic," I figured I didn't need like a Ninja-level blender for my purposes (I literally plan to only make things like smoothies or milkshakes with this). The only requirement I wanted was a blender with a glass jar. this is just what I am used to , but I also noticed that many blenders with plastic jars (including a ninja one I was considering) had reviews that stated plastic jars may crack. So, with that being part of my search criteria, I saw this Hamilton Beach one for an ok price and it had many positive reviews, so I decided to pick it up. With that said, when I received this blender I already missed my B&D one in "look and feel." While I think the glass jar is a nice size/shape, I don't like the quality of the lid. I thought having a pour spout would be nice, but it just feels so flimsy and cheap and when you use it it's like another messy thing to clean. I'm not sure what it adds on just pouring straight from the glass pitcher. Plus I find it cheap and plastic-y and it is difficult to pull off of the glass jar (I've literally damaged my nails trying to release it). In terms of function, I don't think this is all that powerful. I have probably used it about 5-7 times so far, and I'd say 40% of the time I've had to "assist" the blender to process my food because it will get stuck. I have literally only made smoothies with this (and that is all I really intend to use it for). I followed the directions, saying put in liquids first, followed by fruit. My blender literally only has milk, yogurt, and frozen fruit, and it is not able to effectively blend that consistently without me having to open it and push the fruit down with a spoon. The "wave" action doesn't seem to do much. It is also very loud. It think it is louder when it is probably working harder (like when it is blending and not pulling the fruit down). But it really sounds like a vacuum cleaner. So that is annoying because I didn't have to stop and assist the blender on my old B&D. Since I don't think a smoothie is all that complicated/difficult to blend, the fact that it can't do that consistently isn't very encouraging. So I am considering sending this back. If I can find a similarly priced blender to this, I will likely swap it for that. Read more Read more

reviews.title: Good warranty service (at least in Canada!), good blender for the price point
reviews.body: I've used this blender more or less on a daily basis with frozen fruits and vegetables for about two months. I knew that I would be getting a lot of use out of it, and have had experience with both low end blenders (always disappointing) and high end blenders (from working at a smoothie making business), and was a little bit hesitant to purchase one at this price point, rather than investing in a higher end, more powerful one. However, I was happily surprised by both the quality and easy of use of this blender. It has a very solid glass jar (I've had bad experiences with cracks in the jars of cheaper plastic blenders). It also comes apart for easy cleaning and can go in the dishwasher. It's so easy to clean and used so frequently in my household that I rarely need to put it in the dishwasher unless I'm in a rush. The motor may not be as powerful as some more high end blenders, but as long as you make sure to start with a liquid base, it has no trouble blending frozen foods. The only caveat that I have is that, about a month into use, my unit started leaking. At first, I thought it was because I was putting it back together incorrectly, but then I noticed a small crack in the plastic/rubber ring in the cutter assembly. After wondering how difficult it would be to get the part replaced, I continued using it for a few weeks, but finally made the call to the Hamilton Beach office in Canada today. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on January 12, 2026
reviews.title: Easy clean-up, no leaking, works well, good price
reviews.body: Using these with my Cosori 5.8qt air fryer; the liner fits well. When inserting the liner into the preheated air fryer basket, be careful not to burn your hand. I use a spoon or other object to help place the liner flat in the preheated basket. I have used these several times over the past week and never had oil or grease leak through. Even when cooking hamburger meat, which is very greasy! Feels great to just take out the dirty liner and throw it away, leaving a clean air fryer basket for the next meal. Easy! Follow the directions and the liner should work well. Do not place the empty liner in the air fryer while preheating. Preheat the air fryer with nothing in the basket. When it’s ready to cook, put the liner in the basket and be sure to weigh down the liner with food. If there is not enough food on the liner the liner could fly into the heating element and catch on fire. So be sure to weigh down the liner with food first. Then you could start air frying. So far I have not had any burns/fires with these liners. Very pleased with this product. Because I don’t need to wash the air fryer basket after meals, I am using the air fryer more often! Great product.

reviews.title: A Must Have for My Ninja Air Fryer — Makes Cleanup an Absolute Breeze!
reviews.body: I picked up these Air Fryer Paper Liners for my Ninja Air Fryer — the one with the glass containers — and they have honestly been one of the best small purchases I have made for my kitchen! Before I started using these liners, cleaning the glass containers after every cook was such a tedious chore, especially with greasy or saucy foods. Now I simply place a liner in, cook my food and when I am done I just lift it out and throw it away. The glass container underneath is virtually spotless every single time. Cleanup now takes seconds instead of minutes! The fit is perfect for the Ninja glass containers — they sit nicely without folding up or moving around during cooking. They are also perforated which is really important as it allows the hot air to circulate properly so your food still comes out perfectly crispy just as it should in an air fryer. I have used them for everything — chicken wings, vegetables, fish, fries and even reheating leftovers. They handle it all brilliantly without any burning or sticking. The food slides right off every time. 125 pieces is such great value and at this price I will never be without them. If you own a Ninja Air Fryer with glass containers, these liners are an absolute no brainer. Do yourself a favor and grab a pack — you will wonder how you ever managed without them Read more Read more

I saw this 11" size and found it perfect when my 10" wasn't big enough, and the 12" was a little too big. The coating is sturdy, and believe me they are truly non stick! Clean up easily. I really like the construction. The base is solid & distributes heat evenly. Plus, they are dead flat, and oils stay in the middle. I previously invested in that other brand you see on TV. I paid a premium price for that feature , that disappointingly did not come close to that claim. The first was so bad, I sent it back, and the replacement was only slightly better. The SENSARTE line is great, you won't be disappointed and they are reasonably priced. Just to illustrate that this company does things with quality in mind, I attached a picture of the screwdriver that was included to attach the handle. The one on the left came with the $32 fry pan, the one on the right came with a $3,000+ printer that needed multiple parts attached.

reviews.body: This is one heck of a great frying pan! For the money, you can't beat it! It is truly non-stick and works well on induction stoves. Clean up is a breeze but let it cool before washing it and then let air dry. Don't use metal or other hard/abrasive type of materials/utensils on it. The pan comes with a handle that requires assembly. It includes a decent quality Philips screwdriver for assembly. Again, follow the simple directions. This pan feels very sturdy as does the handle. I recommend ordering the one with the lid, but you can buy the lids separately and the fit perfect!! I highly recommend this pan. I've tried those titanium pans, ceramic, honeycomb/hex type and they all fail! This pan passes with flying colours! Read more Read more

It’s non stick finish gives me the result I want when I am cooking scrambled eggs and omelettes—not all “non stick” pans, whatever the price, do this. If you are using the recommended cooking temperature there is no hot spot. I am super careful to only use bamboo or silicon utensils, so no scratches. It is an attractive pan that is showing minimal discoloration. It is a nice weight—not too light or too heavy. I highly recommend this pan, but I will revisit this review at about one year.

name: Rubbermaid Brilliance Glass Food Storage Containers, 3.2 Cup, Set of 4, BPA-Free, Leak-Proof, Clear
description: Rubbermaid Brilliance Glass Food Storage Containers are intelligently crafted, and beautifully designed. These glass containers have clear plastic lids with a 100% airtight, leak-proof seal and secure latches to make reheating and storing easy. Built-in vents under the latches allow steam to escape so you can microwave food splatter-free, without removing the lid. Crystal-clear lids and bases make it easy to see what’s stored inside. The lids are made with StainShield plastic so they're stain and odor resistant, helping them stay looking like new. These food storage containers are space saving, with a modular design for perfect organization and stacking in your fridge or cabinet. The universal lids fit across all Rubbermaid Brilliance containers, regardless of material type. These Rubbermaid containers are BPA-free, microwave-safe, dishwasher-safe, and freezer-safe. The glass container bases are also oven safe up to 450 degrees F.
highlights: LEAK-PROOF AND AIRTIGHT: Rubbermaid Brilliance Glass Food Storage Containers have crystal-clear lids that are 100% airtight and leak-proof, plus odor and stain resistant
EASY STORAGE AND REHEATING: Glass containers have vented, microwave-safe lids for easy, splatter-resistant microwaving
SPACE SAVING: Space-saving, modular Rubbermaid containers are perfect for stacking and organization
OVEN-SAFE: Glass bases are oven-safe up to 450 degrees F, so they double as glass cookware and serveware
EASY TO USE: BPA-free, dishwasher-safe (use top rack for lids), microwave-safe for reheating only with lids unlatched, and freezer-safe
SMART LID SYSTEM: Universal lids fit across all Brilliance containers, regardless of material type
SET INCLUDES: Four 3.2-cup glass containers with lids, for a total of 8 pieces

The sizes are ok, wish they had more of these in different sizes but Rubbermaid does not have any more round storage containers and that is a shame, I love the fact they are great value for the money because the reality is they will last for years to come. Durable and safe, have not chipped or cracked from refrigerator, dishwasher or freezer so far. They do work for stacking and are dishwasher safe. I put them on the top shelf of my dishwasher and the big one on the bottom. I fully recommend using them and buying them. I don't label but you can label them because the glass is thick and study, I wouldn't recommend labeling the lids because they are not glass and I do not know what the plastic is they are using on it but I don't believe it would be easy to remove.

I filled them all up with water and turn them upside down and none of them leaked. * Durable. While we wash the glass in the dishwasher, we still choose to wash the tops by hand even though they say they are dishwasher safe. Unlike some brands, these tops appear very durable. Looks like this set will last for many years. * Interchangeable tops. Several of the sizes use the same tops. * Stackable. This style stacks better than any that we’ve had before. I really like the rectangle sizes. They Stack in the cabinets or refrigerator more efficiently than our old round ones. They also fit perfectly in our lunch box for work. * Sizes. Everyone’s needs are different but these particular sizes meet our needs perfectly. No itty-bitty containers. Perfect sizes for leftovers and lunches. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on June 17, 2023
reviews.title: Great and the weight is a good thing see below
reviews.body: After I learned to season it quick like 7 times, and started using a bush and cup with a dap of oil on the side I kept around to wipe with oil when done, and learned to due to size I needed to have rag on edge of sink when dumping out water, and then I often but not always heat up water in electric kettle to pour in when done cooking in order to not shock it. I find after all this that I actually do basically no scrubbing, just a bit of scraping with the metal spatula while the hot water is in it. And then I use a silicone handle on one side I had gotten and I want to get the other side for the silicone side to hold but I just use a pot holder for the other side, thicker one. But I find now that this is an ideal pan to cook everything and anything and many things. I just leave it on top of my stove for daily use. And the weight, for the same reason a mechanics arms are big while not lifting, their muscles are big purely due to making the same motion every day repetitively with the wrench. It’s called a hermetic stressor, the same way weight session (more stressful obviously) and a hot sauna, cold plunge, run, etc, are all good forms of stress that cause adaptation, So to does this just cause you to lift a heavier pot a few times a day as you cook. For vast majority of us it’s nothing. But I told my mom for instance that she should use it and she complained how heavy it was, and my brutal honest response was “that’s exactly why you need to be using it because it’s not like you are working out”. It won’t make you buff, just a bit heavier than a similiar size pan, but for the older crowd who find it important to get their exercises in at the pool and such, this is no different. Plus, once I learned to clean efficiently and season a couple times, it’s a god send of a pan. I love it. I just want to put that “it’s too heavy” criticisism that comes with cast iron in a new light. Your body adapts, allow it too gradually. I will at some point be adding a top to this, I just haven’t decided if I need to get the lodge glass one which would be nice or a silicone one for this, or just a cast iron one to keep the theme and look and durability forever. (Glass and silicone could both break in different ways). I do love this pan as I will admit, one of the reason I got this pan, being some one who can be hard on things by temperment, after knowing roughly how to care for this pan, I also have a lisence to absolutely abuse it and can’t scratch it or anything. Because once you have researched a couple of ways to take rust off and to totally reseason and recondition an old used on for instance, you have the confidence to own this the rest of your life and not ever feel like your going to rune it. I’m also strategically lazy, I call efficient, my mom thinks different, lol, but anyway, the fastest way to clean any pan is with hot water right when done cooking as it burns and melts stuff right off, I can do that with this without worrying how it affects coatings or anything and I can do that to kingdom come. I just try to throw water from kettle on it ideally but not always. Again, the point of these pans is the amazing non stick coating that develops after use and learning to season a few more times your self, but that you can absolutely abuse these pans and know they will last. There is a certain security that’s nice to feel with knowing that. The fact that it has a great non stick that develops after some use and is durable Af, I’m in love. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on November 17, 2007
reviews.title: Timeless classic for the modern kitchen
reviews.body: Sorry for the long review - for the short review, count the stars! I'm a bit of a purist. I always season my cast iron - new, or used (hey, I don't know WHAT someone else used that old piece of cast iron for - maybe cleaning auto parts). I sand it down to bare metal, starting with about an 80 grit and finishing with 200. Then I season. The end result is a glossy black mirror that puts Teflon to shame. There are two mistakes people make when seasoning - not hot enough, not long enough. These mistakes give the same result - a sticky brown coating that is definitely not non-stick, and the first time they bring any real heat to the pan, clouds of smoke that they neither expected or wanted. I see several complaints here that are completely due to not knowing this. But there were a few pieces I needed (yes, needed, cast iron isn't about want, it's a need), and this was one of them, so I thought I'd give the Lodge pre-seasoning a try. Ordered last Friday, received this Friday - free shipping, yay! The first thing I noticed was the bumpy coating. The inside is actually rougher than the outside, and my hand was itching for the sandpaper, but that would have defeated the experiment. This time, I was going to give the Lodge pre-seasoning a chance before I broke out the sandpaper. So I scrubbed the pan out with a plastic brush and a little soapy water, rinsed well, put it on a medium burner, and waited. Cast iron tip number one - give it a little time. Then give it a little more time. Cast iron conducts heat much more slowly than aluminum, so you have to have a little patience. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 28, 2026
reviews.title: A kitchen classic that gets better with use
reviews.body: I picked this up back in October, and it's quickly become one of my most-used pans. I can see why it's such a top seller. What I love: · Built to last – this thing is solid. No worries about non-stick coatings wearing off or chemicals leaching. It's pure, simple, durable iron. · Great heat retention – once it's hot, it stays hot. Perfect for getting a good sear on steak, crispy skin on chicken, or even baking cornbread. I've used it on the stovetop, in the oven, and on the grill—it handles it all. · Natural non-stick with use – the factory seasoning is a good start, but it truly becomes non-stick the more you cook with it. Fry an egg after a few months of use, and it slides right off. · Versatile – oven-safe, stovetop-safe (including induction), campfire-safe. It really does it all. A few things to know: Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 21, 2019
reviews.title: I cook almost everything in my Lodge Cast Iron Pan
reviews.body: If you haven't already taken the leap into cast iron you should think about it. First off the pan is cheap, durable and generally non-stick. Does this mean nothing will stick to this pan? No but if you have the right temperature and use the right amount of fat/oil when cooking and take care of this pan it will take care of you. I would say this pan excels at proteins first and foremost. I have cooked bacon, eggs and pancakes right after one another and nothing stuck to this pan. I would say vegetables are also a favorite in this pan as well because of the great flavor this pan helps create through the oil and char you can achieve with this pan. If you are the type of person that cooks and then puts the pans in the sink for a later time then cast iron might not be for you but I have left this pan in a sink for a few hours or on the stove top overnight and cleaned the next day so it is worth a shot. If you are the type of person who lets pans soak in water overnight or puts them immediately into a dishwasher for cleaning then this pan is definitely not for you unless you are willing to make an exception with this pan. Did I have immediate success with this pan right away? No I didn't. I had food stick and I had issues with cleaning the pan and reseasoning the pan and had to look for advice on the internet to solve my issues because not many people use cast iron so I couldn't ask them. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 29, 2026
reviews.title: Love Lodge Cast Iron Skillets!!!
reviews.body: I bought 2 of the Lodge Seasoned Cast Iron Skillets 6.5 inches...I already had the 8 inch for a few years now and love it that i wanted some smaller ones for my toaster oven and making a skillet cookie since never in my life have made one...Last night i made a brown butter chocolate chip skillet cooking in this cast iron in my toaster oven and it was perfect...it cooked evenly. fit right into my Hamilton Beach 4 Slice counter top Toaster Oven with no problems..heating up nicely with no problems that i noticed...I sprayed it first with Baker's Joy baking spray with flour on the cast iron then pressed my homemade cookie dough into the cast iron pan without any problems in the toaster oven it went at 350 for 20 min and was nicely done... was easy to pull in and out of the toaster oven its not light as a feather but also not a ton bricks to pick up either has a nice weight to it...I see these lasting me for years to come...once all done I washed it in hot water little bit of dawn dish soap dried it really well right after washing and re seasoned it with carapelli unfiltered extra virgin olive oil... best olive oil and ive tried all kinds over the years and nope nothing compares to this extra virgin olive oil i def recommend trying it out... anyway was very easy to season again and the pre seasoning of it was fine i didn't notice any distinct smell or ruining of my baked good in it at all...it is sturdy and made of excellent quality def would recommend this cast iron pan ... can't wait to get and try out their bread loaf pans and def the cupcake / muffin pan....

reviews.date: Reviewed in Italy on March 12, 2025
reviews.title: Precisione, Praticità e Design Elegante
reviews.body: Piccolo elettrodomestico che si è rivelato un grande aiuto in cucina. Questa bilancia ha le funzioni essenziali per pesare gli ingredienti in cucina: misurazione in grammi, millilitri e once, funzione tara per azzerare il peso del contenitore e spegnimento automatico per risparmiare energia. I comandi sono semplici e intuitivi, rendendo l'utilizzo della bilancia davvero facile e alla portata di tutti, anche per chi non ha molta familiarità con questo tipo di strumenti. Il display LCD retroilluminato è ampio e ben leggibile, anche in condizioni di scarsa illuminazione. I numeri sono grandi e chiari, rendendo la lettura del peso immediata e senza sforzo. Pesare gli ingredienti diventa un'operazione semplice e veloce, senza dover strizzare gli occhi o indovinare le cifre. Essere BPA-free è una caratteristica importante, soprattutto per un oggetto che entra in contatto con gli alimenti. Sapere che questa bilancia è realizzata con materiali sicuri e privi di sostanze nocive mi dà una maggiore tranquillità nell'utilizzarla in cucina, soprattutto quando preparo cibo per la famiglia. La superficie in acciaio inossidabile è facile da pulire con un panno umido, mantenendo la bilancia sempre igienica e in ordine. Un piccolo dettaglio, ma molto apprezzato: le pile sono incluse nella confezione! Appena tirata fuori dalla scatola, la bilancia è pronta all'uso, senza bisogno di correre a cercare le pile o di doverle acquistare separatamente. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 16, 2026
reviews.title: Fantastic Air Fryer!
reviews.body: This air fryer is fantastic. It is very well-made, extremely versatile, easy to use, easy to clean and an all-around amazing kitchen appliance to have. It's powerful, but quiet. The basket is spacious without the appliance being bulky or heavy. The booklet that comes with it is easy to understand and has a wealth of information on how to best use the air fryer. It heats up quickly, gets hot and stays hot. It doesn't smell or taste weird. This item is totally worth the cost.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on August 8, 2025
reviews.title: Best invention since sliced bread. We use the microwave as a breadbox now.
reviews.body: I am not able to do a lot of cooking anymore, but my husband hasn’t retired and is a HungryMan. Any thing that can help is appreciated, and this machine has been a lifesaver. It is so fast, and so easy, and to put it simply, this air fryer makes delicious, beautiful meals at the push of a button. My husband even uses it! That feels like a small miracle in itself. His favorite so far is grilled cheese and ham. My favorite is the air fryer potatoes. I microwave them first, coat in oil and my favorite seasonings (McCormick Garlic and…) and Voila! See the picture below. Tantalizing, crispy taters in minutes. Yesterday I made Sea-Pak popcorn shrimp in 8 minutes. I really like the PreHeat feature; it knows how long it needs to be to PreHeat for whatever temperature I plan to use. And in the box with the machine is a cooking times chart, and a recipe book, But I don’t get hung up on any of those details because it is really just a table top mini-oven that does everything from rotisserie and bake, to roast, broil and air fry. I purchased air fryer oil; every thing needs a spritz before cooking, Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 30, 2026
reviews.title: Perfectly crisped / cooked food EVERY SINGLE TIME.
reviews.body: My family and I use our Cosori air fryer EVERY DAY. We have used the 5qt. unit until its untimely death and were upgraded to the 6qt. Turboblaze when a replacement was needed under warranty due to a failing switch. We absolutely adore the new unit and all of the additional features it provides! THIS UNIT IS SO QUIET my daughter asked me to check to make sure it was running after having started it. Air frying, baking, proofing, dehydrating, even the ability to make Greek yogurt. Five automated fan settings assure even cooking EVERY SINGLE TIME. The porcelain coating is a bonus and makes clean up insanely easy! The safety feature of a locking basket should put every parent at ease as well. I love this particular unit so much, I bought one for my sister while it was recently on sale too! I went with the Cosori brand after combing the internet researching for the best brand in my price range 7-8 years ago. I’ve been so very pleased that I’ve purchased other Cosori products. I’m a huge fan of brands that provide products that actually do what they say they do!!! I will be a lifetime Cosori consumer.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 24, 2026
reviews.title: FABULOUS!!!
reviews.body: I LOVE this air fryer. I watched some reviews and found that this air fryer was always one of the favorites. It was on sale, so I went for it. I can’t believe I waited this long to get one. Preparing food is a breeze and it is so easy to clean. I can make a hamburger, grilled onions, and tater tots in this fryer in 7 minutes. It would take me that long just to go outside to remove the cover, check the propane and light the grill. I love the fact that I have full temperature control. I also love that I can prepare a complete meal for one person (chicken breast and 2 veggies) all at once. I think I’ll take this fryer camping. If you’re interested in a basket style air fryer, you should consider this one.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 19, 2026
reviews.title: Great air fryer, does a great job and has different cooking settings
reviews.body: Great airfryer, I really love this airfryer, it comes with different settings besides air fry, I bought a liner for the inside of the fryer, and it works perfectly, it’s easy to clean and it’s not noisy like my old Phillips model, if buying this product, I would recommend using the nonstick oil that the manufacturer recommends, i just spray the liner, and the food comes out nice and crispy, i posted some pictures

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 30, 2026
reviews.title: Turn the unit off after your food is "Ready" or use "warm" setting
reviews.body: This air fryer is everything they say it is and I highly recommend it The ceramic non stick coating really sealed the deal for me However, I notice that when food is finished cooking or displays"Ready" if I don't turn the unit off after about 5 minutes I smell plastic The unit was set up and prepared for use as per instructions and I've already cooked with it a few times with no problems but I would always pull the basket out and push it back in, or turn the unit off after cooking I suspect the AC coil may still be activated without the fan running Without the DC fan running it could potentially heat up some plastic and if that AC coil does indeed remain active then I would suggest to turn the unit off or unplug the unit I will update if this happens again but I do know that I smelled plastic This could be a design flaw but only time will tell I would suggest to turn the unit off or unplug it when your food is "Ready" or use the "Warm" setting if you must let it sit This could be a big deal if this is indeed a design flaw Other than that, this is probably the best air fryer for the money Just remember to pull the drawer out and push it back in to in to reset the unit to idle Don't let it just sit there with the "Ready" light remaining on Just takes a second or less to tap on the screen to shut it off or pull the tray out and push it back in You could also use the "Warm" feature which does use the DC powered fan with the low heat I hope I am wrong about this because I really love this air fryer and the ceramic non stick coating that it has, and It cooks very very well Again, there really is no need to have the "Ready" light on with the coil still activated It should have an after cooking timer like the instant pot that tells you how long it's been sitting there for reference But the instant pot also has the keep warm feature turned on by default, so you would also have to turn that off or some amount of heat would still be used, but it is designed that way and explained in the users manual This Cosori unit is supposed to shut itself off after 30 minutes of inactivity, but not trusting in that if I smell any plastic fumes I am still keeping the unit despite this potential design flaw I can live with that when everything else about this unit is perfectly fine Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 23, 2026
reviews.title: Outstanding Device, Cosori!
reviews.body: Because we ran somewhat late after working in the yard, we decided to cook frozen Fish Sticks and French Fries in this Air Fryer for our first use of it. Our first reaction was, WOW! This air fryer took these simple foods to another level! Our fish sticks & fries turned out crisp and flavorful; better than we've ever had them before. In fact, we didn't even realize these quick, easy foods could be that good. Prior to getting this Air Fryer, we had always prepared our fish & fries in our big oven at 420 degrees. And, they always took over 20 minutes each; so the fries would be cooled and somewhat wrinkled and shrunken, while trying to keep them warm in the small microwave warmer as we cooked the fish sticks. With this Air Fryer, the fries were done in about 15 minutes and the fish sticks about 12 min; a little over 25 mines combined. We were astonished at how much oil drained out of the sticks & fries through the crisper plate to the bottom of the fryer basket. We didn't realize that these frozen foods had so much oil in them, and how much they retained, when lying in their own oil on a flat cookie sheet to heat. We just wish you had the same model and features with a 7 or 8 qt. size basket that would easily allow cooking a whole 6-7lb chicken or turkey. Lastly, we very much appreciated how effortless the non-stick ceramic crisper plate fryer drawer cleaned up... simple! Outstanding food prep device Cosori! Something to be proud of!

reviews.date: Reviewed in Ireland on November 18, 2025
reviews.title: Great size, versatile and easy to use
reviews.body: Great size, versatile and easy to use. This is my third air fryer and my favourite so far. I prefer this air fryer to many of the others in the market because it is compact but has a wider basket than most models, so the food does not need to be stacked as much as in other fryers. I can fit a 9 inch pizza into it. The frozen fish and chips come out lovely and crispy when I use the frozen option as they recommend. Personally I don't think that it is necessary to preheat beforehand for anything that I have cooked so far. It is quiet and very easy to clean.

reviews.date: Reviewed in Sweden on January 1, 2024
reviews.title: Top produkt!
reviews.body: Grym hela vägen! Vår första airfryer och wow denna kommer användas flitigt. Har bara hört goda historier kring airfryers men denna va sjukt snabb och maten blev perfekt på bara några minuter. Pommes på 14min. Korv till barnen på under 5min!! Jag rekommenderar verkligen denna, den är tyst, ser mycket bra ut på diskbänken och piper inte alls mycket!

reviews.date: Reviewed in Germany on April 12, 2024
reviews.title: Schnell, leise, einfach zu bedienen
reviews.body: Die Turbo Blaze ist schön schnell, leiser als die Ninja Af180 (um die 2-4 db) und ist leicht zu bedienen. Es ist sehr einfach, tolle Ergebnisse zu produzieren. Ein Schnitzel, oder Pommes vom Vortag: in Minuten wieder knusprig! HotdogWürstchen: in Minuten knusprig. Ergebnis besser als im Ofen- hier kommen meine Sachen zwar fertig, aber auch irgendwie ledrig raus. Hat sie einen Mehrwert in Vergleich zum Ofen? Definitiv! Durch Geschwindigkeit und Einfachheit. Wer wirft schon 1 Würstchen oder eine Scheibe Brot in den Ofen oder auf die Pfanne? Mit der Cosori kann man das machen, da es eine Minutensache ist. Da sie wenig Platz braucht, ist sie auch ideal für den Balkon. Mal eben ohne Dreck und Rauch was brutzeln-toll. Als Resteveredler sehr genial. Das Display reagiert schnell und kann man von verschiedenen Winkeln sehr gut ablesen. Die Abluft schießt nicht wie beim Ninja einfach gerade nach hinten, sondern wird nach oben und zur Seite verwirbelt. Gut, falls man hinten nicht viel Platz oder eine empfindliche Wand hat. Ich hatte allerdings auch starke Geruchsprobleme bei meinem 1. Gerät. Selbst nach fast 8 Stunden "ausbrennen" roch es immer noch zu stark nach Plastik in der Wohnung. Hier scheint Cosori in der Produktion wohl ein Problem zu haben, wenn man die vielen Meinungen zum langanhaltenden Geruch liest. Eine Anfrage per Mail am Freitagmittag an Cosori wurde fast umgehend beantwortet und es wurde sich sehr schnell und sehr freundlich um ein Austauschgerät gekümmert. Wollte ich nach der Erfahrung mit dem Geruch eine andere Marke? Irgendwie nicht, denn die Turbo hatte zuvor einfach zuviel Spaß gemacht! So.simple. Das 2. Gerät hat anfangs ebenfalls gestunken (wie die Ninja übrigens auch), aber nach 1 Stunde war der Plastikgeruch zum Glück weg. Ich habe gute Erfahrung mit dieser Lösung gemacht: Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in Belgium on June 30, 2024
reviews.title: J'adore
reviews.body: Airfryer très intuitif, même les enfants l'utilisent (13 ans et 15 ans). Pour une famille de 5, 6.2L c'est assez. Très efficace et rapide. Je ne m'en passe plus. Les viandes, les Croquettes, les bruschetta tout est cuit rapidement!

name: Owala FreeSip Sway Insulated Stainless Steel Water Bottle with Two-Way Spout, Built-In Straw and Bucket Handle, Made for Travel, School, and Sports, 30oz, BPA Free, Leak Proof, Black Cherry
description: Designed for living life to the fullest, the Owala FreeSip Sway is like our original FreeSip—with even more travel-friendly features. In addition to our patented 2-in-1 FreeSip spout (you know, the one with the built-in straw and open swig option), this 30oz stainless steel water bottle holds plenty of water without being oversized. The sturdy bucket handle makes it easy to grab and go—not to mention comfy to hold. The tapered shape fits pretty much anywhere you’d stash a bottle, including most cupholders—whether it’s your car or your spin bike. The triple-layer insulation keeps drinks cold for up to 24 hours, and the sweat-proof exterior means no slippery hands or water rings.The wide-mouth opening makes cleaning easy and adding ice even easier. Plus, it’s made with BPA-free, durable materials. Sway is the ultimate hydration companion that goes with the flow of your day. It’s the ultimate gift for anyone who needs to up their hydration game. (Yourself included.).
highlights: 30-ounce insulated stainless steel water bottle with extra features: includes a 2-in-1 spout that lets you choose how you drink; push-button, leak-proof lid that locks for all your adventures
Choose your sip style—straw or swig: Patented 2-in-1 FreeSip spout is one of a kind; it lets you sip upright through the built-in straw or swig by tilting back the spout
Hygienic push-button lid and easy carry handle: closed lid keeps spout covered and clean between sips; ergonomic bucket handle makes transport easy for sports, campsites, road trips, school, and work
Keeps water cold and fits where you need: Triple-layer insulation keeps drinks colder up to 24 hours; wide opening for easy cleaning and extra ice; cupholder friendly base fits most car cupholders
Safe materials and simple care instructions: BPA, lead, and phthalate-free for safe drinking; hand wash the cup,lid is dishwasher safe and can be easily disassembled; not for use with hot liquids

reviews.body: This water bottle is the best of the best. My whole family has them. Beverages stay cold for a long time. They fit in 90% of cupholders (if you get this model). The lock is great to ensure that there’s no leaking when put in a bag or if it falls on its side. The handle on this model is much better than their others. The built in straw is awesome! The color choices are vast. I bought the wine color one for my mom. It’s very pretty. Target sells some different colors than Amazon. Shipping takes a minute. Cleaning is a breeze if you have a straw cleaner. I wash by hand to be safe.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 16, 2026
reviews.title: Amazing, but gigantic. Gave to my partner.
reviews.body: 5 stars because I got this bottle on sale for the same price as the 30oz, the colors are *gorgeous* and every bit as vibrant as the image shows (which I'm seeing is not typical across the board for owala) and it is indeed just a fabulous, strong water bottle. Easy to drink from the built-in straw, a joy to carry, and fits in every cup holder so far. I've already adapted better hydrating habits compared to using my old daily Nalgene. However, I am 5'5 and wanted a bottle to carry around frequently for work. This thing is like as big as my thigh bone lol! I should have gone to a local Target or Academy to compare the 30oz vs the 40oz bottle because the size difference is significant. If you consider the added weight for a full 40oz, I set myself up for failure in terms of my needs for this bottle. My 6'2" boyfriend who loves colors and works outside all day was happy to adopt this one for me however, and I ended up purchasing a 30oz elsewhere in equally fun colors. We have both had success having ice last a full workday. His keeps cold in the car and mine usually has a good bit of ice still after 3 refills and constant use. So far so good on no-spills, and I really appreciate the lock mechanism as I work around delicate artwork all day. It would be nice if the cups were machine washable alongside the gaskets, lid, and straw-- but alas, it's easy enough to clean in the sink and probably wouldn't fit in a top rack anyway. Each bottle comes wrapped with beautifully thorough care and use instructions as well-- take the time to read it all! Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 19, 2026
reviews.title: The Perfect Bottle
reviews.body: I do not leave reviews, but I had to for this. This is the PERFECT water bottle. It has a handle, it's leak-proof, with a lock, so you don't have to worry when your baby decides to play with the bottle and tips it over -- there won't be a spill! Straw and sip option are perfect and I love that the straw is inside the cup so I don't have to worry about my kids hurting themselves when they drink from the cup. 40 oz is a great size, holds a lot of water but tapered bottom perfectly fits in the cup holder. So easy to use, my 4 year old has no issues. Cleaning takes 3 minutes. I recommend to absolutely everyone.

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 20, 2026
reviews.title: Great Water Bottle!
reviews.body: I was looking for an insulated water bottle that would actually fit in a standard cup holder. I decided to try the Owala FreeSip Sway Insulated Stainless Steel Water Bottle. Upon receiving it, I noticed that it felt solid in my hand. The mechanism worked very smoothly with a simple button press opening the lid. Once open, you can choose to either use the included straw or tip the bottle and drink from the chug spout. In addition to it fitting in my cup holder, I needed it to be leak proof. This bottle is leak proof, and the spout can be locked by engaging a lock in the back. The rugged flip handle is also a convenient way to carry the Owala when it's not in the water bottle holder on my backpack. I hand wash the body of the bottle, but the top disassembles easily and can go right in the dishwasher when I want a deep cleaning. The bottle has a wide opening, so it is very easy to clean the inside. This has quickly become my favorite water bottle due to its ease of cleaning, good insulation, ability to fit in my cup holder, solid construction and materials, and the option of using the straw or chug spout. I have the 30 ounce bottle, but I am going to be picking up a 40 ouncer also. You won't regret purchasing one! I have used the bottle for a couple of weeks now, and here are my observations: It has gone on two camping trips, and it has proven to be durable. I have placed the Owala on its side on my passenger seat (just to test it...it fits perfectly in my car's cup holder!). It did not leak a single drop on a long road trip. On my camping trip, the water in the bottle was still ice cold the next morning, so it keeps cold beverages very cold! Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on February 26, 2026
reviews.title: Quite literally my perfect water bottle
reviews.body: This water bottle is perfect for me and is absolutely everything I wanted in a water bottle. Easy to clean, not too heavy and the handle makes it easy to carry around anyway, keeps water cold for a day but not much longer than that (but that's okay). I haven't dropped it yet so I don't know how well it'll hold up when I inevitably do but it seems plenty sturdy to handle it. I'm so excited about the color too because it's my favorite color and from what I can tell the dark cherry is an Amazon exclusive so I'm glad I found this. I love that it's still slender and can fit in standard cup holders while being a bigger bottle holding 40oz, which was my biggest priority. I've never had an Owala before so I wasn't sure what I'd think of the freesip mouth but I love it. I don't usually need to chug but having the option is nice too! If I ever need to replace this beauty I'll definitely get another Owala. I really could not ask for a more perfect water bottle. I don't even usually write reviews but I love this thing so much I just had to. I usually wouldn't spend this much on a water bottle but given all the praise I have for it the price is perfectly reasonable to me (and I try to be smart with money and responsibly pick when to spend more on something or be more frugal.) This is definitely worth the higher price than I have spent in the past on water bottles, and I'm pretty confident it'll last me a long time anyway, and I use it every single day too. Read more Read more

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on March 7, 2026
reviews.title: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Great quality, leakproof and super convenient!
reviews.body: I’m really impressed with this bottle from Owala. The design is both functional and stylish. The FreeSip spout is my favorite feature because you can either sip through the built in straw or tilt the bottle to drink normally, which makes it really versatile. The handle on the lid makes it easy to carry around, and the push-button flip top opens smoothly while still keeping the lid secure. I also love that it feels very sturdy and well made. The bottle keeps drinks cold for hours thanks to the insulated stainless steel interior. Another big plus is that it’s completely leak-proof, so I can toss it in my bag without worrying about spills. The wide opening also makes it easy to clean and add ice. Overall, this is a high quality water bottle that’s practical, durable, and great for everyday use whether at the gym, work, or on the go. I would definitely recommend it!

reviews.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 9, 2026
reviews.title: The perfect cup!
reviews.body: This cup is beautiful, leak proof and fits in my cup holder (thank god!!!). She's a beaut and the colors are so bright and pretty. I love using this for work so I can walk around with it and still hold all the things I need to hold. It keeps my water cool all day long.
